We're hiring—and we're doing it the Frekuent way. In a world where technology moves fast, we move with purpose. If you're ready to make your next big move and create something meaningful, you're in the right place.

Being part of our dream team isn't just about a job, it's about enjoying the frekuency. If you're ready to be part of this journey and grow together, join us and make a real impact.

Get to know us

Frekuent is a fast-growing company redefining the payment experience, helping businesses turn one-time buyers into lifetime clients through unique loyalty boosters. We believe that the secret to more sales is giving customers less: cashless, contactless and effortless payment solutions that transform the way businesses operate. With Frekuent, transactions happen in seconds, keeping customers coming back for seconds.

Want to know more? Check us out at www.frekuent.com.

About Us

Frekuent is a fast-growing startup redefining the world of payments, helping businesses turn one-time buyers into lifetime clients through unique and rewarding experiences. We believe that the key to more sales is giving customers more. That's why we create painless, cashless, and contactless payment solutions that transform the way businesses operate. With Frekuent, transactions happen in seconds, keeping customers coming back time and again. You can check more at www.frekuent.com

What You’ll Do

At Frekuent, data isn’t a support function, it’s how we make decisions. As our Data Analyst, you’ll sit at the intersection of data and business: turning questions from across the company into clear, reliable answers, and building the foundations that make every team smarter. You’ll have a real impact from day one.

  • Be the data partner for Commercial, Operations Product, and Leadership teams understanding their questions and turning them into data deliverables that actually get used.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Role

As a Data Analyst, you’ll help our customers get up and running on Affinity by importing their data and setting up the platform to support their workflows. You’ll work closely with our Onboarding, Implementation, and Customer Success teams, and play a key role in communicating directly with customers to gather requirements, set expectations, and deliver clear, actionable reports that demonstrate the value of their data setup.

What you’ll do:


Import and transform customer CSV data into Affinity and configure tables, lists, and fields so that customers can hit the ground running.
Manage the data import queue with discipline — tracking status, ETA, priority, and next steps for every import.
Clean, deduplicate, and validate data to ensure high-quality imports.
Work with Onboarding and Implementation teams to map customer data to Affinity’s data model.
Lead requirements-gathering conversations with customers to understand their data structures, workflows, and reporting needs.
Translate customer requirements into well-structured data configurations and reports that align with their business goals.
Build and deliver clear reports and dashboards for customers that surface key insights from their imported data.
Communicate proactively with customers to clarify data formats, align on timelines, and keep stakeholders informed throughout the onboarding process.
Leverage AI tools to automate and templatize repeatable workflows.


What we’re looking for:

Don’t meet every single requirement? At Affinity, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about this role, we encourage you to apply anyways.


Excel / Google Sheets power user — comfortable with VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, pivot tables, and text manipulation.
Strong client communication skills, with the ability to lead requirements-gathering conversations, ask the right questions, and translate business needs into technical solutions.
Confident communicator who can present data findings and reports to non-technical stakeholders in a clear, concise way.
Strong attention to detail with a quality-first mindset.
Familiarity with CRM platforms or database concepts (fields, records, relationships).
Good communication skills.
Organized and able to manage multiple imports with competing deadlines.
Manage the data import queue with discipline — tracking status, ETA, priority, and next steps for every import.
Experience with data analytics, reporting, or dashboard building (Looker, Tableau, or similar).


Nice to have:


Experience in B2B SaaS, customer onboarding, or professional services.


About Affinity

With more than 3,000 customers worldwide and backed by some of Silicon Valley's best firms, Affinity has raised $120M to empower dealmakers to find, manage, and close more deals. How? Our Relationship Intelligence platform uses the wealth of data exhaust from trillions of interactions between Investment Bankers, Venture Capitalists, Consultants, and other strategic dealmakers to deliver automated relationship insights that drive over 450,000 deals every month. We are are proud to have received Inc. and Fortune Best Workplaces awards as well as to be Great Places to Work certified for the last 5 years running. Join us on our mission to make it possible for anyone to cultivate and fully harness their network to succeed.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

We are looking for an exceptional Data Analyst to join the Coches.Net, Motos.Net and Milanuncios.com team. You will work as a member of multidisciplinary team, collaborating closely with Product owners, cluster teams, data engineers and management team stake-holders (Marketing, Sales, Product, BizDev and other Operations teams).

Your main mission is to define and execute analyses of several business context to identify trends, opportunities and problems to improve the offering and services we offer.

Job Requirements


3+ years of experience in eCommerce, an online company.
Advanced knowledge of SQL and relational databases
Advanced knowledge of Python and/or R for data analysis (e.g. pandas, numpy, matplotlib), and advanced analytics (e.g. scikit-learn)
Advanced user of at least one visualisation tool such as Tableau and/or QlikSense.
Analytical skills: know how to interpret data, to extract relevant insights and be able to translate business questions into analytical needs Nice-to-have
Experience working with sales, operations, and strategy teams.
Good knowledge of basic machine learning algorithms (e.g. clustering, classification, regressions)


Nice To Have


Experience working with sales, operations, and strategy teams.
Good knowledge of basic machine learning algorithms (e.g. clustering, classification, regressions)
Some experience with big data technology (e.g. databricks, datalakes)
Some knowledge of data engineering tools (e.g. Airflow, DBT)


Job Responsibilities


Identify, analyse and interpret KPIs and Health Metrics related to our users and customers, creating dashboard and visualisation to monitor them.
Identifying trends, risks and opportunities to provide insights that can make recommendations on how to improve the different initiatives of the vertical.
Create powerful Insights using Advanced analytics to improve the knowledge of our users and customers.
Actively participate in the discovery, and development of advanced analytical models (such as clustering, and predictive models), into their implementation in business use cases, such as customer segmentation, and propensity models for upselling, downselling, and churn prediction.

Title: Senior Data Analyst | Data & Insights | AI-Driven Analytics
Description:
🔹 Senior Data Analyst | Data & Insights | AI-Driven Analytics 🔹







📌 About the role




We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join a Data & Insights team within an international transformation environment.

In this role, you will play a key part in shaping how data supports decision-making across the organisation, working at the intersection of advanced analytics, business performance, and emerging AI capabilities.

You will contribute to high-impact initiatives across commercial performance, operational analytics, finance insights, and the evolution of self-service analytics and data products.

This is a highly business-facing role, ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex datasets into clear insights, building meaningful KPIs, supporting senior stakeholders, and exploring how AI can improve productivity, insight generation, and data discoverability.

If you enjoy working with SQL, Python, Power BI, large datasets, business stakeholders, and AI-driven analytics tools, this could be a great fit.







💻 What you’ll do




🔹 Deliver high-quality analytical insights to support strategic decision-making across commercial, operational, and finance domains

🔹 Translate complex data into clear, structured, and actionable outputs for business and senior stakeholders

🔹 Prepare reports, dashboards, and performance analysis suitable for senior management and executive-level decision-making

🔹 Work closely with business stakeholders to understand needs, define relevant KPIs, and build performance frameworks

🔹 Analyse business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational efficiency, customer behaviour, and commercial performance

🔹 Support and enhance existing reporting solutions, including Power BI dashboards and performance reports

🔹 Contribute to the design and evolution of self-service analytics solutions and data products

🔹 Collaborate with Data Engineers and platform teams to ensure data quality, consistency, and usability

🔹 Work with large datasets using SQL, Python, Power BI, and other analytical tools

🔹 Identify trends, patterns, risks, and opportunities through data analysis and business context

🔹 Explore and experiment with AI-driven tools and approaches to improve insight generation, productivity, and data accessibility

🔹 Help improve data adoption across the organisation by making insights clear, accessible, and actionable

🔹 Communicate complex analytical topics in a clear and structured way to both technical and non-technical audiences

🔹 Work in a fast-paced, transformation-driven environment with cross-functional teams







💡 Must Have




🔹 6+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Data & Insights, or similar roles

🔹 Strong experience working with large datasets and analytical tools

🔹 Strong proficiency in SQL

🔹 Experience with Python for data analysis, automation, or analytical workflows

🔹 Strong experience with Power BI or similar BI / data visualisation tools

🔹 Proven ability to turn complex data into actionable business insights

🔹 Experience building dashboards, reports, KPIs, and performance analysis for business users

🔹 Good understanding of business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational KPIs, commercial performance, or finance metrics

🔹 Experience working with stakeholders at different levels, including senior business stakeholders

🔹 Ability to communicate complex topics in a clear, structured, and business-oriented way

🔹 Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and attention to detail

🔹 Curiosity and willingness to experiment with new technologies, especially AI-driven analytics tools

🔹 Comfortable working in ambiguous, fast-moving, and transformation-driven environments

🔹 Intermediate English level or above







✨ Nice to Have




🔹 Experience preparing reporting for senior management, executive committees, or management committees

🔹 Experience contributing to self-service analytics, data products, or reusable reporting frameworks

🔹 Exposure to AI tools and technologies such as Generative AI, analytics copilots, ML tools, ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, or similar

🔹 Experience using AI tools to improve productivity, automate analysis, generate insights, or support data discoverability

🔹 Experience collaborating with Data Engineers, Analytics Engineers, or platform teams

🔹 Experience with data quality, data governance, data modelling, or analytics engineering practices

🔹 Experience with Tableau, Qlik, Looker, Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or similar tools

🔹 Background in commercial analytics, operational analytics, finance analytics, pricing, revenue management, or performance tracking







🏢 Hybrid model - 2 days on site per week







🌍 Why join this project?




🤝 People first – diverse and inclusive culture in an international environment.

📊 Work on high-impact Data & Insights initiatives across business performance, operations, finance, and commercial domains.

🚀 Help shape how data supports strategic decision-making across a large international organisation.

🧠 Explore the evolution of AI-driven analytics, productivity tools, and data discoverability.

⚙️ Contribute to self-service analytics, data products, and scalable reporting practices.

📈 Work closely with business and senior stakeholders, turning complex data into meaningful insights.

😁 High team stability and collaborative culture.

🎓 €1200 per year training budget and continuous learning opportunities.

💰 Flexible compensation model.

🩺 Private health insurance and benefits package.

⚡ Flexible working hours and hybrid model.

🏋️ Wellhub: fitness, wellness, and mental health support.

⚽ Football and paddle tennis teams sponsored by Capitole.

🥳 Team buildings, global events, and strong tech communities.







✨ Want to know more about us? Click here and discover all the details.




🔍 Curious about our culture? Check out what people are saying about us on Glassdoor.




💬 We know that not every candidate will meet 100% of the requirements. If your profile doesn’t match perfectly but you believe you can add value, we’d still love to hear from you.




👉 Ready for the challenge? Apply now!




Empowering People, Unlocking Innovation.




Information Security Notice

The employee will have access to confidential information related to Capitole and the assigned project.
Compliance with internal security and information protection policies is mandatory.
NDA signature required.

Title: Junior Data Analyst - Property Management
Description:
En Savills España, consultora inmobiliaria internacional, queremos incorporar un/a Junior Data Analyst para formar parte de la División de Property Management basado/a en nuestras oficinas de Barcelona.

Funciones


Análisis de datos mediante tablas, gráficos y modelos analíticos.
Uso de herramientas como Excel, SQL y Power BI para la explotación de información.
Desarrollo, mantenimiento y optimización de dashboards interactivos.
Elaboración de informes (reporting) periódicos y ad hoc para la toma de decisiones.
Identificación de tendencias, patrones y oportunidades de mejora en los datos.
Diseño de soluciones analíticas orientadas al negocio.
Automatización de procesos de reporting y tratamiento de datos.
Colaboración con distintos equipos para entender necesidades y traducirlas en soluciones de datos.


Requisitos


Licenciatura/Grado en áreas como Estadística, Matemáticas, Ingeniería, Economía, Arquitectura o similares.
Valorable Máster en Análisis de Datos, Business Intelligence o áreas afines.
Experiencia mínima de 2 años en análisis de datos, desarrollo de modelos analíticos y elaboración de reporting.
Valorable experiencia en el sector inmobiliario o en entornos de negocio similares (real estate, construcción, asset management).


Savills una de las principales consultoras inmobiliarias del mundo. Title: Junior Business Intelligence (Marketing) Analyst
Description:
Junior Business Intelligence (Marketing) Analyst




MotoGP Sports Entertainment Group is a leading sports entertainment company with exclusive commercial rights to the most exciting sport on earth. MotoGP™ is the premium class of two-wheel racing which sees the world’s best riders compete in events around the globe in front of a fanbase of 632 million. The 2026 calendar features 22 races across 18 countries and 5 continents and each event across the race weekend is broadcast live to more than 200 countries globally. In July 2025 MotoGP was acquired by Liberty Media Corporation, also owners of Formula 1. Liberty’s acquisition of MotoGP marks a significant milestone, as it brings together the two most important motorsport championships in the world and marks an important next step for future growth.




About the Role

We are looking for a Junior Business Intelligence (Marketing) Analyst to join our Business Intelligence team. This role is an excellent opportunity to contribute to the collection, analysis, and transformation of data into actionable insights that support marketing, social media, fan engagement, and commercial strategies.

You will work closely with cross-functional teams and external stakeholders, playing an active role in supporting data-driven decision-making while continuously improving reporting processes and analytics capabilities.




What You’ll Do

Analyze fan, consumer, and market data to inform segmentation strategies and translate insights into actionable recommendations.
Develop and maintain benchmarking and competitor analysis reports to support strategic positioning.
Extract, monitor and analyze social media performance, delivering actionable insights and recommendations to improve engagement and growth.
Collect, clean, and process data from internal and external sources, ensuring data quality and consistency.
Design, build, and enhance Power BI dashboards and reports to support business needs.
Collaborate with marketing, data, and commercial teams to identify opportunities and improve reporting frameworks.




What We’re Looking For

Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Data Analytics, or a related field.
Fluent in English and Spanish.
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Basic knowledge of Power BI is valued but not required.
Solid interest in business insights and marketing performance.
Previous experience in a professional environment is valued, including internships.




Skills & Attributes

Analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into insights.
Proactive and able to take ownership of tasks and deliverables.
Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
Good communication skills, with the ability to explain insights clearly.
Team-oriented.
Curious, proactive, and motivated to grow within a data-driven environment.




MotoGP is an inclusive company and guarantees respect for the diversity of its employees

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción de la empresa

MSX International es un líder global en servicios para el sector de automoción, presente en más de 80 países. Desde nuestra sede en Barcelona trabajamos con diferentes fabricantes internacionales para ofrecer soluciones innovadoras que impulsan la eficiencia, la calidad y el rendimiento de sus operaciones.

Si buscas un entorno dinámico, analítico, internacional y orientado a la mejora continua, este es tu sitio.

Descripción del empleo

Como Analista de Datos, serás responsable de gestionar, analizar y transformar datos clave relacionados con proyectos del sector automoción. Tu misión será convertir grandes volúmenes de datos en insights claros y accionables que apoyen la toma de decisiones, la optimización de procesos y el rendimiento general del negocio.

Requisitos


Realizar análisis diarios, semanales y mensuales utilizando herramientas como Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Identificar tendencias, variaciones y oportunidades de mejora
Preparar informes avanzados alineados con KPIs y requisitos del proyecto
Mantener, actualizar y asegurar la calidad de la documentación y reportes en herramientas como SharePoint y Office365
Identificar patrones en los datos y proponer acciones correctivas u oportunidades de optimización
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para convertir insights en acciones concretas
Garantizar el cumplimiento de estándares internos en informes y dashboards
Presentar análisis, conclusiones e información relevante a equipos internos y dirección
Asegurar la precisión, consistencia y calidad de los datos analizados
Proponer mejoras continuas en procesos, metodologías y flujos de análisis


Requisitos

Conocimientos y habilidades


Habilidades de comunicación y capacidad para trabajar con equipos diversos
Enfoque resolutivo para problemas complejos
Capacidad para gestionar prioridades y trabajar con plazos ajustados
Dominio de Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Conocimientos de sistemas de gestión de datos, minería de datos, procesos ETL y reporting automatizado
Competencias en análisis estadístico y modelos de rendimiento
Experiencia con herramientas de automatización (RPA) es un plus
Conocimiento de Microsoft Dynamics (deseable)


Formación


Grado en Ingeniería, Economía, Estadística, Matemáticas o formación equivalente


Experiencia


Experiencia previa demostrable como Analista de Datos


Idiomas


Español nativo
Inglés B2 (escrito y hablado)


Información adicional

Qué ofrecemos


Puesto permanente con contrato indefinido desde el primer día.
Política de teletrabajo de hasta el 50% de la jornada mensual tras el periodo de adaptación.
Formar parte de un gran equipo de profesionales en una compañía internacional consolidada.
Obtener una visión global del sector automoción, interactuando con clientes, proveedores, consultores y concesionarios.
Salario: 23.000 euros brutos anuales.
Acceso a MSX Bazaar, el portal de compras colectivas con grandes descuentos para empleados.
Posibilidad de adherirte al Plan de Retribución Flexible (seguro médico, tarjeta transporte, tarjeta restaurante, formación...).
23 días laborables de vacaciones al año según convenio y 3 días por año completo trabajado.
Incentivos adicionales por programas de recomendación interna, formadores internos, detección de nuevas oportunidades de negocio, etc.


¿Crees que eres la persona ideal para este puesto? ¡Queremos conocerte! Envíanos tu candidatura ahora.

Title: Data Analyst II Rider Experience
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

YOUR WORK-LIFE OPPORTUNITY:

Join our team and become an integral part of Glovo's Global Logistics! We provide a dynamic and challenging environment filled with numerous opportunities for growth and development.

In this role, you will be responsible for redesigning, testing, and implementing the payment structures that motivate and reward our riders worldwide. By blending deep data analysis and data modeling with creative problem-solving, your work will ensure our logistics network is efficient, scalable, and fair for our riders. The KPIs you will influence are crucial to operations, as every decision made will directly affect the whole operational investment (biggest company cost).

Be a Part Of a Team Where You Will


Explore and find the most optimal logistics payment structure in order to maximize global riders engagement.
Strategically redesign our courier payment framework to improve key operational metrics, designing models that reward factors like vehicle used, distance, weight, and peak-hour availability.
Dive deep into massive datasets to identify trends, drivers, and opportunities. Your insights will be the foundation for optimizing our entire operational investment.
Own the experimentation process to validate your ideas. You will design, run, and analyze experiments (A/B testing, switchback tests) to precisely measure the impact of payment structure changes before a global rollout.
Continuously monitor the performance of your strategies. You will track KPIs, assess the effectiveness of your changes, and make data-driven adjustments as needed.
Understand and address local and regional needs by stepping into the shoes of the riders and truly understanding their perspective.


Qualifications

YOU HAVE


Degree in Engineering, Maths or another STEM field.
2+ years of experience in a data analytics role where you translated insights into tangible business actions and measured their impact.
Strong technical proficiency
Advanced SQL for complex querying and data extraction.
Proficiency in Python for data analysis and data orchestration.
Experience with data visualization tools such as Looker, Tableau, or PowerBI.
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. You are comfortable breaking down ambiguous, complex problems into manageable steps and driving towards a clear solution.
Nice to have experience with experimentation. Designing and/or analyzing A/B tests or other statistical testing methodologies to measure impact.
Comfortable working in a high-growth and high-performance environment, dealing with ambiguity while maintaining a positive mindset.
You can clearly and concisely explain complex analytical findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Fluent English is a must.
You are organized, take ownership of your work, and thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

Additional Information

Individuals representing diverse profiles, and abilities, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves


💪 Top-notch private health insurance to keep you at your peak.
🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.
🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.


At Glovo, your success is defined by both results and behaviors. We hire for excellence in craft and for the Leadership Principles that shape how we think, decide, and collaborate. What you achieve matters and how you achieve it defines us. Together, they move the business forward and deliver great experiences. Learn more about our Leadership Principles here.

Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds – all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Delve into our culture by taking a peek at our Instagram and check out our LinkedIn and website!

Title: Product Data Analyst | EU based/Remote
Description:
Join Hostinger, and we’ll grow fast! 🚀

We’re shaping the future of online success - powered by AI and driven by people. With 900+ talented professionals and over 5 million clients in 150 countries, we help creators and entrepreneurs bring their ideas to life faster and easier than ever before.

Our mission: To provide tools that help individuals and small businesses succeed online faster and easier.

Our culture: Guided by 10 company principles.

Our formula for success: Customer obsession, innovative products, and talented teams.

Your day-to-day:


Partner with Product teams to tackle challenges, reveal untapped potential, challenge status-quo and empower data-driven choices.
Use Amplitude to analyse user journeys and conduct A/B tests.
Use SQL and Tableau to extract and analyze data.
Build dashboards that make an impact - create powerful visualizations that give teams at Hostinger the visibility and confidence to take action, while ensuring data integrity and validity.
Use Lumos - our internal AI data agent to surface new insights and business recommendations.
Collaborate with our top-notch Data Platform team - work closely with data engineers and analytics engineers to ensure data availability, efficient processes and successful project execution.


Real projects you might work on:


Analysing AI-Powered Marketing Tool Users - our AI-powered Reach Team needs to understand product usage, user segments and conversions. Your insights could help us refine our approach and shape the future of Hostinger's Reach product. If you want to go the extra mile - some product-related user flow analysis and A/B testing would be welcome too!
Decoding AI WebMail Clients Engagement - our Mail Team is rapidly evolving the communication experience, and they require deep analytical support to fuel their growth. You will dive into customer behavior patterns, providing the strategic clarity needed to shape the next generation of Hostinger’s email solutions through data-backed innovation.
Optimizing Our Client Referral Program - As this initiative scales rapidly, your data-driven expertise will be essential in refining our commission frameworks. You will help us architect high-impact partnership strategies, ensuring we build deeper, more rewarding connections with our global client base.
Empowering Our Innovation Team - Innovation is at the core of our success. As we explore diverse new ventures, your analytical expertise will be vital in enriching these ideas with data. You will provide strategic guidance to Product Managers on whether and how to scale emerging business opportunities effectively.
Defining Product Adoption Success - We want to ensure our clients navigate our tools seamlessly and reach their objectives with ease. You will dive deep into product usage patterns and customer behavior, providing strategic recommendations to elevate the overall Hostinger experience through data-driven improvements.
And many more, depending on your skills, interests and business needs.


Your skills and experience:


2+ years of experience in a product analyst, data analyst, business analyst or similar role.
Experience with A/B testing and user journey analysis.
Hands-on SQL expertise - you can write queries, create and optimize dashboard models. Basically, Claude asks you to check queries.
Proficiency in data visualization tools - we use Tableau, but experience with other tools works too.
A results-driven mindset - you love turning raw data into clear, actionable insights that drive business impact.
Strong communication & collaboration skills - you can explain complex data in a simple way and work well with cross-functional teams.
Bonus: Experience with Amplitude or other product analytics tools.
Extra points for: experience with Python or R for data analysis, A/B testing experience or knowledge of statistical concepts.


Benefits for you:


🚀 360 Growth: We provide limitless learning opportunities: access to platforms like Reforge and CoachHub, global conferences, feedback culture, digital libraries, and industry-leading subscriptions. Advance your career with internal mobility, and grow with a team eager to share knowledge and support your success.
🎯 Freedom & responsibility: Work on your terms, whether from the comfort of home or anywhere in the world. With a home office budget, you can elevate your workspace. Enjoy the flexibility to manage your schedule while bringing your ideas to life in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
💪 Wellness simplified: Your health comes first with company-provided sick days, 20 paid suspension days, Headspace subscriptions, and recharge leave. Participate in wellbeing-dedicated events and simply enjoy the balance of a lifestyle that prioritizes wellness.
🎉 Work hard - party hard: Recognize hard work with company events like Town Hall, Meet the Client initiatives, team-building experiences, and workations. Celebrate life’s big moments with milestone gifts for weddings, new parenthood, and graduations.


Compensation:

We’re looking for people who can make a meaningful impact, and we recognize that different levels of experience bring different levels of value. Compensation for this role will be discussed throughout the hiring process and tailored based on your skills, experience, and potential contribution to the team.

Get ready to take your personal and professional growth to new heights! Join Hostinger today and be part of our journey 🚀

Three. Two. Onboard

Title: Operations Data Analyst - Specialist
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

YOUR WORK-LIFE OPPORTUNITY:

Join our team and become an integral part of Glovo's Global Logistics! We provide a dynamic and challenging environment filled with numerous opportunities for growth and development.

In this role, you will be responsible for redesigning, testing, and implementing the payment structures that motivate and reward our riders worldwide. By blending deep data analysis and data modeling with creative problem-solving, your work will ensure our logistics network is efficient, scalable, and fair for our riders. The KPIs you will influence are crucial to operations, as every decision made will directly affect the whole operational investment (biggest company cost).

Be a Part Of a Team Where You Will


Explore and find the most optimal logistics payment structure in order to maximize global riders engagement.
Strategically redesign our courier payment framework to improve key operational metrics, designing models that reward factors like vehicle used, distance, weight, and peak-hour availability.
Dive deep into massive datasets to identify trends, drivers, and opportunities. Your insights will be the foundation for optimizing our entire operational investment.
Own the experimentation process to validate your ideas. You will design, run, and analyze experiments (A/B testing, switchback tests) to precisely measure the impact of payment structure changes before a global rollout.
Continuously monitor the performance of your strategies. You will track KPIs, assess the effectiveness of your changes, and make data-driven adjustments as needed.
Understand and address local and regional needs by stepping into the shoes of the riders and truly understanding their perspective.


Qualifications

YOU HAVE


Degree in Engineering, Maths or another STEM field.
2+ years of experience in a data analytics role where you translated insights into tangible business actions and measured their impact.
Strong technical proficiency
Advanced SQL for complex querying and data extraction.
Proficiency in Python for data analysis and data orchestration.
Experience with data visualization tools such as Looker, Tableau, or PowerBI.
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. You are comfortable breaking down ambiguous, complex problems into manageable steps and driving towards a clear solution.
Nice to have experience with experimentation. Designing and/or analyzing A/B tests or other statistical testing methodologies to measure impact.
Comfortable working in a high-growth and high-performance environment, dealing with ambiguity while maintaining a positive mindset.
You can clearly and concisely explain complex analytical findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Fluent English is a must.
You are organized, take ownership of your work, and thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

Additional Information

Individuals representing diverse profiles, and abilities, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves


💪 Top-notch private health insurance to keep you at your peak.
🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.
🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.


At Glovo, your success is defined by both results and behaviors. We hire for excellence in craft and for the Leadership Principles that shape how we think, decide, and collaborate. What you achieve matters and how you achieve it defines us. Together, they move the business forward and deliver great experiences. Learn more about our Leadership Principles here.

Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds – all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Title: Revenue Management Analyst
Description:
Overview

Revenue Management Analyst

Design and implement the Pack, Price and Promotion Strategy across the different channels, with the objective of maximizing PepsiCo’s Net Revenue & Profitability.

This role sits at the intersection of consumer & commercial strategy, and financial performance, ensuring the right value propositions reach the right consumers, through the right channels, at the right price and in the right formats, while balancing profitability, competitiveness, and customer needs.

This role must have close collaboration with Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Category teams to drive sustainable growth through data-driven decision-making.

Responsibilities

Main Accountabilities:


Design and recommend Pack & Price strategies across brands and different sub-channels, grounded in insights and performance analysis
Lead Promotional strategy definition and implementation. Define the annual promo calendar, based on shopper insights to maximize performance. Perform periodic analysis to understand results & tailor the strategy.
Drive mix development initiatives to maximize Net Revenue Realization (NRR), acting as a category-wide owner of mix levers across channels and departments.
Own PPA initiatives, working together with sales, marketing & commercialization, ensuring market-fit and strategic relevance.
Lead the AOP strategy & algorithm, aligning short- and long-term initiatives to deliver volume, revenue, and profitability targets.
Create x-channel commercial policy, and define list prices for all SKUs, participating in the innovation gates.
Partner with Sales to co-create commercially sound proposals, including multipacks , bonuspacks or I&Os in order to boost volumes.
Support the implementation & adoption RGM Digital tools (e.g. elasticity, ROI, Uplift models…)
Anticipate proactively the market changes, for analysis and decision making (VAT or Tax impacts, legislations, sustainability goals..etc)
Monitor and track implemented initiatives in order to obtain learnings & insights to optimize and define future strategies.
Ensure cross-functional alignment across Sales, Trade Marketing, Insights, Finance, Marketing, Category Management, and Commercialization.


Qualifications

Experience required


At least 7 years experience in Revenue Management, Finance Planning, Insights, Commercial roles or similar.
Experience in the use of internal & external data bases (Nielsen, SAP,…)
Knowledge of RGM & finance KPIs (NR, MC, Elasticity, ROI..) preferred
Proactivity, ability to work in a cross functional environment and with collaboration spirit
Strong Microsoft Office skills (Excel and ppt)
Fluent in Spanish and English (advanced level)


Competencies Required


Good story telling and communication skills
Very strong analytical skills & high business knowledge as a broad perspective is needed to identify channel opportunities
Strategic thinking. Ability to see connection in data, anticipate potential risks/opportunities, and optimize & simplify processes.
Self-driven, able to work independently and take initiative
Team player & Proactive collaboration
Resilience and adaptability to change
Capacity to work under pressure and tight deadlines
Experience in stakeholder management


Why PepsiCo Iberia?

PepsiCo Iberia, part of the Western Europe Operating Unit, is home to a team of nearly 3,000 talented employees across our Barcelona, Madrid, Vitoria and Lisbon headquarters, and our production sites in Spain and Portugal.

With iconic brands like Pepsi, 7UP, Alvalle, Lay’s or Doritos, we continue to shape the food and beverage landscape as one of the region’s leading companies.

At PepsiCo, you’re not just joining a business - you’re joining a culture that champions innovation, encourages bold thinking and empowers every individual to make an impact.

Title: Business Intelligence Trainee
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
Esade es una institución académica global que mantiene estrechos vínculos con el mundo de la empresa y es reconocida por la calidad de su educación, su alcance internacional y un enfoque claro en el desarrollo integral de la persona.

Do Good Do Better es nuestra identidad y en Esade la construimos a través de los siguientes valores:


Integridad
Diversidad
Sensibilidad
Contribución al bien común
Justicia y sostenibilidad


Basándonos en estos pilares, trabajamos con una metodología cuya misión es la formación integral de profesionales competentes y socialmente responsables. Por eso, fomentamos el liderazgo inclusivo, la innovación académica, la tecnología y la solidaridad.

Si quieres formar parte de una organización como la nuestra, lee con atención la siguiente oferta:

Misión

Dar soporte en la atención, gestión y resolución de las peticiones de los usuarios de Salesforce de Esade, así como realizar tareas relacionadas con el Data Quality y la integración del dato en los diferentes sistemas, con la finalidad de proporcionar un alto de nivel de servicio a los clientes internos.

Tus Principales Funciones 🏆


Atención y gestión de peticiones de cliente a través del CRM Salesforce
Interlocución con el equipo técnico del área
Ejecución y resolución de peticiones del área comercial y corporativa de la empresa
Control y empuje de tareas a través de herramientas de seguimiento (Cases, Kanban, JIRA)
Participación en los proyectos que se desarrollan en la nube de Marketing de Salesforce (Marketing Cloud)
Gestión de tareas relacionadas con el Data Quality y la integración del dato en los diferentes sistemas
Divulgación interna de las Best Practices a los usuarios de la herramienta.


Requisitos 🎯


Convenio de colaboración con la Universidad en la que estés estudiando.
Proactividad, trabajo en equipo y ¡Ganas de aprender!
Habilidades comunicativas y relacionales.
Capacidad de planificación y organización.
Idiomas: Catalán/Castellano Alto; Inglés B2


Qué Ofrecemos 🚀


Posibilidad de realizar prácticas entre 4 y 6 meses
4 horas diarias
Horario mañana o tarde según las necesidades del estudiante
Remuneración 6,50€ brutos/hora
Política de teletrabajo: hasta 4 días a la semana
Incorporación Octubre 2026
Ubicación: Campus Sant Cugat


Compromiso con la diversidad y la inclusión

En ESADE estamos comprometidos/as con la diversidad y ofrecemos las mismas oportunidades profesionales a todas las personas. Fomentamos el aprendizaje inclusivo y, para nosotros, el talento es un factor clave para promover la diversidad. Por ello, se valorarán especialmente candidaturas de personas con capacidades diversas.

Le informamos que Fundació ESADE como responsable del tratamiento tratará sus datos con la finalidad de gestionar su candidatura. El interesado puede ejercitar, si lo desea, los derechos de acceso, rectificación, y supresión de los datos, así como solicitar que se limite el tratamiento de sus datos personales, oponerse al mismo, solicitar la portabilidad de sus datos, a no ser objeto de decisiones individuales automatizadas, cuando proceda, así como a retirar su consentimiento en cualquier momento dirigiéndose al Delegado de Protección de Datos en la dirección Av. Pedralbes, 60-62, 08034 Barcelona a través de correo electrónico a la dirección dpo@esade.edu

Puede consultar información adicional y detallada sobre Protección de Datos aquí: https://www.esade.edu/es/proteccion-datos.

Dar soporte en la atención, gestión y resolución de las peticiones de los usuarios de Salesforce de Esade, así como realizar tareas relacionadas con el Data Quality y la integración del dato en los diferentes sistemas, con la finalidad de proporcionar un alto de nivel de servicio a los clientes internos.

Tus Principales Funciones 🏆

  • Atención y gestión de peticiones de cliente a través del CRM Salesforce
  • Interlocución con el equipo técnico del área
  • Ejecución y resolución de peticiones del área comercial y corporativa de la empresa
  • Control y empuje de tareas a través de herramientas de seguimiento (Cases, Kanban, JIRA)
  • Participación en los proyectos que se desarrollan en la nube de Marketing de Salesforce (Marketing Cloud)
  • Gestión de tareas relacionadas con el Data Quality y la integración del dato en los diferentes sistemas
  • Divulgación interna de las Best Practices a los usuarios de la herramienta.

Requisitos 🎯

  • Convenio de colaboración con la Universidad en la que estés estudiando.
  • Proactividad, trabajo en equipo y ¡Ganas de aprender!
  • Habilidades comunicativas y relacionales.
  • Capacidad de planificación y organización.
  • Idiomas: Catalán/Castellano Alto; Inglés B2

Qué Ofrecemos 🚀

  • Posibilidad de realizar prácticas entre 4 y 6 meses
  • 4 horas diarias
  • Horario mañana o tarde según las necesidades del estudiante
  • Remuneración 6,50€ brutos/hora
  • Política de teletrabajo: hasta 4 días a la semana
  • Incorporación Octubre 2026
  • Ubicación: Campus Sant Cugat

Compromiso con la diversidad y la inclusión

En ESADE estamos comprometidos/as con la diversidad y ofrecemos las mismas oportunidades profesionales a todas las personas. Fomentamos el aprendizaje inclusivo y, para nosotros, el talento es un factor clave para promover la diversidad. Por ello, se valorarán especialmente candidaturas de personas con capacidades diversas.

Le informamos que Fundació ESADE como responsable del tratamiento tratará sus datos con la finalidad de gestionar su candidatura. El interesado puede ejercitar, si lo desea, los derechos de acceso, rectificación, y supresión de los datos, así como solicitar que se limite el tratamiento de sus datos personales, oponerse al mismo, solicitar la portabilidad de sus datos, a no ser objeto de decisiones individuales automatizadas, cuando proceda, así como a retirar su consentimiento en cualquier momento dirigiéndose al Delegado de Protección de Datos en la dirección Av. Pedralbes, 60-62, 08034 Barcelona a través de correo electrónico a la dirección dpo@esade.edu

Puede consultar información adicional y detallada sobre Protección de Datos aquí: https://www.esade.edu/es/proteccion-datos.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Level Up Your Career with Zynga!

At Zynga, we bring people together through the power of play. As a global leader in interactive entertainment and a proud label of Take-Two Interactive, our games have been downloaded over 6 billion times—connecting players in 175+ countries through fun, strategy, and a little friendly competition.

From thrilling casino spins to epic strategy battles, mind-bending puzzles, and social word challenges, our diverse game portfolio has something for everyone. Fan-favorites and latest hits include FarmVille™, Words With Friends™, Zynga Poker™, Game of Thrones Slots Casino™, Wizard of Oz Slots™, Hit it Rich! Slots™, Wonka Slots™, Top Eleven™, Toon Blast™, Empires & Puzzles™, Merge Dragons!™, CSR Racing™, Harry Potter: Puzzles & Spells™, Match Factory™, and Color Block Jam™—plus many more!

Founded in 2007 and headquartered in California, our teams span North America, Europe, and Asia, working together to craft unforgettable gaming experiences. Whether you're spinning, strategizing, matching, or competing, Zynga is where fun meets innovation—and where you can take your career to the next level.

Join us and be part of the play!

Position Overview

We are looking to welcome yet another brilliant Senior Marketing Data Analyst into our Marketing Analytics team.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you’ll be responsible for designing and driving high-impact analytics solutions, from developing advanced statistical frameworks to evaluate marketing performance, to building data pipelines that integrate third-party sources, to creating interactive tools that empower partner teams. These are just a few examples of the initiatives you’ll help lead and deliver end-to-end, playing a key role in shaping the technical direction of our analytics efforts. The demands on this team are constantly evolving with the needs of Socialpoint’s and Zynga’s dynamic marketing teams, so it is important that you be able to adapt and utilize your time efficiently.

What You'll Do


Partner closely with marketing and cross-functional teams to identify growth opportunities, improve efficiency, and shape strategic initiatives.
Lead analytical deep dives to surface insights and translate them into actionable recommendations for senior stakeholders.
Visualize and communicate analytical findings clearly, enabling data-driven decision-making across the organization.
Apply statistical methodologies to evaluate performance and account for uncertainties in major initiatives.
Proactively explore and prototype opportunities using AI-driven automation to streamline investigations and improve decision-making.


What You Bring


Demonstrated experience with some or all of the following: predictive modeling, statistics, experimental design, optimization, data visualization
Understanding of marketing metrics and KPIs, with experience in campaign optimization, Lifetime Value (LTV) modeling, and incrementality analysis
Proficiency in SQL and Python with hands-on experience in Redshift, Databricks, PySpark, and Airflow
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate ambiguous questions into data-driven insights
Strong communication skills — you can explain complex data in simple, actionable terms
Familiarity with AI/ML concepts or experience in automating recurring analytics workflows is a plus
A team player, you establish effective relationships to work with others for the win
Proven work experience in data analytics or data science roles
BS in Computer Science, Math, Statistics, Economics, or other quantitative field; Masters, MBA or PhD preferred


What's in it for you as a Socialpointer?


Hybrid working model: we ask you to come to our Barcelona office at least 2 days per week
A generous 25 days of annual leave, plus public holidays
Private medical insurance for you and your family to keep your health bar full
Disability and life insurance
Choose from the latest iOS or Android device when you join (phone or tablet)
Free language lessons in English, Spanish and Catalan
Spotify Premium to add the perfect soundtrack to your adventure
Legendary social events and parties to let off some steam
Take-Two Interactive employee stock purchase plan (eligibility criteria applies)
Professional development budget for skills training


Onsite Perks Include


State of the art gym facilities
Free breakfast, lunch, and specialty coffee
Onsite physiotherapist and hairdresser (no wallet needed!)


The list goes on and on… so get your game on and apply now! Click “Apply” to level up and submit your application in English.

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, which means we are committed to creating and celebrating diverse thoughts, cultures, and backgrounds throughout our organization. Employment with us is based on substantive ability, objective qualifications, and work ethic – not an individual’s race, creed, color, religion, sex or gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin or ancestry, alienage or citizenship status, physical or mental disability, pregnancy, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, status as a victim of domestic violence or sex offenses, reproductive health decision, or any other characteristics protected by applicable law.

As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to providing the necessary support and accommodation to qualified individuals with disabilities, health conditions, or impairments (subject to any local qualifying requirements) to ensure their full participation in the job application or interview process. Please contact us at accommodationrequest@zynga.com to request any accommodations or for support related to your application for an open position.

Please be aware that Zynga does not conduct job interviews or make job offers over third-party messaging apps such as Telegram, WhatsApp, or others. Zynga also does not engage in any financial exchanges during the recruitment or onboarding process, and will never ask a candidate for their personal or financial information over an app or other unofficial chat channel. Any attempt to do so may be the result of a scamp or phishing attack, and you should not engage. Zynga’s in-house recruitment team will only contact individuals through their official Company email addresses (i.e., via a zynga.com, take2games.com, naturalmotion.com, smallgiantgames.com, themavens.com, gram.gs email domain).

Title: Data Analyst Intern - Business Ops Data Enablement (September 2026)
Description:
What You'll Do:

Are you passionate about turning data into impact? At Criteo, our Business Ops Data Enablement team empowers analysts across the globe to make smarter decisions through better data, tools, and collaboration.

With a community of 250+ analysts worldwide, we’ve built a company-wide coordination team to manage analytical expertise, define usage policies, and align teams across regions. Our mission is to ensure that data is used effectively, sustainably, and collaboratively.

As a Data Analyst Intern, you won’t just learn—you’ll contribute. You’ll be part of a dynamic team that connects business and tech, contribute to data pipelines, and builds tools that scale globally. This is your chance to grow your technical skills, gain real-world experience, and make a meaningful difference.

During your internship, you’ll:


Build real tools: Learn how to design and improve a platform using Python and SQL
Modernize data workflows: Get hands-on experience optimizing SQL-based ETL jobs, making data pipelines faster and more reliable.
Bridge business and tech: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business needs into smart, scalable data solutions.
Empower a global community: Share best practices, deliver mini-trainings, and support analysts across Americas, APAC, and EMEA.
Champion data quality: Learn how to identify and fix data issues, and help define standards for excellence.
Grow with mentorship: Be coached by experienced analysts and engineers, and develop your skills in Python, SQL, and more.
Leverage LLMs: Develop the skill of writing effective prompts to boost your productivity or improve your code using LLMs like Copilot.



Who You Are:

You’re curious, collaborative, and eager to learn. You don’t need to tick every box, but you should be:


Interested in business intelligence and analytics
0–2 years data analysis experience with both technical and analytical components (Data Engineer, Data Analytics, etc.)
Basic understanding of data analysis concepts and tools (e.g. SQL).
Familiar with Python or another programming language
A problem-solver who enjoys working with data and is detailed
Comfortable presenting insights to different audiences
A team player who values community and collaboration
Fluent in English



We acknowledge that many candidates may not meet every single role requirement listed above. If your experience looks a little different from our requirements but you believe that you can still bring value to the role, we’d love to see your application!

Who We Are:

We’re Criteo, the Commerce Intelligence Platform. Criteo helps businesses turn shopper signals into commerce outcomes while delivering more relevant experiences for shoppers. We use proprietary commerce intelligence and AI decisioning to drive relevance for shoppers and performance for businesses.

At Criteo, our culture is as unique as it is diverse. From our offices across the globe or from the comfort of home, our 3,600 Criteos collaborate together to build an open, impactful, and forward-thinking environment.

We foster a workplace where everyone is valued, and employment decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and business needs—never on non-job-related factors or legally protected characteristics.

What We Offer:

🏢 Ways of working – Our hybrid model blends home with in-office experiences, making space for both.

📈 Grow with us – Learning, mentorship & career development programs.

💪 Your wellbeing matters – Health benefits, wellness perks & mental health support.

🤝 A team that cares – Diverse, inclusive, and globally connected.

💸 Fair pay & perks – Attractive salary, with performance-based rewards and family-friendly policies, plus the potential for equity depending on role and level.

Additional benefits may vary depending on the country where you work and the nature of your employment with Criteo.

Title: Digital Transformation Intern
Description:
Digital Transformation intern

6 months

September starting, but possibility to start earlier

Coty is one of the world’s largest beauty companies with an iconic portfolio of brands across fragrance, color cosmetics, skin care and body care. COTY is the global leader in fragrance and number three in color cosmetics. COTY’s products are sold in over 150 countries around the world. COTY and its brands are committed to range of social causes as well as seeking to minimize its impact on the environment. For additional information about Coty Inc., please visit www.coty.com.

We’re looking for Students to join our Global Planning Hub in Barcelona. The Planning Hub is responsible for Luxury and Consumer Beauty products worldwide. The Hub is located in a brand new building in the heart of Barcelona city.

Our responsibilities cover most of the supply chain functions, such as: material sourcing, production planning, product distribution, demand planning, divisional planning, customer service, logistics, new product development (initiatives planning), inventory management, master data management.

The Divisional Planning team more specifically is responsible for leading multiple transversal activities across the life cycle management: Innovation Management & Catalogue Management, in close collaboration with Business Supply Planning (HQ) & Supply Teams. On top, the team manage Global Demand Planning activities ensuring demand reasonability and supporting overall the S&OP process in close collaboration with our Demand Planning and Regional/Global S&OP partners.

English is our work language.

About The Role

We are looking for a motivated intern to support our team in improving operational efficiency through digitalization and data-driven tools. The role focuses on helping transform manual processes into smarter digital workflows and supporting the creation of dashboards and reporting solutions (e.g., Power BI).

This is an excellent opportunity for someone interested in business analytics, digital transformation, process improvement, and data visualization and at the same time acquire knowledge of supply chain end-to-end on a global level, working within a truly multifunctional and international team on challenging projects and daily operations.

Key Responsibilities


Support the digitalization of internal processes and data management
Assist in creating dashboards and reports using Power BI
Collect, clean, organize, and structure data from different sources
Help automate repetitive tasks and improve reporting efficiency
Analyse workflows and identify opportunities for process optimization
Collaborate with different teams to understand operational needs
Document processes and propose digital solutions
Support implementation of new tools and systems where relevant


Desired Skills & Qualifications


Currently pursuing or recently completed studies in:
Business Analytics
Information Systems
Data Science
or a related field
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Good knowledge of Excel and data handling
Familiarity with Power BI or similar visualization tools
Interest in digital transformation and process optimization
Basic understanding of databases, automation tools, or data analysis is a plus
Comfortable working with structured and unstructured data
Proactive attitude and willingness to learn
Good communication skills and ability to work independently


Nice-to-Have Skills

You can include some of these depending on the level you want:


Knowledge of SQL
Experience with Python, VBA, or Power Automate
Familiarity with ERP systems or business software
Experience with dashboards, KPIs, or reporting tools
Understanding of process mapping / Lean / continuous improvement concepts


What We Offer


Hands-on experience in digital transformation projects
Exposure to real business operations and decision-making
Opportunity to develop technical and analytical skills
Collaborative and dynamic work environment
Possibility to contribute directly to efficiency improvements

Title: Data Science Team Lead
Description:
Smadex is a leading advertising technology company founded in Barcelona in 2011 and sold to American-based and stock-listed Entravision in 2018 (NYSE::EVC). We are a fully transparent platform built with top technology, countless programmatic management features and powerful machine learning algorithms to help advertisers grow their business at scale. We are currently considered one of the top mobile ad-tech companies in the world.

About the Role

As a Data Science Lead, you will bridge the gap between complex big data , strategic business and product decisions. You will lead a talented team of 3 to 5 data analysts, overseeing the analysis of massive programmatic advertising datasets. You will be responsible for ensuring high-quality insights to shape your product development..

Your tasks and responsibilities:

Lead, mentor, and scale a team of 3-5 data analysts. Own the team’s operational workflow, prioritize incoming analytical requests from cross-functional stakeholders, and optimize delivery timelines.
Deep-dive into massive, fast-moving RTB datasets to decode auction dynamics, diagnose market fluctuations, and identify revenue opportunities; translate these findings into automated, self-service BI frameworks that democratize data access and eliminate ad-hoc extraction across the business.
Define and implement frameworks for peer review, data validation, and documentation. Ensure that all insights, models and custom dashboards delivered by the team meet the highest standards of statistical accuracy.
Work directly with Product Management, Data Science, and Engineering as a technical peer to define how performance signals are monitored, and translate complex data trends into actionable product decisions.

Empower Self-Service BI: 

Drive the strategy for automated reporting and data democratization, reducing ad-hoc data extraction requests and enabling other teams to make data-driven decisions independently.

Skills and requirements:

Technical degree in a quantitative field such as Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or Data Science.
5+ years of experience in data-heavy analysis or business intelligence roles, with a proven track record in digital advertising or marketplace environments.
3+ years of experience leading, managing, or mentoring a team of data professionals (as a Team Lead, Tech Lead, or Manager).
Fluency with Big Data: Expert-level mastery of SQL and strong capability to work independently in Python or R notebooks for advanced data manipulation.
Excellent written and verbal communication. Ability to synthesize complex technical anomalies or analytical conclusions into clear executive summaries for non-technical stakeholders.

Please note that we do NOT provide VISA sponsorship. Candidates without a work authorization to work in Spain won't be considered.

Integrate a highly motivated, young and dynamic team
Great compensation package
Top location at the heart of Barcelona with a rooftop terrace, Barbecue, and a fully stocked fridge
Great work-life balance: work from home (2 days per week), flexible hours
Meal vouchers - Ticket Restaurant monthly allowance
Monthly gym allowance: Choose between DiR and Wellhub
LinkedIn Learning and training opportunities
Monthly TGIF events
Regular team-building events
Fun and friendly work environment with talented marketers and engineers from over 40 countries
And more!

Title: Senior Manager, Product Data Analyst
Description:
Contentsquare is the all-in-one experience intelligence platform designed to be easily used by anyone who cares about digital journeys. With our flexible and scalable platform, organizations quickly get a deep understanding of their customers’ whole online journey.

We are a global leader in the experience analytics space, with a growing presence across 15 offices worldwide. We’re here to stay—and we’re looking for team members who are excited to drive impact and help us scale even further.

Our aim is to create an inclusive workplace where everyone learns and succeeds. Contentsquare has built a community of individuals who are daring, understanding, and deliberate. We invite you to join us in making the complex simpler—for our customers, their customers, and each other.

Important note: Be careful of scammers pretending to be from Contentsquare. We will never ask for money or contact you through random texts. Any communication from our in house Talent Acquisition team will only ever come from our contentsquare.com or @contentsquare-ext.com domain. For more information, visit our careers blog.

As a Product Data Analytics Senior Manager, you will lead and develop a team of three analysts dedicated to our core product for all sales-driven business (another data analytics manager handles our self-serve initiatives). In close collaboration with the SVP of Product, you will leverage data with your team to drive strategic decision-making and optimize product performance. This role is integral to our product team, ensuring data-driven decisions guide our product strategy and innovation efforts.

You’ll join the Product Insights department, composed of the Product Data Analytics team and Product Research team. In charge of having a 360 customer knowledge view, we combine quantitative and qualitative insights to inform the product roadmap. Reporting to the Senior Director of Product Insights, you will ensure your team is aligned with the broader company goals and operates with the velocity required to influence strategic decision-making.

What You'll Do

Strategic Alignment:


Understand the vision of the different product groups you support and ensure that your team’s focuses on the highest priority topics.
Showcase critical thinking by advising the Product SVP on which analyses and monitoring would be most beneficial given the current knowledge gaps and desired impacts.


Complex Project Management:


Direct the execution of complex initiatives involving multiple stakeholders, ensuring the team delivers timely, actionable insights.
Collaboration or dependencies might include technical teams, such as information systems, data ingestion and engineering, or business teams such as customer success, sales, lifecycle and implementation teams.


Team Leadership:


Develop and retain talent by identifying and nurturing individual strengths and career aspirations for your three analysts.
Align with them on clear OKRs and projects to tackle during the quarter, as well as providing ongoing feedback on their analyses, ensuring they focus on high-impact work with an optimized time-to-insight.


Data Empowerment:


Identify weaknesses in data empowerment across the product lines.
Guide and empower product teams with advanced methodologies and analytical tools to foster a data-driven culture.
Provide training and support to ensure teams can independently leverage data insights in their decision-making processes.


Strategic Collaboration:


Partner with product research leaders to establish direction and purpose, and work with Product Directors/VPs to advocate for insights-driven decision-making.


What You'll Need To Succeed


Proficiency in statistical analysis, data modeling, and data visualization. Ability to interpret complex data sets and translate findings into actionable insights.
Experience with data analysis, data modeling and visualization tools (SQL, DBT, Excel, Tableau, or similar software). Expertise in creating advanced data visualizations that effectively communicate complex insights to a broad audience, including dashboards and interactive reports.
Capacity to identify issues, analyze root causes, and develop effective solutions. Ability to approach problems methodically and think critically. Meticulous in analyzing data and ensuring accuracy. Ability to validate findings thoroughly and maintain high standards in data integrity and reporting.
Ability to convey complex data insights clearly and effectively to non-technical stakeholders. Strong written and verbal communication skills for reporting and presentations.
Experience working in cross-functional teams, including product managers, engineers, and designers. Ability to build relationships and influence stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Experience with project management tools and methodologies.
Management Experience: Some experience managing data analyst(s), even better if you have managed a team of product analysts, aligning them to a strategic vision without requiring constant guidance.
Understanding of business strategy and how data-driven insights can support and enhance product and business goals. Ability to align data analysis with business objectives.
You’ll need to have a prior experience as a data analyst and to hold a business or engineering degree.


What Makes You Stand Out


Previous experience of working closely with product leaders to understand the full product lifecycle and strategic considerations, and good knowledge of SaaS Product metrics.
Understanding of machine learning concepts and experience with implementing predictive models to forecast product performance and user behavior.
Being able to be hands on to unblock your team, as well as being a strategic communicator.
You have a prior experience using Contentsquare platform or another similar tool.


Why you should join Contentsquare

We invest in our people through career development, mentorship, social events, philanthropic activities, and competitive benefits. We are always assessing the perks we offer to ensure we’re aligned with the employees' needs.

Here are a few we want to highlight:


Virtual onboarding, Hackathon, and various opportunities to interact with your team and global colleagues both on and offsite each year
Work flexibility: hybrid and remote work policies
Generous paid time-off policy (every location is different)
Lifestyle allowance
A Culture Crew in every country we’re based in to coordinate regular activities for employees to get to know each other and bond outside of work
Every full-time employee receives stock options, allowing them to share in the company’s success
We have multiple Employee Resource Groups, that offer a safe space for individuals who share common identities, life experiences, or allyship to connect, support one another, and passionately advocate for the issues close to their hearts
And more benefits tailored to each country


Contentsquare is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Your personal data is used by Contentsquare for recruitment purposes only. Read our Job Candidate Privacy Notice to find out more about data protection at Contentsquare and your rights. You can exercise your rights by using our dedicated Data Subject Rights Portal here .

Your personal data will be securely stored in our hosting provider’s data center in Oregon (US west). We have implemented appropriate transfer mechanisms under applicable data protection laws.

Contentsquare may use AI-assisted tools to help review and screen applications. All decisions involving hiring are made by human reviewers, and your personal data will be processed in accordance with our Candidate Privacy Policy .

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Document Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Document Reviewer

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.

Key Responsibilities


Carefully review large volumes of structured and unstructured data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Identify and flag issues related to data quality, consistency, and potential risks involving Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Annotate, classify, and, where necessary, redact sensitive information to maintain privacy and compliance standards.
Follow strict guidelines, taxonomies, and workflow processes for data categorization and validation.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks to verify data integrity and validate outputs.
Maintain detailed records of reviewed documents and actions taken to ensure traceability and accountability.
Collaborate with the customer's team, providing clear and concise written and verbal communication about findings and recommendations.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience in document review, auditing, annotation, redaction, or data classification at scale.
Exceptional attention to detail with a high level of accuracy in repetitive tasks.
Familiarity with handling and protecting Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to document findings and communicate effectively with team members.
Demonstrated ability to follow complex guidelines and adhere to established taxonomies.
Experience performing QA or validation work on large data sets.
Ability to balance efficiency with precision in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in legal, compliance, privacy, auditing, compliance or information security domains.
Prior experience supporting data-driven AI or machine learning projects.
Comfort with collaboration tools and digital workflow management platforms.
