Title: Data & Analytics Specialist
Description:
At Bayer we’re visionaries, driven to solve the world’s toughest challenges and striving for a world where ,Health for all, Hunger for none’ is no longer a dream, but a real possibility. We’re doing it with energy, curiosity and sheer dedication, always learning from unique perspectives of those around us, expanding our thinking, growing our capabilities and redefining ‘impossible’. There are so many reasons to join us. If you’re hungry to build a varied and meaningful career in a community of brilliant and diverse minds to make a real difference, there’s only one choice.

Data & Analytics Specialist

Purpose:

The Data & Analytics Specialist is a hands-on operational role responsible for supporting the reliable, accurate, and timely flow of data that powers business decisions, commercial intelligence, and AI enablement. The role focuses on the execution of data quality tasks, vendor feed monitoring, reporting support, business rule maintenance, and process documentation — contributing to the team's ability to deliver trusted, activation-ready data at scale.

This role represents the evolving business data analyst — moving beyond static reporting toward ensuring data flows, quality, and structure are robust enough to support both human decision-making and AI-driven intelligence. The specialist supports hands-on data work (analytics requests, business rule updates, issue resolution, vendor feed coordination) while building capability in making data AI-ready and leveraging AI tools in daily operations. They develop business context — learning what the data means commercially — while building the technical fluency to work across platforms, taxonomies, and data pipelines.

This is not a traditional reporting role. It is a role for someone who recognizes that the value chain of data is shifting — from "pull a report" to "ensure the machine can interpret, validate, and surface insight autonomously." The specialist supports ensuring data is structured, documented, governed, and contextually enriched so that both BI dashboards and AI products deliver trusted, actionable outputs. They are developing the ability to move fluidly between resolving a data quality issue, monitoring a vendor delivery, supporting a Power BI report, or preparing data for an AI model — and they are building the business understanding to know why each task matters commercially.

This role operates with an AI-aware mindset. Tasks — from data validation to documentation — are executed with growing awareness of how they enable or constrain AI tools downstream. The specialist learns that clean, well-structured, semantically rich data is the fuel for AI-powered products and begins to shape their own work to maximize AI readiness. This means developing the habit of thinking beyond "is this data correct?" toward "can a machine interpret this data accurately and consistently?"

As organizations transition from manual, request-driven analytics to self-service, AI-augmented intelligence, this role contributes to the operational backbone that makes that transition possible — supporting data feeds, taxonomy updates, remediation workflows, and AI-ready datasets under the guidance of senior team members.

Scope (global, regional or local):

Global /Local

Key Tasks/ What You'll Do


Support the coordination and monitoring of data deliveries from self-service analytics (SSA) platforms and external vendors — checking timeliness, completeness, and format compliance — and escalating gaps, anomalies, or issues to senior team members.
Maintain and update business rules, mapping logic, taxonomies, and classification standards under guidance — ensuring they reflect current market realities and are structured for both human and machine consumption.
Investigate and help resolve data quality issues — supporting root cause analysis, implementing corrective actions, and documenting resolutions for future prevention.
Support analytics and BI reporting requests — preparing data, validating outputs, assisting with Power BI dashboard maintenance, and ensuring reports reflect accurate and timely information.
Assist with local market data requests and ad-hoc analytical needs — acting as a supporting link between market teams, global data platforms, and cross-functional stakeholders.
Flag opportunities to transition recurring manual requests into self-service reporting or automated delivery.
Prepare and structure data for AI use cases — ensuring datasets are clean, well-labeled, and documented with the context AI models need to interpret them accurately, with growing consideration for how attributes will be consumed by AI models.
Support the operationalization of AI products — assisting with data validation, output testing, and feedback loops under the direction of senior specialists.
Use AI tools (e.g., Copilot, GenAI assistants, n8n, automation scripts) in daily work to increase efficiency and reduce manual effort — developing fluency in AI-augmented operations.
Identify potential automation opportunities within data operations — flagging where AI or rule-based automation could replace repetitive manual tasks in mapping, validation, and quality checks.
Create and maintain operational documentation — including process SOPs, mapping logic, vendor feed specifications, and troubleshooting guides — written with clarity and structure that supports both human users and AI system context layers.
Support onboarding of new team members and contribute to continuous improvement by flagging recurring issues and suggesting process enhancements.


Key Working Relation:


Global / Local Team
Local/Regional Business Teams & Stakeholders
IT / Data Engineering & Data Science teams
External Data Providers (e.g., IQVIA, NielsenIQ, GfK, Euromonitor)
Commercial Analytics & BI users
AI / Digital Innovation teams


Qualification & Competencies:


Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Information Management, or related field.
1–3 years of experience in data operations, data quality, business analytics support, or commercial data analytics — experience in FMCG, Consumer Health, or Pharmaceuticals is a plus but not required.
Some exposure to syndicated market data (IQVIA, NielsenIQ, GfK) or willingness to learn — including familiarity with data structures, delivery formats, and common quality issues.
Basic experience or understanding of data mapping rules, product matching logic, and classification standards.
Good analytical and problem-solving skills — ability to investigate data issues methodically and support resolution.
Proficiency in Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, data manipulation); good knowledge of SQL, Power BI, or similar tools is a plus.
Strong personal drive to explore, test, and use AI tools, systems, and automation approaches in daily work — with a proactive habit of experimenting and learning.
Interest in automation concepts (e.g., scripting basics, workflow tools, AI-assisted data processes, prompt engineering fundamentals).
Detail-oriented with good organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks with appropriate prioritization.
Ability to create clear process documentation and follow established SOPs.
Good communicator — able to work across cultures and functions, explain data issues clearly to colleagues and stakeholders.
Self-motivated, reliable, and comfortable working in a global, matrixed environment with remote collaboration.
Growth mindset — views AI as a learning opportunity and actively seeks to integrate AI into personal workflows.
Fluent English required; additional languages a plus.


YOUR APPLICATION

This is your opportunity to tackle the world’s biggest challenges with us: Maintaining our health, feeding growing populations and slowing the rate of climate change. You have a voice, ideas and perspectives and we want to hear them. Because our success begins with you. Be part of something big. Be Bayer.

Bayer welcomes applications from all individuals, regardless of race, national origin, gender, age, physical characteristics, social origin, disability, union membership, religion, family status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression or any unlawful criterion under applicable law. We are committed to treating all applicants fairly and avoiding discrimination.

Location:

Spain : Cataluña : Barcelona

Division:

Enabling Functions

Reference Code:

875473

Title: Data Analyst - AI Insights
Description:
What makes Cognizant Netcentric a great fit for you?




At Cognizant Netcentric, we create memorable digital experiences for the world’s leading brands. We are a diverse, global community of Adobe experts working across countries, cultures, and technologies.




Our inclusive culture blends the entrepreneurial spirit of a startup with the stability, resources, and learning opportunities of a global organisation.




With Holacracy at our core, every individual is empowered to make an impact.




The role




We're looking for a Data Analyst to translate AI-generated insights from customer call transcripts into actionable product and operational improvements.




Embedded in a cross-functional team alongside Data Scientists and Product Managers, you'll bridge the gap between technical insights and real-world implementation — helping identify why customers call and driving initiatives that reduce call volume and improve customer experience. The role sits at the intersection of data analytics, customer experience,

and product development.




What you’ll do




Work with AI-generated outputs from call transcript analysis (e.g., contact reasons,
intent classification, conversation themes)
Interpret and validate AI insights in collaboration with Data Scientists, ensuring business
relevance and clarity
Identify and structure key call drivers, customer pain points, and recurring issues
Translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations for product and
operational teams
Collaborate with product teams to define features, improvements, and initiatives
aimed at reducing call volume
Develop a deep understanding of customer journeys
Act as a bridge between Technical Team and Product Teams ensuring shared
understanding of insights
Analyze call center performance using SQL and/or Python (Databricks) across structured
metrics and conversational datasets when needed
Hands-on experience in connecting to various data sources, managing data integrations,
and exploring and analyzing data across multiple platforms and systems.
Design, build, and maintain Tableau dashboards to track live chat KPIs and enable self-
serve insights
Identify trends, customer pain points, and behavioral patterns, and translate them into
actionable insights
Write advanced queries and data transformations to process large-scale, multi-source
datasets
Perform data preparation, exploratory analysis, and reporting automation
Ensure data consistency, KPI alignment, and clarity across teams




What you bring




3–5 years of experience in Data and Product Analytics
Strong ability to interpret data and translate insights into business actions
Solid working knowledge of SQL and data exploration (Databricks preferred)
Solid understanding of prompting and GPT models
Experience working with cross-functional teams (product, operations, data science)
Understanding of customer journeys and contact center environments
Experience building dashboards in Tableau or similar BI tools
Strong communication skills across technical and non-technical stakeholders
Hands-on, proactive mindset with ownership of end-to-end topics




At Cognizant Netcentric you get:

Permanent contract
30 working days of vacation
Flexible working hours
Recognition bonus system
Private health insurance
Apple work equipment
WFH equipment + welcome package
WFH monthly expenses compensation
Access to Udemy courses
Adobe certification
Sponsored gym membership
Tax-free benefits (ticket restaurant, kindergarten and transport)
A dynamic and international team
Everyone has a voice; we are a Holacracy organisation







At Cognizant Netcentric, we embrace an inclusive workplace, one where diversity is not just respected but celebrated. We do not discriminate based on age, gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected status. Our doors are wide open to all those who meet our high standards, and we cherish the distinct individuality and unique perspectives in our teams.

We firmly believe that a diverse and equitable workforce is the catalyst for groundbreaking innovation, boundless creativity, and seamless collaboration. We are aware of bias and have established clear criteria to take them out of the decision-making process, ensuring all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment.

Title: Automatization & Reporting Data Analyst
Description:
Proyectos reales, retos desde el primer día

En WSP trabajamos para transformar la ingeniería a través de la innovación, la técnica y la excelencia en el diseño. Nuestros equipos participan en proyectos que combinan complejidad, impacto y ambición. Si acabas de terminar tus estudios o tienes poca experiencia, y quieres crecer rápido en un entorno exigente, éste es tu sitio.

Estamos ampliando nuestro equipo de Bidding & Commercial en Santander o Madrid y buscamos incorporar un perfil con sólidos conocimientos en bases de datos y un marcado interés por el análisis de datos, capaz de ofrecer soporte especializado a nuestros equipos de inteligencia de negocio.

¿Cuál será tu rol?

Buscamos a una persona con sólidos conocimientos en bases de datos y manejo de herramientas de visualización dentro de Power Platform (Power BI, Power Automate y/o Power Apps), capaz de preparar, transformar y estructurar datos empresariales para crear dashboards y automatizar informes y procesos. El rol requiere una alta capacidad de gestión de proyectos de automatización —desde la recogida y análisis de requisitos hasta la planificación, el seguimiento y la evaluación mediante KPIs— trabajando con metodologías ágiles y equipos multidisciplinares. Integrada en el equipo de Data Analysis y Business Intelligence, esta posición tendrá un enfoque de soporte y colaboración con distintos equipos internos para mejorar la calidad, el análisis, la visualización y la consistencia de los datos en la toma de decisiones. Valoramos especialmente un perfil proactivo, con actitud analítica, orientación a resultados y capacidad para desenvolverse en entornos dinámicos.

Entre tus principales funciones se incluyen analizar procesos internos y de clientes para identificar oportunidades de mejora basadas en datos y automatización; diseñar, desarrollar y mantener informes y dashboards en Power BI; crear, optimizar y monitorizar automatizaciones en Power Automate; desarrollar aplicaciones internas o para clientes con Power Apps; gestionar y estructurar espacios de trabajo, listas y bibliotecas; documentar requerimientos técnicos y funcionales; elaborar manuales y guías de usuario; dar soporte en la implantación de nuevas soluciones y ofrecer formación básica; así como colaborar estrechamente con equipos internos para comprender necesidades, priorizar iniciativas y traducirlas en soluciones tecnológicas eficientes y escalables

¿Qué buscamos?

Buscamos un perfil con más de 5 años de experiencia, sólidos conocimientos de SQL y un dominio experto de la plataforma Microsoft Power (Power BI, Power Automate y/o Power Apps), capaz de integrar Power Platform con diversas fuentes de datos y sistemas internos o externos. Valoramos una alta capacidad analítica, organización, atención al detalle y una marcada orientación al aprendizaje continuo, especialmente en tecnologías emergentes que puedan resultar clave para la evolución de la organización.

¿Qué te ofrecemos?


Incorporación a un equipo técnico con proyectos reales desde el primer día
Jornada flexible y plan de formación continua
Acompañamiento de perfiles senior y entorno de aprendizaje real
Retribución flexible y acceso a beneficios para empleados/as


¡Da el primer paso!

Si quieres empezar tu carrera profesional con un equipo que te exija, te apoye y te permita crecer desde el minuto uno, postúlate y construyamos juntos tu trayectoria.

Nuestro Proceso De Selección


Entrevista telefónica: Una primera conversación breve para conocerte mejor y hablar sobre tu experiencia y expectativas.
Entrevista técnica y personal: Te reunirás con nuestro equipo de captación de talento y con el responsable del área para evaluar conjuntamente tus habilidades técnicas y tu encaje en el equipo.
Entrevista final: Conversarás con la Dirección de la Unidad de Negocio sobre tu potencial rol y cómo encaja con nuestra visión estratégica.


Sobre Nosotros

HTML:
Proyectos reales, retos desde el primer día

En WSP trabajamos para transformar la ingeniería a través de la innovación, la técnica y la excelencia en el diseño. Nuestros equipos participan en proyectos que combinan complejidad, impacto y ambición. Si acabas de terminar tus estudios o tienes poca experiencia, y quieres crecer rápido en un entorno exigente, éste es tu sitio.

Estamos ampliando nuestro equipo de Bidding & Commercial en Santander o Madrid y buscamos incorporar un perfil con sólidos conocimientos en bases de datos y un marcado interés por el análisis de datos, capaz de ofrecer soporte especializado a nuestros equipos de inteligencia de negocio.

¿Cuál será tu rol?

Buscamos a una persona con sólidos conocimientos en bases de datos y manejo de herramientas de visualización dentro de Power Platform (Power BI, Power Automate y/o Power Apps), capaz de preparar, transformar y estructurar datos empresariales para crear dashboards y automatizar informes y procesos. El rol requiere una alta capacidad de gestión de proyectos de automatización —desde la recogida y análisis de requisitos hasta la planificación, el seguimiento y la evaluación mediante KPIs— trabajando con metodologías ágiles y equipos multidisciplinares. Integrada en el equipo de Data Analysis y Business Intelligence, esta posición tendrá un enfoque de soporte y colaboración con distintos equipos internos para mejorar la calidad, el análisis, la visualización y la consistencia de los datos en la toma de decisiones. Valoramos especialmente un perfil proactivo, con actitud analítica, orientación a resultados y capacidad para desenvolverse en entornos dinámicos.

Entre tus principales funciones se incluyen analizar procesos internos y de clientes para identificar oportunidades de mejora basadas en datos y automatización; diseñar, desarrollar y mantener informes y dashboards en Power BI; crear, optimizar y monitorizar automatizaciones en Power Automate; desarrollar aplicaciones internas o para clientes con Power Apps; gestionar y estructurar espacios de trabajo, listas y bibliotecas; documentar requerimientos técnicos y funcionales; elaborar manuales y guías de usuario; dar soporte en la implantación de nuevas soluciones y ofrecer formación básica; así como colaborar estrechamente con equipos internos para comprender necesidades, priorizar iniciativas y traducirlas en soluciones tecnológicas eficientes y escalables

¿Qué buscamos?

Buscamos un perfil con más de 5 años de experiencia, sólidos conocimientos de SQL y un dominio experto de la plataforma Microsoft Power (Power BI, Power Automate y/o Power Apps), capaz de integrar Power Platform con diversas fuentes de datos y sistemas internos o externos. Valoramos una alta capacidad analítica, organización, atención al detalle y una marcada orientación al aprendizaje continuo, especialmente en tecnologías emergentes que puedan resultar clave para la evolución de la organización.

¿Qué te ofrecemos?

  • Incorporación a un equipo técnico con proyectos reales desde el primer día
  • Jornada flexible y plan de formación continua
  • Acompañamiento de perfiles senior y entorno de aprendizaje real
  • Retribución flexible y acceso a beneficios para empleados/as

Si quieres empezar tu carrera profesional con un equipo que te exija, te apoye y te permita crecer desde el minuto uno, postúlate y construyamos juntos tu trayectoria.

Agile methodologies

Title: Data & Analytics Engineer
HTML:
We are looking for a Data Engineer to join our team.What you'll do:

  • Partner with business stakeholders to identify data-driven opportunities and translate business needs into technical solutions;

  • Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines and data models to support analytics, reporting, and AI/ML initiatives;

  • Develop and maintain data products, reporting solutions, and self-service analytics using modern data lake architectures;

  • Process, transform, and integrate structured and unstructured data while ensuring data quality, governance, and reliability;

  • Analyze data from multiple sources to generate insights and support business decision-making;

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deploy data solutions and monitor their performance throughout their lifecycle;

  • Leverage cloud technologies and modern data platforms to build scalable and efficient data solutions;

  • Communicate insights through clear visualizations and reports tailored to both technical and business audiences;

  • Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives, promoting best practices in data management and analytics.

What you will need to bring:

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Data Science, or a related field;

  • 4+ years of experience in Data Engineering, Data Analytics, or Data Management;

  • Strong experience designing and maintaining data pipelines and working with large datasets;

  • Advanced SQL and Python skills for data processing and analysis;

  • Experience with cloud platforms, preferably AWS, and modern data lake architectures;

  • Solid knowledge of data modelling, ETL processes, and data governance principles;

  • Experience with data visualization and reporting tools such as AWS QuickSight, Power BI (Microsoft Fabric), or Qlik Sense;

  • Familiarity with Agile methodologies (Scrum);

  • Knowledge of Git and collaborative development workflows is a plus;

  • Experience with SSIS, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), R, or object-oriented programming is considered an advantage;

  • Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills;

  • Fluent English is mandatory; Portuguese is highly valued.**

What we can offer:

  • Integration into an innovative and data-driven environment with international projects;

  • Opportunity to work with modern cloud and data technologies;

  • Continuous learning and professional development;

  • Career progression within challenging data and analytics initiatives.

By applying, you accept our GDPR policies. Your personal data in your CV and documents will be used solely for processing your application at SYONE. SYONE, S.A. (NIPC 504729624) is the data controller, located at Rua Alfredo da Silva, 8-A, Edifício Stern, Piso 3D, 2610-016 Amadora. We will use your data to tailor your application to relevant vacancies and for potential employment contracts. If selected, your data will be kept during your employment and for at least two years after. If not selected, your data will be kept for two years for future vacancies and then deleted, except for your contact details. You can exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, etc.) by emailing data.privacy@syone.com. Your data may be shared with our HR service providers and will be stored securely. You can withdraw your consent at any time. We will not use your data for other purposes without your express consent. For more information, email data.privacy@syone.com.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Charging your car should feel as natural as charging your phone. That's the future we're building: a network of EV chargers where life happens. We don't just install chargers. We accelerate sustainable mobility. We obsess over partnerships. We grow relentlessly: our network, our partners, ourselves.

If you're here, you're probably not looking for "just a job." Neither are we. 😎 We're driven by impact, and always looking for people who thrive on turning bold ideas into reality. If that sounds like you, you'll feel at home here. We're looking for people who see challenges as puzzles, not problems, thrive in fast-growing, high-energy environments, and want to shape the future of mobility, not just watch it unfold. At Powerdot, you'll get freedom, ownership, and responsibility. The space to experiment. The chance to scale your ideas. And a team that's always pushing the edge of what's possible.

🚀 About This Role

As our Data Analyst, you'll be part of the Central Data team at Powerdot and will play a key role in the current phase of accelerated growth. Your mission is to transform raw data into clear, actionable insights that power smarter decisions across the business — ensuring every team has the visibility they need to move fast and move well. The ideal candidate has strong analytical and problem-solving skills, combined with the ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical audiences.

How You'll Shape Our Story:

Your day-to-day will include

Gathering and transforming data from a variety of sources — including internal databases, external APIs, and surveys, ensuring data is ready for analysis.
Analyzing data using statistical techniques — identifying trends, patterns, and insights that drive smarter business decisions.
Developing data visualizations — creating clear, compelling dashboards and reports that communicate findings to stakeholders in a concise and actionable way.
Partnering with stakeholders — understanding their needs and developing data-driven solutions that address real business questions.
Championing data quality — maintaining and supporting a culture of strong data quality across teams and workflows.
Staying ahead of analytics trends — keeping up-to-date on the latest tools, techniques, and best practices in data analytics.
Mentoring colleagues — coaching co-workers on analytics best practices and helping elevate the team's overall data capability.

This Is About You, Not Us 🫵🏼:

Up to 4 years of experience in data analytics or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills — you know how to break down complex questions and find the signal in the noise.
Technical proficiency — familiar with SQL, Python, and at least one data visualization tool such as Tableau, Metabase, or Power BI.
Eagerness to learn and develop — you thrive in a fast-moving environment and proactively grow your skills.
Great communication skills — you can translate technical findings into clear narratives for non-technical audiences.
Comfort with ambiguity and complexity — you navigate unstructured problems with ease and bring clarity to chaos.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team — you're self-driven but collaborative when it counts.

The Kind of Energy We're Looking For:

We're not into titles, egos, or corporate buzzwords. We're into people who make things happen.

You lead and listen. Whether you're steering the ship or rowing alongside the crew, you bring others with you and keep communication wide open.
You own your stuff. You don't wait for instructions: you see an opportunity, grab it, and make it better than anyone expected.
You've got a green heartbeat. Sustainability isn't just cool, it's core. You care about doing work that leaves the world a little better than you found it.
You balance hustle with humanity. You love the buzz of in-person teamwork, but you also know when to recharge (pun totally intended 🔋).
You grow on purpose. You're hungry to learn, to stretch, and to build something meaningful, not just rack up LinkedIn bullet points.

How you'll be Powered up?

🏥 Private health insurance coverage
💪🏼 Gym membership to help you stay active
⏰ Flexible working hours: you own your time
🌍 Annual offsite where we celebrate the year's achievements and spend quality time together (this year we went to Algarve, Portugal!)
🤝🏼 Regular team-building events with your local teammates
📚 English Training — as English is our language used to connect with all markets and teams
🍩 Free office snacks and unlimited coffee & Meal allowance for every worked day
⚡️ Special discounts for charging your electric vehicle

Our Promise To You

The best solutions come from different voices. We're building a culture where inclusion, respect, and equal opportunity aren't policies — they're lived every day. At Powerdot, everyone has the space to speak up, challenge the status quo, and make a difference. Your voice powers the future — literally.

Title: Data Services Analyst
Description:
What Makes Us Unique


At Cloudbeds, we're not just building software, we’re transforming hospitality. Our intelligently designed platform powers properties across 150 countries, processing billions in bookings annually. From independent properties to hotel groups, we help hoteliers transform operations and uplevel their commercial strategy through a unified platform that integrates with hundreds of partners. And we do it with a completely remote team. Imagine working alongside global innovators to build AI-powered solutions that solve hoteliers' biggest challenges. Since our founding in 2012, we've become the World's Best Hotel PMS Solutions Provider and landed on Deloitte's Technology Fast 500 again in 2024 – but we're just getting started. 


How You'll Make an Impact:



We are seeking a dynamic Data Import & Reporting Analyst to join our customer success team. This role bridges the gap between data services and business intelligence.

Your mission is two-fold: first, you will orchestrate smooth data migrations, helping new clients transition their historical data (reservations, guest profiles, inventory) from legacy systems to Cloudbeds. Second, you will act as a reporting strategist, coaching clients on how to leverage Cloudbeds’ reporting features to extract meaningful insights, track KPIs, and drive data-backed business decisions from day one.


Our Data Services Team:



We are a collaborative, remote-first team energised by solving complex data puzzles and transforming technical transitions into seamless, empowering experiences for hoteliers worldwide. We thrive on cross-functional teamwork, blending analytical precision with a passion for client education so that no property is left in the dark. If you love deep-diving into spreadsheets just as much as celebrating a client’s "aha!" moment when their data reporting clicks, these are your people.


What You Bring to the Team:



Lead Data Migrations: Act as the primary technical advisor guiding new clients through the smooth transition of historical data (reservations, guest profiles, inventory) into Cloudbeds.
Execute Data Manipulation: Extract, clean, map, and validate client data from CSV/Excel formats to ensure flawless system uploads.
Drive Technical Troubleshooting: Proactively identify and resolve formatting discrepancies or system mismatches before clients go live.
Empower Clients Through Coaching: Train properties on how to build, customize, and automate operational and financial reports within the Cloudbeds platform.
Ensure Data Integrity: Guide clients through post-migration audits to validate accuracy across financial histories and occupancy metrics.
Advise on Best Practices: Consult with property managers on key performance indicators (KPIs) to help optimize their daily operations from day one.


What Sets You Up for Success:



Hospitality Tech Background: Direct experience working with Hotel Property Management Systems (e.g., Cloudbeds, Opera, Mews, Protel, or similar software).
Client-Facing Expertise: A proven track record in onboarding, implementation, or Tier 2/3 technical support with a natural ability to teach and guide users.
Advanced Spreadsheet Proficiency: Power-user skills in Excel and Google Sheets (VLOOKUPs, Pivot Tables, advanced formulas, and data cleaning).
Analytical Mindset: Strong understanding of basic data management principles, data mapping, parsing, and root-cause error analysis.
Meticulous Attention to Detail: Exceptional precision when handling sensitive historical, financial, and guest records.
Remote-First Readiness: Highly tech-savvy with cloud-based workflows and equipped with a reliable, high-speed internet connection for video coaching sessions.
Multilingual Capabilities: Professional proficiency (verbal and written) in both Portuguese and Spanish


Bonus Skills to Stand Out (Optional):



BI & Analytics Familiarity: Prior experience working with business intelligence dashboards or advanced financial reporting structures.

What to Expect - Your Journey with Us


Behind Cloudbeds' revolutionary technology is a team of redefining what's possible in hospitality. We're 650+ employees across 40+ countries, bringing together elite engineers, AI architects, world-class designers, and hospitality veterans to solve challenges others haven't dared to tackle. Our diverse team speaks 30+ languages, but we all share one language: a passion for innovation and travel. From pioneering breakthroughs in machine learning to revolutionizing how hotels operate, we're not just watching the future of hospitality unfold – we're coding it, designing it, writing it, and shipping it. If you're ready to work alongside some of the brightest minds in tech who are obsessed with using AI to transform a trillion-dollar industry, this is your chance to be part of something extraordinary.

Learn more online at cloudbeds.com



Company Awards to Check Out! 



Best All-In-One Hotel Management System | HotelTechAwards (2025)
Overall 10 Best Places to Work | HotelTechAwards (2025)
Most Loved Workplace® Certified (2024) 
Top 10 People’s Choice(2024)
Deloitte Technology Fast 500 (2024)

Discover our Benefits:



Remote First, Remote Always 
PTO in accordance with local labor requirements
Monthly Wellness Fridays - enjoy an extra long weekend every month
Full Paid Parental Leave
Home office stipend based on country of residency
Professional development courses in Cloudbeds University
Access to professional development, including manager training, upskilling and knowledge transfer.

Everyone is Welcome - A Culture of Inclusion  



Cloudbeds is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer that celebrates the diversity in our global team! We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

Cloudbeds is committed to the full inclusion of all qualified individuals. As part of this commitment, Cloudbeds will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations in the hiring process. We encourage deaf, hard of hearing, deaf-blind, and deaf-disabled individuals to apply. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process or to perform essential job functions, please contact our HR team by phone at (858) 201-7832 or via email at accommodations@cloudbeds.com

. Cloudbeds will provide an American Sign Language (ASL) interpreter where needed as a reasonable accommodation for the hiring processes.

To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Cloudbeds. Staffing, recruiting agencies, and individuals being represented by an agency are not authorized to use this site or to submit applications, and any such submissions will be considered unsolicited. Cloudbeds does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Cloudbeds employees, or any other company location. Cloudbeds is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

From independent properties to hotel groups, we help hoteliers transform operations and uplevel their commercial strategy through a unified platform that integrates with hundreds of partners. And we do it with a completely remote team. Imagine working alongside global innovators to build AI-powered solutions that solve hoteliers' biggest challenges. Since our founding in 2012, we've become the World's Best Hotel PMS Solutions Provider and landed on Deloitte's Technology Fast 500 again in 2024 – but we're just getting started. 


How You'll Make an Impact:

We are seeking a dynamic Data Import & Reporting Analyst to join our customer success team. This role bridges the gap between data services and business intelligence.

Your mission is two-fold: first, you will orchestrate smooth data migrations, helping new clients transition their historical data (reservations, guest profiles, inventory) from legacy systems to Cloudbeds. Second, you will act as a reporting strategist, coaching clients on how to leverage Cloudbeds’ reporting features to extract meaningful insights, track KPIs, and drive data-backed business decisions from day one.


Our Data Services Team:

We are a collaborative, remote-first team energised by solving complex data puzzles and transforming technical transitions into seamless, empowering experiences for hoteliers worldwide. We thrive on cross-functional teamwork, blending analytical precision with a passion for client education so that no property is left in the dark. If you love deep-diving into spreadsheets just as much as celebrating a client’s "aha!" moment when their data reporting clicks, these are your people.


What You Bring to the Team:

  • Lead Data Migrations: Act as the primary technical advisor guiding new clients through the smooth transition of historical data (reservations, guest profiles, inventory) into Cloudbeds.
  • Execute Data Manipulation: Extract, clean, map, and validate client data from CSV/Excel formats to ensure flawless system uploads.
  • Drive Technical Troubleshooting: Proactively identify and resolve formatting discrepancies or system mismatches before clients go live.
  • Empower Clients Through Coaching: Train properties on how to build, customize, and automate operational and financial reports within the Cloudbeds platform.
  • Ensure Data Integrity: Guide clients through post-migration audits to validate accuracy across financial histories and occupancy metrics.
  • Advise on Best Practices: Consult with property managers on key performance indicators (KPIs) to help optimize their daily operations from day one.


What Sets You Up for Success:

  • Hospitality Tech Background: Direct experience working with Hotel Property Management Systems (e.g., Cloudbeds, Opera, Mews, Protel, or similar software).
  • Client-Facing Expertise: A proven track record in onboarding, implementation, or Tier 2/3 technical support with a natural ability to teach and guide users.
  • Advanced Spreadsheet Proficiency: Power-user skills in Excel and Google Sheets (VLOOKUPs, Pivot Tables, advanced formulas, and data cleaning).
  • Analytical Mindset: Strong understanding of basic data management principles, data mapping, parsing, and root-cause error analysis.
  • Meticulous Attention to Detail: Exceptional precision when handling sensitive historical, financial, and guest records.
  • Remote-First Readiness: Highly tech-savvy with cloud-based workflows and equipped with a reliable, high-speed internet connection for video coaching sessions.
  • Multilingual Capabilities: Professional proficiency (verbal and written) in both Portuguese and Spanish


Bonus Skills to Stand Out (Optional):

  • BI & Analytics Familiarity: Prior experience working with business intelligence dashboards or advanced financial reporting structures.

What to Expect - Your Journey with Us
Behind Cloudbeds' revolutionary technology is a team of redefining what's possible in hospitality. We're 650+ employees across 40+ countries, bringing together elite engineers, AI architects, world-class designers, and hospitality veterans to solve challenges others haven't dared to tackle. Our diverse team speaks 30+ languages, but we all share one language: a passion for innovation and travel. From pioneering breakthroughs in machine learning to revolutionizing how hotels operate, we're not just watching the future of hospitality unfold – we're coding it, designing it, writing it, and shipping it. If you're ready to work alongside some of the brightest minds in tech who are obsessed with using AI to transform a trillion-dollar industry, this is your chance to be part of something extraordinary.

Learn more online at cloudbeds.com



Company Awards to Check Out! 

  • Best All-In-One Hotel Management System | HotelTechAwards (2025)
  • Overall 10 Best Places to Work | HotelTechAwards (2025)
  • Most Loved Workplace® Certified (2024) 
  • Top 10 People’s Choice(2024)
  • Deloitte Technology Fast 500 (2024)

Discover our Benefits:

  • Remote First, Remote Always 
  • PTO in accordance with local labor requirements
  • Monthly Wellness Fridays - enjoy an extra long weekend every month
  • Full Paid Parental Leave
  • Home office stipend based on country of residency
  • Professional development courses in Cloudbeds University
  • Access to professional development, including manager training, upskilling and knowledge transfer.

Everyone is Welcome - A Culture of Inclusion  

Cloudbeds is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer that celebrates the diversity in our global team! We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

Cloudbeds is committed to the full inclusion of all qualified individuals. As part of this commitment, Cloudbeds will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations in the hiring process. We encourage deaf, hard of hearing, deaf-blind, and deaf-disabled individuals to apply. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process or to perform essential job functions, please contact our HR team by phone at (858) 201-7832 or via email at accommodations@cloudbeds.com

. Cloudbeds will provide an American Sign Language (ASL) interpreter where needed as a reasonable accommodation for the hiring processes.

To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Cloudbeds. Staffing, recruiting agencies, and individuals being represented by an agency are not authorized to use this site or to submit applications, and any such submissions will be considered unsolicited. Cloudbeds does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Cloudbeds employees, or any other company location. Cloudbeds is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

Title: AI Product Analyst - Voice AI Agents
Description:
MEDvidi is an AI-powered mental healthcare platform setting a new standard for safe, effective, and scalable psychiatric care in the United States. We combine licensed providers with proprietary AI tools to deliver consistent, outcomes-driven treatment for conditions like ADHD, anxiety, depression, and more. MEDvidi's technology automates charting, follow-ups, and treatment planning, freeing providers to focus on patient care while improving efficiency and clinical quality.

At MEDvidi, the AI Receptionist Team builds and improves Voice AI Agents that interact directly with patients and automate critical parts of the patient journey. These systems handle real-world conversations, support operational workflows, and help patients navigate care more efficiently.

As these agents become more autonomous and handle increasingly complex interactions, understanding how they behave in production becomes a critical product and business challenge.

We're hiring an AI Product Analyst to help us evaluate, monitor, and improve Voice AI systems operating at scale. You'll work at the intersection of Product, Engineering, Operations, and AI, helping the team understand how AI behaves in the real world, where it creates value, where it fails, and what should be improved next.

Why this role


Work on one of the most visible AI products inside MEDvidi.
Help shape the future of Voice AI in healthcare.
Influence product decisions using real production evidence.
High ownership in a fast-moving, product-driven medical tech company.


Responsibilities:


Analyze production behavior of Voice AI Agents and conversational AI workflows.
Investigate hallucinations, inconsistencies, silent failures, and quality degradation in AI-driven conversations.
Work directly with call transcripts, logs, generated outputs, and operational datasets to uncover patterns and actionable insights.
Design evaluation frameworks for measuring AI quality, reliability, and business impact.
Build monitoring systems and dashboards for AI performance visibility.
Measure the impact of AI systems on operational efficiency, business outcomes, and patient experience.
Detect quality shifts, anomalies, and emerging failure modes in production environments.
Partner closely with Product, Engineering, QA, and Operations teams on AI initiatives.
Support rapid decision-making in fast-changing and ambiguous environments.


This role is not for you if


You prefer working only with structured datasets and predefined metrics.
You are uncomfortable analyzing raw conversations, transcripts, and AI-generated outputs.
You expect clearly defined answers rather than investigating ambiguous problems.
You are not interested in understanding how AI systems fail in production.


Requirements:


3+ years of experience in Product Analytics, Data Analytics, Operational Analytics, or analytics roles within complex production systems.
Strong SQL skills.
Python for analytical investigations and working with large-scale text datasets.
Understanding of LLM limitations, hallucinations, evaluation challenges, prompt sensitivity, and AI failure modes.
Experience building monitoring systems, analytical frameworks, or operational dashboards.
Strong communication skills and ability to translate analysis into product decisions.
Ability to work effectively in ambiguous and fast-changing environments.
Fluent Russian; English level B2 or higher.
Ability to work CET-overlapping hours.


Would be a plus


Experience with conversational AI or AI agents.
Experience evaluating LLM outputs.
Experience with AI quality metrics and evaluation methodologies.
Experience with prompt iteration and AI evaluation frameworks.
Experience working with ML pipelines or AI-powered products.


What we offer:


High-impact environment: A chance to influence how AI is used in real patient interactions.
Growth & compensation: Competitive compensation and clear growth opportunities.
Remote-first: Fully remote, long-term B2B collaboration.
Health & wellness: Health insurance after the probation period, plus sports & wellness compensation.
Career development: Career growth, including personalized English lessons via Preply.
Time off: 19 working days of vacation per year, 3 additional wellness days, and paid sick leave for the first 5 working days.
Culture: Thoughtful gifts for important life events.


Ready to make an impact? Send us your profile, let's build something meaningful together.

MEDvidi is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and contractors. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.

Title: CRM Data Analyst
Description:
Country: Portugal

COMEÇA AQUI

O Santander (www.santander.com) está a evoluir de uma marca global de grande impacto para uma organização orientada pela tecnologia, e as nossas pessoas estão no centro desta jornada.

Juntos, estamos a liderar uma transformação centrada no cliente, que valoriza o pensamento crítico, a inovação e a coragem de desafiar o que é possível.

Esta é mais do que uma mudança estratégica. É uma oportunidade para pessoas motivadas crescerem, aprenderem e fazerem uma diferença real.

A nossa missão é contribuir para que mais pessoas e empresas prosperem. Adotámos uma cultura de risco sólida, e todas as nossas pessoas, em todos os níveis, têm um papel ativo e responsável na gestão de riscos.

O TEU IMPACTO

O Santander Portugal está à procura de um CRM Data Analyst para Lisboa.

Estamos a redefinir a forma como trabalhamos através da inovação, da tecnologia de ponta, da colaboração e da liberdade para explorar novas ideias.

Para ter sucesso nesta função, serás responsável por:


Analisar dados de cliente, definir e implementar novas segmentações e clusters comerciais
Definir e implementar jornadas multicanal (online & offline)
Definir e implementar KPI’s de seguimento da performance das campanhas e jornadas.
Medição da eficiência das interações no funnel de cliente
Medição do impacto nos objetivos comerciais
Identificar oportunidades de crescimento e propor estratégias Challenger alinhadas com os objetivos definidos;


O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

Experiência Profissional


Mínimo 4 anos como Data Analyst (Obrigatório)


Formação Académica


Formação superior em Engenharia, Ciências da Computação, Matemática ou áreas similares. (Obrigatório)


Idiomas


Inglês (Preferencial)


Hard Skills


Experiência profissional na manipulação de bases de dados (Big Data) e técnicas de performance (Obrigatório)
Domínio das ferramentas SAS (SAS GUIDE) e Bases de dados ORACLE (Obrigatório)
Domínio de ferramentas de manipulação de dados em ambiente Azure Cloud (Obrigatório)
Experiência na construção de análises, reports e acompanhamento de indicadores de performance (Preferencial)
Experiência em contexto de CRM, campanhas ou análise de cliente (Preferencial)
Conhecimento Salesforce MKT Cloud - Journey Buider (plus)


Soft Skills


Perfil Analítico e Data-Driven
Excelente comunicador – capacidade de tradução e adaptação do discurso técnico/analítico em impacto no negócio.
Capacidade de transformar dados em insights relevantes para o negócio
Capacidade de trabalhar com múltiplos stakeholders e ambientes multidisciplinares


VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.


Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.


Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.

Title: Lead Analytics Specialist
HTML:
Join Us

At Vodafone, we’re not just shaping the future of connectivity for our customers – we’re shaping the future for everyone who joins our team. When you work with us, you’re part of a global mission to connect people, solve complex challenges, and create a sustainable and more inclusive world. If you want to grow your career whilst finding the perfect balance between work and life, Vodafone offers the opportunities to help you belong and make a real impact.

Cyber Defence Operations (CDO) is Vodafone Group’s Cyber Defence Centre of Excellence, accountable for protecting Vodafone, its customers and its services against global cyber risk.

The Analytics Specialist is responsible for developing cross-Cyber Defence analytics and insight products that provide a holistic, asset-centric view of Vodafone’s security, operational and risk posture.

The role works across CTU, CSOC, CDIR and Security Testing & Exposure Management to aggregate, model and interpret data from multiple platforms, helping identify systemic patterns, control weaknesses, operational trends and emerging risks.

The role translates complex technical and operational data into trusted, reusable analytics that support incident response, threat-led defence, vulnerability prioritisation, exposure reduction, control improvement and leadership decision-making.

What You’ll Do

  • Define and own cross-Cyber Defence analytics use cases that provide trend-based and asset-centric insight across threat, monitoring, incident response, vulnerability and exposure management domains;
  • Design and maintain end-to-end analytics pipelines, from data sourcing and modelling through to dashboards, insights and executive-ready outputs;
  • Aggregate and normalise data from multiple platforms including Elastic data lakes, Dynatrace, Power BI, cloud platforms and cyber security tooling, ensuring consistency, quality and reusability;
  • Develop asset-centric views that correlate telemetry across infrastructure, applications, services, incidents, vulnerabilities and security controls to support a joined-up understanding of risk and performance;
  • Produce long-term trend analysis, behavioural baselining and forecasting to identify emerging risks, control degradation, systemic issues and opportunities for improvement;
  • Build and maintain dashboards and analytical products that translate complex datasets into clear, actionable insight for technical and non-technical stakeholders;
  • Work across CTU, CSOC, Incident Management, STEM, and wider Technology, Network and Platform teams to shape analytics requirements, define success measures and ensure outputs align to real operational and strategic questions
  • Apply analytical and statistical techniques such as time-series analysis, clustering and regression to extract meaning from large, high-volume datasets;
  • Ensure analytics outputs are explainable, repeatable and trusted, with clear documentation, assumptions and data lineage;
  • Contribute analytical insight to strategic planning, operational reviews, risk discussions, investment decisions and control improvement initiatives across Vodafone;
  • Support the evolution of Cyber Defence analytics capabilities, including improved data models, shared metrics, and more effective use of security, operational and exposure data;
  • Contribute to cyber reporting, dashboards and briefings, ensuring accurate, timely and data-driven insights are shared with the right stakeholders


Who You Are

  • Demonstrable experience (typically 3–5 years) in data analytics, business intelligence, data science or a related analytical role;
  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience;
  • Working towards, or willingness to obtain, relevant certifications such as Power BI Data Analyst, Azure Data Fundamentals, or data analytics / data science certifications is desirable;
  • Strong experience in data analytics, business intelligence or data science, ideally within a cyber, technology or risk environment;
  • Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate broad operational questions into scalable data models, metrics and visualisations;
  • Experience working with enterprise analytics and observability platforms such as Power BI, Elastic, Dynatrace, Splunk or similar;
  • Proven ability to work across large and complex datasets to identify trends, correlations, anomalies and emerging risks;
  • Comfortable engaging with stakeholders across cyber security, technology and leadership to understand problems and frame useful analytical questions;
  • Able to communicate insights clearly through visuals, storytelling and written narrative, tailored to different audiences;
  • Advanced capability in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, visualisation and dashboard design;
  • Working knowledge of SQL and/or KQL for querying large-scale analytical datasets;
  • Experience using Python or similar languages for data preparation, analysis and statistical modelling is desirable;
  • Understanding of time-series analysis, trend analysis, statistical baselining and forecasting techniques;
  • Familiarity with data pipelines, data quality management and schema harmonisation across multiple data sources;
  • Practical understanding of cyber risk concepts across security monitoring, incident response, threat intelligence, vulnerability management and exposure management;
  • Ability to balance analytical rigour with pragmatism, delivering outputs that are useful, trusted and timely;
  • Experience integrating diverse data sources such as cloud platforms, endpoint telemetry, network/security tooling, threat intelligence feeds, vulnerability data and external exposure data;
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across teams and support both operational and leadership decision-making;
  • Fluency in English


Not a perfect fit?

Worried that you don’t meet all the desired criteria exactly? At Vodafone we are passionate about empowering people and creating a workplace where everyone can thrive, whatever their personal or professional background. If you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align exactly with every part of the job description, we encourage you to still apply as you may be the right candidate for this role or another opportunity.

What's In It For You

  • Hybrid Work Model - Flexible hybrid work model with 8-10 in-office days per month, managed by team leaders;
  • Vodafone Products and Services - Employees get a mobile phone, free communication plan, data card, and various discounts on services and products;
  • Recognition - Recognition programs for innovative, creative, high-potential employees and exemplary behaviors;
  • Health and Well-being - Well-being Program offers nutrition and psychological consultations, webinars, workshops, and discounts on various services and products;
  • Learning - Access to Communities of Practice and a customizable digital training platform with high-quality content (namely Harvard Business Publishing, Skillsoft and Speexx);
  • Local and International Mobility - Internal recruitment with local and international rotation opportunities across departments and roles


Who We Are

We are a leading international Telco, serving millions of customers. At Vodafone, we believe that connectivity is a force for good. If we use it for the things that really matter, it can improve people's lives and the world around us. Through our technology we empower people, connecting everyone regardless of who they are or where they live and we protect the planet, whilst helping our customers do the same.

Belonging at Vodafone isn't a concept; it's lived, breathed, and cultivated through everything we do. You'll be part of a global and diverse community, with many different minds, abilities, backgrounds and cultures. We're committed to increase diversity, ensure equal representation, and make Vodafone a place everyone feels safe, valued and included.

If you require any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request as part of your recruitment journey, for example, extended time or breaks in between online assessments, please refer to https://careers.vodafone.com/application-adjustments/ for guidance.

Together we can.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
At PwC, we focus on creating a promising future where trust and innovation go side by side. Do you want to face this challenge with us?

A career in our Technology Consulting practice, will provide you with the opportunity to help clients seize essential advantages by working alongside business leaders to solve their toughest problems and capture their greatest opportunities. 

The role – Junior Data Analyst

As a Junior Data Analyst, you will contribute to the design, development, and optimization of data solutions that support strategic decision-making. Working alongside experienced professionals, you will help automate data processes and deliver actionable insights through advanced analytics and visualization tools. Your main responsibilities will include, but are not limited to: 


Analyse large and complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and business insights
Design, develop, and maintain ETL processes to ensure reliable and efficient data integration
Build scalable data models and interactive dashboards to support reporting and decision-making
Develop and optimize data engineering pipelines for data processing and transformation
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to enhance data quality, governance, and consistency
Support the implementation of innovative analytics and AI-driven solutions.


Your profile


BSc or MSc in Computer Science, Engineering or a related technical field
Proficiency in Python
Knowledge of JavaScript and Node.js
Understanding of data modelling principles and ETL frameworks
Familiarity with data visualization and reporting tools is valued
Experience working with Microsoft Azure cloud services is an advantage
Exposure to Generative AI (GenAI) technologies and frameworks is an asset
Fluent in both written and spoken English and Portuguese. 


We believe that success lies in people and in your willingness to push boundaries. If this resonates with you, you've found your place. It's time to bring your expertise to the 'Team PwC' and drive impactful change. 

We spark curiosity so you can do inspiring work. 

Grow here. Go further.

Title: Data Analyst & Data Visualization Specialist
Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a global leader in IT services, dedicated to providing innovative solutions to our clients. We are committed to fostering a dynamic, inclusive workplace that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration thrive. We operate in 19 countries with more than 28,000 employees worldwide.

If you are looking for a dynamic, innovative, and technology-driven company, Inetum is the place for you! Come be Inetum!

Job Description

We are looking for a Data Analyst with strong skills in Data Science, ETL, and Data Visualization to support cybersecurity risk management activities.

The selected candidate will lead end‑to‑end dashboard projects, from gathering requirements to developing data models and delivering analytical insights. This role involves close collaboration with technical and business teams to transform data into meaningful, actionable information.

Main Responsibilities


Understand business requirements and define key KPIs related to cybersecurity risk management.
Collect, clean, and transform datasets using ETL tools to build effective data models.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports, ensuring data accuracy and availability.
Use data processing and analysis platforms, applying statistical techniques when needed.
Leverage machine learning algorithms to uncover additional insights and identify correlations between indicators.
Present findings to stakeholders clearly, providing recommendations to improve dashboards and reporting.
Monitor data quality and continuously improve data models and processes.
Identify problems proactively and propose effective solutions.
Collaborate with team members across different locations to ensure smooth project execution.
Manage multiple projects simultaneously, demonstrating autonomy and strong time‑management skills.


Qualifications


A bachelor's degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, or a related subject.
3+ years of experience as data Analyst, developing Dashboard and DataViz
Knowledge of algorithms is a plus and will enable the candidate to be a force for proposing useful content related to cyber security risk management.
Expertise with POWERBI, TABLEAU, DATAIKU, PostgreSQL and ETL tools (e.g., TABLEAU Certified Data Analyst, DATAIKU Advanced Designer Certificate)
Basic Python skills
English B2+

Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a global leader in IT services, dedicated to delivering innovative solutions to our clients. We are committed to fostering a dynamic and inclusive work environment that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration can thrive. We are present in 19 countries and have more than 28,000 employees worldwide.

If you are looking for a dynamic, innovative, and technology-driven company, Inetum is the place for you! Come be part of Inetum!

Job Description


Identify business problems and propose data-driven solutions to support decision-making and growth
Retrieve, clean, prepare, and analyze complex datasets from multiple sources
Design and implement analyses using statistical methods and data models
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations following best practices (Tableau, Power BI)
Build automated tools and solutions to deliver recurring insights efficiently
Contribute to data feed industrialization, ETL pipelines, and data warehousing processes
Translate business requirements into technical specifications for the development team
Communicate complex analytical results clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Collaborate closely with traders, sales teams, business sponsors, and technical teams


Qualifications


Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Professional experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis using programming languages, preferably Python (Pandas)
Strong ability to analyze, synthesize, and simplify complex information
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus
Familiarity with Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto, and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus
Fluent in English

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
In 1966, in Marseille, Pierre Bellon laid the foundations of what would become Sodexo, guided by a simple yet powerful idea: improving everyday life through services that truly matter.

Today, Sodexo is a global leader in Food and Services, supporting people at every moment of life, at work, in schools, hospitals and communities. With 426,000 colleagues across 43 countries, we serve 80 million consumers every day, generating €24.1 billion in revenues. Our growth goes hand in hand with social and environmental responsibility, driven by our purpose: to create a better everyday for everyone and build a better life for all.




About Sodexo Business Services (SBS)




Portugal is no exception! Seven years ago, we established Sodexo Business Services (SBS), our shared service center. Here, you will have the opportunity to join various Financial teams such as Record to Report, Order to Cash, Purchase to Pay, Financial Analysis, and Master Data, supporting European markets and ensuring business growth.




Join Us!

We are looking for talented professionals to be part of our team, bringing unique skills and perspectives to help us grow even further.




Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for the technical execution of reporting and insight that enhances transparency & business performance with particular emphasis on digitization (Power BI), process efficiency & solution




⚙What will you do?

Enhancing our digital reporting suite & ensuring smooth execution of business-critical activities.
Demonstrate strong technical capability across reporting tools,
Accountable for scoping, designing, and delivering reporting developments.
Explore new ways to leverage reporting technologies, with a focus on building advanced proficiency in tools such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Ensure that reporting output and architecture is coherent and consistent with our governance framework and standards.
Evaluate reporting processes and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce non‑value‑adding analysis.
Engage proactively with stakeholders to deliver reporting and insights, building effective business partnerships and communicating data in a clear, relatable narrative.
Translate broad or unclear stakeholder requirements into structured reporting plans and deliverables.
Be able to shift between data modelling, content creation, commercial insight & business partnering.




🤙What You Need to Succeed:

Bachelor’s degree in relevant field
Fluency in English required, ability to speak other European language is a plus
Focus on produt excellence, integrity and costumer focus
Experiency in Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Continuous improvement mindset
Communication skills
Problem solving
Be flexible and versatile




What we have to offer you?

Hybrid working model;
Flexible working hours;
Health & Life Insurance;
Meal allowance paid in Meal Card;
Additional Days off: extra vacation day, employee’s birthday, volunteering day;
Opportunity to grow professionally inside the Company;
Possibility to participate in multicultural projects;
Several internal activities aiming to promote our team´s wellbeing.




📩 Apply now! Send your CV to Recruitment.SBS.Portugal@sodexo.com with the job title in the subject line. Explore all our career opportunities here: Sodexo Careers.

Learn more about Sodexo: YouTube - Sodexo.




Diversity & Inclusion Statement

Sodexo is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment based on merit.




🔒 Data Privacy:

We respect your privacy. Your personal data will be used exclusively for recruitment purposes, processed under GDPR regulations, and treated confidentially. For further details, contact privacy.sbs@sodexo.com.

Title: Analyst Trainee para a Unidade de Análise de Operações
Description:
Country: Portugal

COMEÇA AQUI

O Santander (www.santander.com) está a evoluir de uma marca global de grande impacto para uma organização orientada pela tecnologia, e as nossas pessoas estão no centro desta jornada.

Juntos, estamos a liderar uma transformação centrada no cliente, que valoriza o pensamento crítico, a inovação e a coragem de desafiar o que é possível.

Esta é mais do que uma mudança estratégica. É uma oportunidade para pessoas motivadas crescerem, aprenderem e fazerem uma diferença real.

A nossa missão é contribuir para que mais pessoas e empresas prosperem. Adotámos uma cultura de risco sólida, e todas as nossas pessoas, em todos os níveis, têm um papel ativo e responsável na gestão de riscos.

O TEU IMPACTO

O Santander Portugal está à procura de um Analyst Trainee para a Unidade de Análise de Operações motivado(a), com forte curiosidade intelectual e vontade de aprender, para integrar a equipa de Riscos de Crédito no Porto.

Nesta função, terás a oportunidade de trabalhar lado a lado com uma equipa experiente e multidisciplinar, desenvolvendo competências técnicas e analíticas essenciais na avaliação de risco de crédito em operações de financiamento de pequenos negócios ou empresas.

A área de riscos de Crédito é a unidade especializada na análise de operações de crédito propostas pelos nossos clientes em todos os segmentos de clientes do banco (Particulares, empresas e Grandes Empresas

Estamos a redefinir a forma como trabalhamos através da inovação, da tecnologia de ponta, da colaboração e da liberdade para explorar novas ideias.

Para ter sucesso nesta função, serás responsável por:


Analisar as propostas em fase de admissão que sejam remetidas pelo motor de decisão automático e que não sejam decididas por esse sistema para todos os produtos de retalho, para o segmento de Clientes Particulares, nomeadamente: crédito habitação, crédito ao consumo e cartões de crédito, avaliando a solvabilidade conforme as regras macroprudenciais e perfil dos clientes, garantindo o cumprimento dos critérios de avaliação de risco do Santander;
Cumprir com os níveis de serviço (SLA) estabelecidos, assegurando a resposta adequada e atempada no processo de admissão de risco de crédito particulares;
Analisar pedidos de alterações aos contratos em curso
Analisar pedidos de renegociação das condições contratuais motivadas por eventuais incapacidades de pagamento do crédito (Clientes PARI e PERSI), entre outros;
Decidir as operações analisadas dentro dos poderes de crédito delegados ou emitir parecer com apresentação e submissão das propostas a comissão de crédito;
Propor melhorias / fine tuning de regras de admissão que possam ser industrializadas e incorporadas no motor de decisão, com base na experiência da atividade gerida, que contribuam para o aumento da eficiência do sistema de admissão.


O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

Experiência Profissional


Experiência prévia em estágio (preferencialmente numa Big4) é valorizada, mas não obrigatória — o mais importante é a motivação e vontade de aprender.


Formação Académica


Formação académica concluída ou em fase de conclusão em Economia, Gestão, Finanças, Contabilidade, ou áreas similares.


Idiomas


Domínio de inglês.
Conhecimento de espanhol (valorizado).


Hard Skills


Bons conhecimentos de Microsoft Office, nomeadamente Excel avançado e Powerpoint (conhecimentos de Power BI são valorizados);


Soft Skills


Sentido de responsabilidade e compromisso com a qualidade do trabalho.
Atenção ao detalhe e foco no rigor.
Vontade genuína de aprender, crescer e assumir novos desafios.
Capacidade analítica e curiosidade intelectual.
Capacidade de gerir pressão e de manter a qualidade do trabalho em momentos de maior exigência.
Boa organização, capacidade de gerir prioridades.
Gosto por trabalhar em equipa e comunicar de forma clara.
Interesse por análise empresas, mercados financeiros e risco.


VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.


Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.


Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.
