  • Analisar pedidos de renegociação das condições contratuais motivadas por eventuais incapacidades de pagamento do crédito (Clientes PARI e PERSI), entre outros;
  • Decidir as operações analisadas dentro dos poderes de crédito delegados ou emitir parecer com apresentação e submissão das propostas a comissão de crédito;
  • Propor melhorias / fine tuning de regras de admissão que possam ser industrializadas e incorporadas no motor de decisão, com base na experiência da atividade gerida, que contribuam para o aumento da eficiência do sistema de admissão.

O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

Experiência Profissional

  • Experiência prévia em estágio (preferencialmente numa Big4) é valorizada, mas não obrigatória — o mais importante é a motivação e vontade de aprender.

Formação Académica

  • Formação académica concluída ou em fase de conclusão em Economia, Gestão, Finanças, Contabilidade, ou áreas similares.

Idiomas

  • Domínio de inglês.
  • Conhecimento de espanhol (valorizado).

Hard Skills

  • Bons conhecimentos de Microsoft Office, nomeadamente Excel avançado e Powerpoint (conhecimentos de Power BI são valorizados);

Soft Skills

  • Sentido de responsabilidade e compromisso com a qualidade do trabalho.
  • Atenção ao detalhe e foco no rigor.
  • Vontade genuína de aprender, crescer e assumir novos desafios.
  • Capacidade analítica e curiosidade intelectual.
  • Capacidade de gerir pressão e de manter a qualidade do trabalho em momentos de maior exigência.
  • Boa organização, capacidade de gerir prioridades.
  • Gosto por trabalhar em equipa e comunicar de forma clara.
  • Interesse por análise empresas, mercados financeiros e risco.

VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.

  • Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
  • A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
  • Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
  • Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
  • Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.

Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 We're Hiring | Python Developer – Risk Analytics & Process Automation




Are you passionate about Python, Data Analytics, and Financial Risk? We're looking for a Python Developer to join an international team focused on building innovative digital solutions that transform risk management processes.

Working alongside risk specialists and technology teams, you'll develop automation tools, interactive dashboards, and data-driven applications that improve efficiency, reduce operational risk, and support better business decision-making.




What you'll do

Design and develop Python-based solutions to automate risk management processes.
Analyse and re-engineer existing workflows to improve efficiency and user experience.
Build data processing tools and analytics solutions using modern Python libraries.
Collaborate closely with risk analysts to translate business requirements into technical solutions.
Develop dashboards, APIs, and automation tools following Agile and software engineering best practices.
Work with large datasets to generate meaningful insights and support decision-making.
Contribute to the continuous evolution of digital solutions used by international teams.




What we're looking for

Master's degree in Finance, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
2–5 years of experience in Financial Services, Risk Management, Data Analytics, or a similar environment.
Strong programming skills in Python.
Solid experience with:
Pandas
NumPy
Data analysis and data manipulation
Experience analysing large datasets and transforming business requirements into technical solutions.
Knowledge of Market Risk, Counterparty Risk, or Cross-Margining is highly valued.
Experience working in Agile environments is a plus.




Nice to have

Experience developing dashboards, APIs, or automation tools.
Familiarity with HTML, CSS, or JavaScript.
Interest in AI, process automation, or data-driven applications.




Soft skills

✔ Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities

✔ Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

✔ Collaborative mindset and team spirit

✔ Curious, proactive, and eager to learn

✔ Strong organisational and prioritisation skills

✔ Client-focused with attention to detail and quality




Language

Fluent English (mandatory)




Why join?

Work on innovative projects combining Python, Data Analytics, Automation, and Financial Risk.
Collaborate with international teams in a highly technical and business-focused environment.
Gain exposure to modern technologies, Agile practices, and AI-driven initiatives.
Make a real impact by developing tools that support critical risk management decisions.




📩 Interested? Apply now or send me a direct message to learn more about this opportunity.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.




As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Data Analyst!




Responsabilities:

Collect, process, and analyze large datasets to support the Global Markets Front Office.
Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL processes and automated data pipelines.
Build, optimize, and maintain data warehouses and data models to support business reporting and analytics.
Develop dashboards, reports, and data visualizations that provide actionable business insights.
Automate data processing and reporting workflows using Python and SQL.
Collaborate with business sponsors, Sales, Traders, and technical teams to understand data requirements and deliver analytical solutions.
Perform data validation, quality assurance, and reconciliation to ensure data accuracy and consistency.
Translate business requirements into technical data solutions and analytical models.
Support the industrialization and optimization of data processing activities.
Identify opportunities to improve data quality, reporting efficiency, and operational processes.
Document data flows, ETL processes, and analytical solutions following best practices.




Requirements:

Technical Skills

Experience in Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, or Data Engineering roles.
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL for data processing, automation, and analysis.
Experience designing and maintaining ETL processes and data pipelines.
Solid knowledge of Data Warehousing concepts, data modeling, and relational databases.
Experience building reports, dashboards, and data visualizations using BI tools.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and data interpretation skills.
Experience working with large and complex datasets.
Knowledge of data quality, data validation, and reporting best practices.
Ability to gather business requirements and translate them into technical data solutions.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with experience working directly with business users and technical teams.
Good level of english.




Nice to Have

Experience within Global Markets, Capital Markets, Investment Banking, or Financial Services.
Knowledge of Front Office business processes and financial products.
Experience with data automation and process industrialization initiatives.
Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms and modern analytics ecosystems.
Experience working in Agile environments.




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us:

Long-term projects with national and international context (if applicable)
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com with reference “CA/DataAnalyst".




Thank You! 😊




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data Analyst – Commercial (m/f/d)
HTML:
Chemical Industry | Data Analyst – Commercial



Your new Company


Our client is a Global Recognized Organization operating at the forefront of advanced materials and specialty solutions. With a strong international footprint and a workforce of around 1 000 professionals.


Driven by deep scientific expertise and a strong commitment to innovation, the Organization leverages advanced capabilities in material sciences, formulation technologies, and surface modification to deliver tailored solutions.


With a clear focus on innovation, operational excellence, and professional development, this is an opportunity to join a Company that combines global scale with a dynamic and forward-thinking mindset.



Your new Role


The Data Analyst – Commercial transforms complex Datasets into clear, actionable insights that support decision making across the organization. Working within a Matrix structure and closely aligned with the Commercial function, this person partners with business stakeholders to understand priorities, interpret commercial Data, and deliver high-quality analysis, reporting, dashboards, and presentations.


This person reports to the Business Data Manager, and will collaborate closely with Commercial stakeholders, Business Leaders, Technical Specialists, and Data Governance teams to support performance cycles, improve reporting capabilities, and strengthen data-driven decision making across the business.


The Data Analyst – Commercial will:

  • Partner with Commercial stakeholders to understand business priorities and deliver Data-driven insights that support operational and strategic decision-making;
  • Produce accurate and timely management reporting, analyzing commercial performance and identifying trends, risks, opportunities, and deviations from plan;
  • Support functional performance cycles (including sales pipeline reviews, demand planning, SKU rationalization activities, and sales forecasting);
  • Execute and support Data creation, updates, clean-up, archiving, and deletion activities related to customer Data, pricing, rebates, discounts, commissions, sales areas, and commercial taxonomies;
  • Ensure commercial Data is accurate and consistent across business systems, validating and preparing Datasets in line with Data governance standards;
  • Act as a subject matter expert in SalesForce, providing user support, training, guidance, and continuous improvement recommendations;
  • Develop, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards, reports, and visualizations to improve visibility of commercial performance and decision-making;
  • Prepare and deliver reports, presentations, and commercial insights for sales meetings, customer reviews, and other business reviews;
  • Prepare reports linking SalesForce and Sales Data and support updates of R&D/NBO pipeline information for internal and external communications;
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to improve Data quality, analytical capabilities, reporting processes, and business tools;


A typical day will include analyzing commercial performance, maintaining dashboards, supporting sales and demand planning activities, attending sales meetings, validating Data quality, supporting SalesForce users, and delivering insights that support key business decisions.


Please consider that this role requires availability for occasional travel to other company sites, approximately once per year.



What you will need to succeed


As a Data Analyst – Commercial, you will need:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Information Systems, Data Management, or another analytical or business-related field;
  • +3 years of experience within an International Manufacturing or Industrial environment;
  • +3 years of experience in analytical or support roles within Sales, Commercial, or related business functions;
  • Proven experience developing and maintaining Power BI dashboards, reports, and Data visualizations that deliver actionable business insights;
  • Experience supporting demand planning, sales performance reviews, pipeline analysis, customer Data management, pricing, and commercial reporting;
  • Advanced Data collection, cleansing, validation, and reporting skills;
  • Experience working within Matrix Organizations and collaborating with Cross-Functional Teams (would be a plus);
  • Advanced Excel skills (including Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP, advanced functions, and graphs);
  • Experience with project management tools;
  • Knowledge of Python, SQL, and SharePoint management (would be a plus);
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality outputs in a fast-paced international environment;
  • Excellent communication skills – Fluency in English.


What the Company can offer you


Have the opportunity to join a cross-functional team in an international company with a multicultural working environment!



Next Steps


If you are interested in this opportunity, please send us your updated CV. If you are looking for another type of professional challenge, please contact us to discuss other career opportunities, always in complete confidence.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Sobre a equipa

Como Data Analyst farás parte da nossa equipa de Data Intelligence, onde oferecemos soluções inovadoras na área da saúde para melhorar a eficiência e a qualidade do percurso do paciente, antecipando todas as suas necessidades.

Sobre a role


Criar e manter dashboards e relatórios em Power BI para diferentes perfis de utilizador — desde gestores operacionais a equipas clínicas
Colaborar com stakeholders das unidades de saúde para levantar requisitos, validar métricas e garantir que os outputs respondem às necessidades reais
Desenvolver e manter pipelines de dados (ETL/SSIS) para integração de fontes heterogéneas presentes em unidades de saúde
Construir e otimizar queries T-SQL para extração, transformação e análise de dados clínicos e operacionais
Garantir a qualidade, consistência e documentação dos dados ao longo do ciclo de vida dos produtos
Participar ativamente na implementação dos produtos de dados nos clientes, incluindo formação e suporte


Que skills procuramos?


2 a 4 anos de experiência em funções de análise de dados ou Business Intelligence
Conhecimento e experiência em SQL / T-SQL
Experiência prática com SSIS ou processos de ETL equivalentes
Experiência prática com SSAS, Modelação e Calculations MDX
Capacidade para desenvolver relatórios e dashboards em Power BI (modelação de dados, DAX, boas práticas de visualização)
Familiaridade com o setor da saúde — sistemas de informação clínica, terminologias ou processos hospitalares são valorizados
Perfil analítico, orientado ao detalhe e com boa capacidade de comunicação com perfis não técnicos


Valorizamos ainda:


Experiência na área da saúde
Autonomia na gestão de tarefas e proatividade


Quem somos?

Somos a Glintt Global, uma empresa de referência na Península Ibérica em Consultoria e Serviços Tecnológicos, com mais de 30 anos de experiência. Pretendemos impactar a qualidade de vida das pessoas, através da inovação, tecnologia, do conhecimento e da ambição que nos acompanham. Este é o nosso compromisso!

Encontra aqui mais sobre a Cultura global | Glintt Global!

Somos a Glintt Global, uma empresa de referência na Península Ibérica em Consultoria e Serviços Tecnológicos, com mais de 30 anos de experiência. Pretendemos impactar a qualidade de vida das pessoas, através da inovação, tecnologia, do conhecimento e da ambição que nos acompanham. Este é o nosso compromisso!

Who are we ?

Quanteam, founding entity of RAINBOW PARTNERS, is a consulting firm specialized in Banking and Financial Services. With more than 1200 consultants across 10 countries, we combine business and IT expertise to support leading institutions in transformation projects.

Title: Data Analyst (m/f/d)
Place: Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto, Portugal
Description:
A NIW é uma empresa de Consultoria que atua em diversas áreas, com destaque para as Tecnologias de Informação. Fazemos parte da Salvador Caetano e a nossa missão é acompanhar, apoiar e assegurar os diversos negócios do Grupo.




Sabias que o setor automóvel é um dos setores de maior inovação e de reinvenção na atualidade? A tecnologia e a digitalização são fatores chave para a sua evolução.




Se estás à procura de desafios em diversas áreas, não gostas de rotina e queres evoluir, este é o lugar certo para ti! Contamos contigo para juntos fazermos avançar o negócio!




Sobre a Função:

Para integrar a equipa de Analytics, procuramos alguém responsável por garantir que os dados certos chegam, no formato certo, às pessoas certas — transformando dados brutos em informação fiável, bem estruturada e pronta para análise.




Transformação e preparação de dados

- Recolher, limpar e transformar dados provenientes de múltiplas fontes

- Extrair, limpar e transformar dados provenientes de múltiplas fontes (bases de dados, APIs, ficheiros, cloud), utilizando SQL como linguagem principal

- Garantir a qualidade, consistência e integridade dos dados ao longo de todo o pipeline analítico

- Documentar regras de negócio, transformações aplicadas e critérios de validação de dados

- Identificar e resolver proativamente problemas de qualidade de dados antes de chegarem ao modelo, assim como a preocupação constante da performance da solução




Modelação para o modelo semântico (Power BI)

- Construir e manter modelos semânticos robustos no Power BI, de acordo com as normas e boas práticas definidas na equipa

- Otimizar o modelo para performance — redução de cardinalidade, gestão de DirectQuery vs Import, medidas DAX, etc

- Assegurar que o modelo semântico é reutilizável, escalável e auditável por outros elementos da equipa




Análise de dados

- Questionar os dados antes de os apresentar — validar fontes, identificar inconsistências e garantir que os números fazem sentido de negócio




Colaboração com equipas de dados

- Trabalhar com Data Engineers na definição de contratos de dados e estrutura de tabelas analíticas

- Explorar e consumir dados a partir de Lakehouses e Warehouses no Microsoft Fabric




O que valorizamos:

- Experiência de 2–3 anos em funções de análise ou engenharia analítica de dados

- Experiência intermédia/avançada com SQL — joins, CTEs, agregações, subqueries

- Experiência prática com Power BI e familiaridade com o ecossistema Microsoft Fabric

- Bons conhecimentos em construção de modelos dimensionais no Power BI — relações, hierarquias, tabelas de datas

- Bons conhecimentos em medidas de complexidade média/avançada — time intelligence, CALCULATE, variáveis, iteradores

- Boa capacidade de comunicação escrita e oral, adaptando o discurso à audiência

- Orientação para a qualidade — preocupação genuína com a fiabilidade e rastreabilidade dos dados

- Curiosidade analítica — vai além da pergunta inicial e procura o contexto que dá sentido aos números

- Proatividade e pensamento crítico




O que oferecemos:

- Dia extra de férias

- Soluções de trabalho flexível

- Comparticipação das despesas de saúde extensível ao agregado familiar

- Atribuição de subsídios de apoio aos projetos de vida pessoal e familiar

- Programa de benefícios flexíveis

- Descontos na compra de viaturas representadas pelo Grupo

- Vantagens na aquisição de produtos/serviços em diversas áreas (lazer, tecnologia, bem-estar, educação…)

- Seguro de vida

- Fundo de pensões

- Formação e desenvolvimento contínuo

- Oportunidades de progressão de carreira num grupo com forte representação nacional e internacional

- Integração em equipa dinâmica com espírito jovem.




Na Salvador Caetano ajudamos as Pessoas a moverem-se.




Na Salvador Caetano queremos contribuir para um futuro inclusivo e representativo. Acreditamos que a diversidade de talento empodera as nossas equipas, cria ambientes propícios à inovação e contribui para a evolução do negócio. Aqui incentivamos a diferença e incluímo-la. Independentemente da idade, género, etnia ou religião, promovemos a igualdade de oportunidades e garante a integração numa cultura baseada na dignidade e respeito por todas as pessoas.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.




Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid







What will you do as Data Analyst:

Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.




Key qualifications:

Must have:

Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.




Nice have:

Familiarity with emissions data or GHG concepts is an advantage but not required.
Comfortable in repetitive, detail-oriented validation work.




Personal skills:

Strong attention to detail and careful execution.
Strong communication and reliability under time pressure.




What ISS Offers You:

We focus on creating an environment where you can grow and perform at your best:

Permanent contract.
Private health insurance.
22 days of annual leave + 3 additional company days to recharge.
Flexible working hours and a hybrid model that supports balance and collaboration.
Continuous learning opportunities (technical and behavioural development) to support your growth.
Modern, well-equipped office in the heart of Porto.
Free parking for your convenience.
Access to ISS Tech Communities to connect, learn and share knowledge.
Free access to the company gym to support your wellbeing.
Snacks and drinks available at the office.




Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. By creating exceptional service moments and transforming workplaces into spaces where employees feel valued, engaged and productive, we enhance productivity and help our customers to attract talent and grow their businesses. This begins with our own people through training, career development, and a supportive culture – empowering them to deliver outstanding service. We know that when our people thrive, they create spaces where our customers’ employees and businesses thrive too.




ISS is a Place to Be You.

Be who you are. Become what you want. Be part of something bigger.

Become more. Become ISS.




ISS seeks to BE a place of belonging and CREATE places where every person is welcomed, embraced, and valued for exactly who they are. Places where people feel safe, respected, represented, and supported as their authentic selves.

Title: Data analyst / Controller Júnior
Description:
Sobre Nós:

Somos uma Travel Management Company (TMC) com foco em oferecer soluções inovadoras e personalizadas para os nossos clientes. Procuramos uma pessoa analítica, rigorosa e com forte apetência tecnológica, capaz de compreender rapidamente novos sistemas e trabalhar com grandes volumes de informação.

Procuramos alguém que compreenda a lógica dos dados, consiga validar informação, identificar inconsistências e desenvolver ferramentas que tornem os nossos processos mais eficientes.




Responsabilidades:

Conferência e validação de faturação de clientes e fornecedores.
Preparação, tratamento e análise de dados.
Manutenção de dashboards em Power BI.
Utilização de SQL para extração, validação e análise de informação.
Criação e melhoria de ferramentas de apoio à gestão.
Identificação de erros, inconsistências e oportunidades de melhoria.
Aprendizagem e utilização de plataformas especializadas de Business Travel, nomeadamente o Tripoint.




O perfil que procuramos

Formação superior em Gestão, Economia, Engenharia, Informática ou área semelhante.
Excel avançado.
Bons conhecimentos de SQL.
Experiência em Power BI (Power Query, modelação de dados e DAX).
Facilidade em aprender novas aplicações e plataformas tecnológicas.
Capacidade para compreender a lógica de bases de dados e a relação entre tabelas.
Elevada capacidade analítica e espírito crítico.
Rigor, atenção ao detalhe e gosto por trabalhar com números.
Capacidade para organizar informação e transformar dados em conhecimento útil para o negócio.




Será valorizado

Experiência em Business Intelligence ou Data Analytics.
Experiência com ERPs ou sistemas de gestão.
Conhecimentos do setor das viagens corporativas (Business Travel).

Title: Data Analyst | Porto | Hybrid
Description:
We’re looking for the special, unique and amazing YOU!




@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, team work.

At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability or any factor other than merit. All applications with skills for the position are welcome!




We are looking for an Amazing: DATA ANALYST

Seniority: 3+

Type of position: Hybrid @Porto




The amazing you, will have:




3 to 6 years of experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role.
Strong SQL skills.
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools.
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Excel.
Experience working with relational databases.
Good understanding of data modelling and data warehousing concepts.
Experience gathering and analysing business requirements.
Knowledge of ETL processes is a plus.
Familiarity with Python or R for data analysis is considered an advantage.
Experience working in Agile environments is valued.
Good communication and stakeholder management skills.
Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail.
Fluency in English.




Why should you become part of our family?

You can develop a career that fits you: your career development is personalized, taking in consideration your needs and goals from a short to long term;

Interesting Challenges Ahead: you can work for several clients from different sectors of activity; Free training programs: Our training and certification programs in languages, tech, behavior and business will help you to reach your full potential faster International projects in Benelux: you can gain international experience in Benelux and balance a new way of living with work Type of projects (depending on the project you might find one of this types of projects):In hybrid Systems: Is important to balance work with socialization, that´s why a hybrid system works for us and for you Full Remote projects: If you want to work while you enjoy the comfort of your home Full Onsite projects: if you preferer the company of your colleagues! Take care of your well-being: Enjoy our free nutrition, psychologist, general medicine appointments and our yoga and personal training days… all remote!




Who are we?

Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Innovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto, Leiria and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.

Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Innovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto, Leiria and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.

Title: Business Data Analyst (Maintenance)
Description:
Imagine your career taking you to the depths of innovation and the heights of impact. Our people enable continuous progress. Their commitment, collective expertise, and unique capabilities are the engine room behind SBM Offshore’s True. Blue. Transition. - shaping the future of energy, and beyond.

About Us:

SBM Offshore is a global leader in deepwater ocean infrastructure, delivering floating production solutions across the full asset lifecycle—from design and construction to installation and operation. Supported by a global team of more than 8,000 professionals, the Company operates a long-term, asset-backed business model that delivers high-availability assets and predictable cash flows. SBM Offshore combines engineering expertise, operational reliability, and selective innovation to support safe, efficient, and lower-carbon energy production, while extending its capabilities into new opportunities across the blue economy.

Purpose



As a Business Data Analyst, you analyze and interpret complex data to provide actionable insights that support business decision-making and strategy development. Work closely with stakeholders to identify data needs, create reports, and develop data models that drive business performance and optimize operational efficiency. Ensure data accuracy and contribute to the continuous improvement of data processes and tools.



Responsibilities



Analyze and interpret complex data sets to provide actionable insights that support business decision-making and strategy development.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify business needs and data requirements, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and objectives.
Develop and maintain data models, dashboards, and reports, providing clear and concise visualizations that communicate key findings to stakeholders.
Ensure data accuracy and integrity by performing data validation, cleansing, and quality checks, maintaining high standards of data governance.
Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources into analytical tools, ensuring data is prepared and structured for analysis.
Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies within data sets, providing insights that drive business process improvements and operational efficiency.
Support the development and implementation of data-driven strategies, working closely with business units to optimize performance and achieve key objectives.
Present findings and recommendations to senior management and stakeholders, translating complex data insights into understandable and actionable business terms.
Stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies in data analytics, integrating new tools and methodologies to enhance analytical capabilities.
Provide training and support to team members and other departments on data analysis techniques and the use of analytical tools, fostering a data-driven culture within the organization.



Education



Bachelor's degree in data science, business analytics, computer science, statistics, or a related field is required. Certifications such as Certified Business Analysis Professional (CBAP), Microsoft Certified: Data Analyst Associate, or Certified Analytics Professional (CAP) are highly recommended. Proficiency in data analysis, statistical tools, and business intelligence (BI) platforms is essential. A master’s degree in a related field is preferred but not mandatory.



Experience



Minimum of 5 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field, with demonstrated expertise in analyzing business data to support decision-making processes.
Proven experience in collecting, cleaning, and analyzing large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights that drive business performance.
Strong technical background with hands-on experience in using data analysis tools and languages such as SQL, Python, R, or Excel, and proficiency in business intelligence platforms like Power BI, Tableau, or Qlik.
Demonstrated ability to develop and implement data models, reports, and dashboards that provide actionable insights to stakeholders across the organization.
Experience in working closely with business units to understand their data needs, translating business requirements into data-driven solutions that support strategic goals.
Proficiency in data visualization techniques, with the ability to present complex data in a clear and compelling manner to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Knowledge of data governance, data quality management, and best practices for ensuring data integrity and accuracy across systems.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, with the ability to identify data-related issues and recommend effective solutions to improve business processes.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams, including IT, finance, and operations, to support data-driven initiatives.
Relevant certifications such as Certified Business Analysis Professional (CBAP), Microsoft Certified: Data Analyst Associate, or Certified Analytics Professional (CAP) are preferred.



Functional Competencies

Compliance

Governance, Risk and Control

Analytics and reporting

IT Tools and application

Cost and budget control

Business Partnering

Digital savvy

Business Acumen

Contract Management

Management of change application

GENERAL INFORMATION

SBM Offshore N.V. is a listed holding company that is headquartered in Amsterdam. It holds direct and indirect interests in other companies that collectively with SBM Offshore N.V. form the SBM Offshore Group (“the Company”). For further information, please visit our website at www.sbmoffshore.com. The companies in which SBM Offshore N.V., directly and indirectly, owns investments are separate entities.

SBM Offshore is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. SBM Offshore does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, non-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status, or any other basis covered by appropriate law. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need.

Title: Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst
Description:
HBK is a leading organization in the testing and measurement industry that is on a journey to ensure it leverages data-driven insights to make strategic business decisions. We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Business Intelligence Analyst to join our dynamic team. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in enhancing our data analytics and reporting capabilities. The team is currently rebuilding our data landscape, and this is a real opportunity to drive change within the business area.

Job Summary:

As a Business Intelligence Analyst, you will play a key role in gathering, analyzing, and transforming data into actionable insights that drive informed decision-making at HBK. Drawing upon your extensive expertise in Business Intelligence, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver advanced data solutions and enhance operational efficiency.

Key Responsibilities:


Data Strategy: Develop and execute a comprehensive data strategy that leverages our data assets to meet the organization’s analytical needs and objectives.
Advanced Analytics: Apply advanced statistical and predictive analytics techniques to uncover trends, patterns, and insights in data, enabling more informed decision-making.
Reporting Excellence: Lead the design and maintenance of sophisticated, interactive reports and dashboards using modern business intelligence and data visualization platforms (SAP Analytics Cloud, Power BI, or similar tools). Create highly visual and intuitive data visualizations for use by business stakeholders.
Data Integration and Governance: Oversee the integration of data from diverse sources, ensuring data quality, consistency, and adherence to data governance policies. Collaborate with IT and data engineering teams to optimize data pipelines.
Performance Optimization: Continuously enhance the performance of our systems, including optimizing data processing and reporting capabilities.
Business Requirements Analysis: Work closely with business leaders to understand their analytical needs and translate these requirements into actionable data solutions.
Data Modeling Expertise: Design and implement complex data models to facilitate advanced reporting and analytics, encompassing both logical and physical data models.
Data Security and Compliance: Ensure strict data security and compliance with internal policies and industry regulations throughout all data handling and reporting activities.
Mentorship and Training: Provide guidance and training to other analysts and end-users, fostering self-service analytics adoption.


Qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Business, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field (Master's degree preferred).
A minimum of 3 years of experience as a Business Intelligence Analyst.
In-depth expertise in data warehousing, ETL processes, data modeling, and advanced analytics techniques.
Proficiency in SQL and familiarity with data visualization tools.
Exceptional problem-solving skills and analytical thinking.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Proven ability to work effectively within cross-functional teams.
Knowledge of industry best practices and emerging trends in business intelligence and data analytics.


Additional Information:


Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package.
Opportunities for career growth and professional development within our organization.
A collaborative and innovative work environment equipped with state-of-the-art data analytics tools.


If you are a seasoned professional with a passion for driving advanced data-driven decision-making, we invite you to apply and become a pivotal part of our team at HBK. Join us in shaping the future of data analytics for our business's success.

We Offer

Freedom with responsibility is the framework for HBK’s employees. This allows for a good balance between work and family life and for constant development of professional and personal skills in an international and enjoyable working environment.

One Company – HBK

On 1 January 2019, Brüel & Kjær and HBM merged their activities into a new company called HBK (Hottinger Brüel & Kjær). HBK is a subsidiary of Spectris Limited, and employees 3,500 employees worldwide and production facilities in Denmark, Germany, the UK, the USA, China and Portugal and presence in 80 countries, HBK is a leading player in the test and measurement area. For further info please visit www.hbkworld.com.

ALTEN is a multinational group created more than 30 years ago, specialized in consulting services, information technology and engineering services to the main companies in the market. We achieved a turnover of more than 4.14 billion euros in 2024 and now have 57 700 employees, 88% of whom are consulting engineers.

ABOUT PORTO & POSITION:

To support our European intensive growth, the Group has decided to build a Center based in Porto with the aim to provide administrative and financial support to the European subsidiaries. Support budgeting, forecasting, and monthly closing activities,

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data Analyst

This is your mission

Are you passionate about transforming data into actionable insights? Do you enjoy working closely with business stakeholders and building analytical solutions that drive decision-making? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst, you will work at the intersection of business and technology, helping transform complex data into meaningful insights, dashboards, reports, and automation solutions. You will collaborate directly with business stakeholders to understand their challenges, identify opportunities, and deliver data-driven solutions that support strategic decision-making.

Build Side


Gather and analyze business requirements and translate them into analytical solutions.
Extract, prepare, and analyze complex datasets from multiple data sources.
Design and develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using modern BI tools.
Build automated reporting solutions to improve business efficiency and decision-making.
Develop data analysis workflows using Python and SQL.
Contribute to the industrialization of data feeds, ETL processes, and data quality initiatives.
Create data models and analytical frameworks to generate business insights.
Partner with technical teams to deliver scalable and sustainable analytics solutions.


Run Side


Maintain and enhance existing dashboards, reports, and analytical tools.
Monitor data quality and ensure the reliability of reporting solutions.
Support business users by providing analysis and recommendations based on data findings.
Present analytical results and insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and improve operational efficiency.
Troubleshoot reporting and data-related issues.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams in an agile and fast-paced environment.
Continuously improve reporting standards, methodologies, and visualization practices.


This is your background

You have experience working with data analysis, reporting, and business intelligence solutions. You enjoy solving business problems through data and are comfortable interacting with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

You are curious, analytical, and motivated by transforming data into impactful business decisions

This Is Your Expertise


Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field.
Professional experience in Data Analysis or Business Intelligence roles.
Strong experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI.
Experience with data analysis using Python, particularly Pandas.
Good SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation.
Ability to analyze, synthesize, and simplify complex information.
Experience creating dashboards and presenting analytical findings to stakeholders.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Familiarity with Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto, or Airflow is a plus.
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus.
Experience working in financial services, capital markets, or trading environments is considered an advantage.


This is how you work as part of a team:


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Ability to explain complex concepts in a clear and concise manner.
Business-oriented mindset with strong stakeholder management skills.
Curious and proactive approach to identifying improvement opportunities.
Passion for automation and process optimization.
Adaptable and comfortable working in dynamic environments.
Enjoys tackling programming and analytical challenges.


Language Skills :


English: C1


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The recruitment process:


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Location: Lisbon or Porto

Contract: Permanent contract

Remote work: Hybrid (50%)

Salary: From 28K€ to 40K€ (depending on experience) gross annual salary

Job category: Data Analytics / Business Intelligence / Data Visualization

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.

Title: Procurement Data & Business Analyst
Description:
Hydro Extrusions is a world-leading aluminium extrusion business counting around 100 production sites in 40 countries and employing 20,000 people. Through our unique combination of local expertise, global network, and unmatched R&D capabilities, we can offer everything from standard profiles, to advanced development and manufacturing for most industries.




About The Job

We are seeking a highly analytical Procurement Data & Business Analyst to join the Hydro Extrusions Procurement team, a strategic organization that oversees spend within several important indirect categories across sites all over the world.

In close collaboration with Category Managers, you will play a critical role ensuring that procurement decisions are based on accurate data and insightful analysis. In this position, you will have to structure and analyze complex datasets to provide actionable recommendations that will support sourcing decisions, cost optimizations and supplier performance.

This role operates across EU and NA regions, in a fast-paced and imperfect data environment. It requires strong problem-solving skills and the ability to bring structure to complexity with a pragmatic, solution-oriented and results-driven mindset.




What you will be doing

In close partnership with Category Managers, you will play a key role in driving procurement excellence through data.

Data analysis:

Collect, clean, structure and analyze procurement data from multiple sources (multiple ERP systems, supplier reports, sites databases).
Develop and maintain Excel-based tools (databases, bid sheets, assessments files) to run wide tenders and support decision-making.
Analyze supplier performance, pricing, cost structure and spend to provide actionable insights and identify savings opportunities.
Contribute to supplier negotiations and strategic decisions with clear and structures analysis.
Create and maintain dashboards to track savings and categories procurement strategies implementation.

Stakeholder management:

Support execution of complex tenders involving cross-functional teams and stakeholders in different locations.
Provide solutions and recommendations even when data is incomplete or imperfect, ensuring projects move forward with pragmatic and data-driven recommendations.
Uphold high personal standards and expectations for quality and impact.




What will make you successful?

Required Skills & Qualifications

Degree in Mathematics, Business, Economics, Engineering or similar analytical fields.
Experience in Procurement, Project Management or related Business-oriented role is a plus.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH, data modeling, complex formulas and VBA Macros).
Ability to work in project-based environment, interpret large datasets, and turn complexity into clear insights and pragmatic recommendations, while ensuring continued projects execution.
Excellent communication skills to present insights clearly to different stakeholders across multiple locations (proficiency in both Excel and PowerPoint).
Comfortable working with imperfect data and making sound decisions under uncertainty while maintaining accuracy and delivering results.
Fluency in English is essential, while proficiency in other European languages is a plus.

If you recognize yourself in the description below, don’t wait to apply!

You enjoy solving complex problems and turning data into clear, actionable insights.
You are comfortable working with imperfect and incomplete data and thrive finding and bringing structure into it.
You know how to build comprehensive analyses and present them in a structured, synthetic, clear and visual way.
You are curious, proactive, adaptable and like to challenge the status quo.
You are comfortable working in a fast-paced project-based environment handling multiple tasks in parallel.
You are excited to navigate a dynamic matrix organization and travel up to 25%-30% of time.
Self-motivated, you are able to perform under time constraints and pressure.
Excel is your playground; experience with Power Query, Power BI is a plus.




What we offer you

Whether it’s developing skills or advancing your expertise even further, we’ll support you with continuous learning and development opportunities, to help discuss and steer your long-term Norsk Hydro career path. Opportunity to work in an international, multi-site automotive environment
Professional development in procurement analytics and category management
Hybrid working model
Stable employment in a global industrial organization




Equal opportunities

Hydro values diverse skills and perspectives among employees. We encourage all qualified candidates to apply. Qualified applicants will be considered regardless of race, religion, nationality, ethnicity, age, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability. We strive to provide equal opportunities for all to contribute and succeed with us.

Hydro Extrusions is a world-leading aluminium extrusion business counting around 100 production sites in 40 countries and employing 20,000 people. Through our unique combination of local expertise, global network, and unmatched R&D capabilities, we can offer everything from standard profiles, to advanced development and manufacturing for most industries.


About The Job

We are seeking a highly analytical Procurement Data & Business Analyst to join the Hydro Extrusions Procurement team, a strategic organization that oversees spend within several important indirect categories across sites all over the world.

In close collaboration with Category Managers, you will play a critical role ensuring that procurement decisions are based on accurate data and insightful analysis. In this position, you will have to structure and analyze complex datasets to provide actionable recommendations that will support sourcing decisions, cost optimizations and supplier performance.

This role operates across EU and NA regions, in a fast-paced and imperfect data environment. It requires strong problem-solving skills and the ability to bring structure to complexity with a pragmatic, solution-oriented and results-driven mindset.


What you will be doing

In close partnership with Category Managers, you will play a key role in driving procurement excellence through data.

Data analysis:

  • Collect, clean, structure and analyze procurement data from multiple sources (multiple ERP systems, supplier reports, sites databases).
  • Develop and maintain Excel-based tools (databases, bid sheets, assessments files) to run wide tenders and support decision-making.
  • Analyze supplier performance, pricing, cost structure and spend to provide actionable insights and identify savings opportunities.
  • Contribute to supplier negotiations and strategic decisions with clear and structures analysis.
  • Create and maintain dashboards to track savings and categories procurement strategies implementation.

Stakeholder management:

  • Support execution of complex tenders involving cross-functional teams and stakeholders in different locations.
  • Provide solutions and recommendations even when data is incomplete or imperfect, ensuring projects move forward with pragmatic and data-driven recommendations.
  • Uphold high personal standards and expectations for quality and impact.


What will make you successful?

Required Skills & Qualifications

  • Degree in Mathematics, Business, Economics, Engineering or similar analytical fields.
  • Experience in Procurement, Project Management or related Business-oriented role is a plus.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH, data modeling, complex formulas and VBA Macros).
  • Ability to work in project-based environment, interpret large datasets, and turn complexity into clear insights and pragmatic recommendations, while ensuring continued projects execution.
  • Excellent communication skills to present insights clearly to different stakeholders across multiple locations (proficiency in both Excel and PowerPoint).
  • Comfortable working with imperfect data and making sound decisions under uncertainty while maintaining accuracy and delivering results.
  • Fluency in English is essential, while proficiency in other European languages is a plus.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
🚀 Procuramos um(a) Data Engineer para integrar uma equipa focada na construção de pipelines de dados robustos, escaláveis e preparados para ambientes modernos de dados.




Este é um papel com forte componente técnica, onde vais trabalhar com processamento batch e real-time, otimização de performance e colaboração próxima com equipas de dados e produto. A fluência em inglês é essencial, já que a colaboração diária será feita com stakeholders internacionais.




🧑 💻 Como será o teu dia a dia:

Desenhar, desenvolver, testar e fazer deploy de pipelines de dados batch e real-time com Python e Spark / PySpark
Otimizar armazenamento de dados e performance de queries em data lakes, data warehouses e formatos modernos como Delta Lake ou Iceberg
Escrever código limpo, maintainable e bem documentado
Participar em code reviews e promover boas práticas de engenharia, incluindo CI/CD e unit testing
Garantir a fiabilidade dos pipelines através de monitorização, logging e alerting
Fazer troubleshooting e resolver falhas em pipelines de dados
Colaborar com Data Scientists, Analysts e Product Managers para traduzir necessidades em soluções técnicas
Otimizar jobs de Spark com foco em performance, memória e eficiência de custo




🧠 O que procuramos:

Entre 5 e 7 anos de experiência prática em funções de Data Engineering
Proficiência avançada em Python, incluindo OOP, pandas e frameworks de testes
Experiência forte e prática com Apache Spark, especialmente PySpark
Excelente domínio de SQL
Experiência com bases de dados relacionais como PostgreSQL e MySQL, e com data warehouses como Snowflake, BigQuery ou Redshift
Experiência com pelo menos um grande cloud provider: AWS, GCP ou Azure
Experiência com ferramentas de orquestração como Apache Airflow, Prefect ou Dagster
Inglês excelente, escrito e falado, para colaboração diária em contexto internacional




⭐ Valorizamos ainda:

Experiência com arquiteturas modernas de dados
Capacidade para trabalhar em ambientes escaláveis e orientados a performance
Espírito colaborativo e atenção à qualidade de código
Capacidade para transformar requisitos de negócio em soluções técnicas robustas




💡 O que oferecemos:

Projeto desafiante com forte componente técnica
Ambiente colaborativo e orientado a boas práticas de engenharia
Oportunidade de trabalhar com tecnologias modernas de dados
Contexto internacional e de aprendizagem contínua
Full visa support, se necessário




📍 Localização: Porto, Portugal

⚠️ Modelo: Híbrido




Pacote Salarial Junior: 12.880€ a 21.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Pacote Salarial Mid: 14.000€ a 28.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Pacote Salarial Senior: 20.000€ a 49.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Title: Data analyst & dashboard designer
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data analyst & dashboard designer

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data analytics, cybersecurity risk management and data visualization? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst & Dashboard Designer, you will be responsible for leading end-to-end dashboard initiatives within a large-scale international cybersecurity environment, contributing to Enterprise Security activities and cyber risk governance.

Build Side


Understand business requirements and identify relevant KPIs related to cybersecurity risk management
Design and implement robust data models to support cyber risk dashboards
Collect, clean and transform large datasets using ETL tools
Develop and maintain interactive dashboards using Power BI and Tableau
Use Dataiku and PostgreSQL for advanced data processing and statistical analysis
Leverage machine learning algorithms to uncover hidden correlations and generate additional insights
Continuously improve data models to enhance accuracy, usability and performance


Run Side


Ensure data accuracy, consistency and availability across dashboards
Monitor data quality and proactively address inconsistencies
Present findings and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Collaborate with cybersecurity, IT risk and resilience teams across multiple locations
Manage multiple dashboard projects simultaneously with autonomy and strong time management
Contribute to the continuous improvement of dashboard production processes


This is your background

You have at least 3 years of experience as a Data Analyst in demanding, data-driven environments.

You enjoy transforming complex datasets into meaningful visual insights and are constantly looking for ways to innovate through data science and automation. You are looking for an environment where your ideas matter and where technology goes hand in hand with collaboration.

This Is Your Expertise


Power BI & Tableau (dashboard design and advanced DataViz)
Dataiku (data preparation, data science workflows)
PostgreSQL (data extraction, transformation and querying)
ETL tools and data modeling best practices
Statistical analysis and basic machine learning concepts
Basic Python skills
Microsoft collaboration tools (PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams)


Certifications such as Tableau Certified Data Analyst or Dataiku Advanced Designer are a plus.

Languag Skills


English: C1


This is how you work as part of a team:


Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
Excellent communication skills in English (French is a plus)
Ability to collaborate effectively with distributed cybersecurity teams
Autonomous, organized and capable of managing multiple priorities
Proactive in identifying issues and proposing concrete solutions


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE, pension plan
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The recruitment process:


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Location: Lisbon or Porto

Contract: Permanent contract

Remote work: Hybjrid (50%)

Range: €39,000–€52,000 (depending on experience) gross annual salary

Job category: Data / Cybersecurity / IT Risk

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.

Title: Data Science Analyst GenAI
Description:
Who We Are

The next step of your career starts here, where you can bring your own unique mix of skills and perspectives to a fast-growing team.

Metyis is a global and forward-thinking firm operating across a wide range of industries, developing and delivering AI & Data, Digital Commerce, Marketing & Design solutions and Advisory services. At Metyis, our long-term partnership model brings long-lasting impact and growth to our business partners and clients through extensive execution capabilities.

With our team, you can experience a collaborative environment with highly skilled multidisciplinary experts, where everyone has room to build bigger and bolder ideas. Being part of Metyis means you can speak your mind and be creative with your knowledge. Imagine the things you can achieve with a team that encourages you to be the best version of yourself.




We are Metyis. Partners for Impact.




What We Offer




A balanced work environment where you will have autonomy, responsibility, and ownership while working with a vibrant and dynamic team.
A modern office with everything you need (including food and beverages).
Become part of a fast-growing international and diverse team.




What You Will Do




Assist in the development and testing of generative AI models under the guidance of senior team members.
Support cross-functional teams by gathering requirements and preparing data for AI applications.
Stay up to date with emerging trends in generative AI and contribute to internal knowledge sharing.
Help prepare presentations and documentation to communicate technical findings to internal stakeholders.




What You’ll Bring




At least 1-year of working experience in AI
Master’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.
Some hands-on experience with generative AI models (e.g., through internships or previous working experience).
Solid understanding of machine learning fundamentals and AI concepts.
Proficiency in Python and familiarity with AI libraries.
Basic understanding of cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Clear communication skills and a willingness to learn from others.




Good to have:

Internship or project experience in a consulting or business environment.
Contributions to open-source or academic publications.




At Metyis, we are driven by curiosity and collaboration. We value diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEIB) in all its forms as it makes us stronger as an organisation and promotes creativity and innovation. We welcome all talents and are committed to creating a workplace where every employee can make a meaningful impact and grow.

Title: Distributed Cloud | Data Analyst
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description

We are looking for a sharp and insightful Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence (BI) team. You will be the crucial link between raw data and strategic decisions, responsible for gathering business requirements, transforming complex data, and creating compelling visualizations that drive action across the organization.

Key Responsibilities


Gather and translate business needs into technical requirements for data models and reporting solutions.
Develop, optimize, and execute SQL queries to extract, transform, and load data from various sources, ensuring data accuracy and integrity.
Design and build dynamic dashboards and reports, primarily using Power BI, to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide actionable business insights.
Perform root-cause analysis on data discrepancies and collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure data quality and availability.
Present findings and strategic recommendations to stakeholders, clearly communicating complex analytical concepts to non-technical audiences.
Participate in the full lifecycle of BI projects, from requirements gathering to final deployment.



Qualifications


3+ years of proven experience as a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or in a similar role focused on data and reporting.
Strong mandatory proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience with Python for data analysis, data manipulation, automation, and scripting (e.g., using Pandas, NumPy, or similar libraries).
Expert hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI (or similar BI tools) for data modeling, visualization, and publishing reports.
Proven ability to gather, document, and manage business requirements effectively.
Excellent communication, presentation, and storytelling skills with data.
Experience with Microsoft Excel for data analysis and modeling is expected.



Valued Skills:


Experience with other components of the Microsoft Data Stack (e.g., Azure Synapse, Azure Analysis Services).
Knowledge of Data Modeling principles (e.g., Dimensional Modeling).
Professional fluency (B2+) in French is highly valued and may be a mandatory requirement for allocation on specific projects with Francophone clients.



Additional Information

The Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

You will be the crucial link between raw data and strategic decisions, responsible for gathering business requirements, transforming complex data, and creating compelling visualizations that drive action across the organization.

Key Responsibilities

  • Gather and translate business needs into technical requirements for data models and reporting solutions.
  • Develop, optimize, and execute SQL queries to extract, transform, and load data from various sources, ensuring data accuracy and integrity.
  • Design and build dynamic dashboards and reports, primarily using Power BI, to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide actionable business insights.
  • Perform root-cause analysis on data discrepancies and collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure data quality and availability.
  • Present findings and strategic recommendations to stakeholders, clearly communicating complex analytical concepts to non-technical audiences.
  • Participate in the full lifecycle of BI projects, from requirements gathering to final deployment.


Qualifications

  • 3+ years of proven experience as a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or in a similar role focused on data and reporting.
  • Strong mandatory proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating large datasets.
  • Hands-on experience with Python for data analysis, data manipulation, automation, and scripting (e.g., using Pandas, NumPy, or similar libraries).
  • Expert hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI (or similar BI tools) for data modeling, visualization, and publishing reports.
  • Proven ability to gather, document, and manage business requirements effectively.
  • Excellent communication, presentation, and storytelling skills with data.
  • Experience with Microsoft Excel for data analysis and modeling is expected.


Valued Skills:

  • Experience with other components of the Microsoft Data Stack (e.g., Azure Synapse, Azure Analysis Services).
  • Knowledge of Data Modeling principles (e.g., Dimensional Modeling).
  • Professional fluency (B2+) in French is highly valued and may be a mandatory requirement for allocation on specific projects with Francophone clients.


Additional Information

The Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

Title: Junior DLP (Data Loss Prevention) Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) Analyst to join an international cybersecurity team focused on protecting sensitive information and strengthening data security controls across a large and complex global environment.

This role is ideal for professionals with experience in Cybersecurity Operations, Data Protection, Security Monitoring, or Information Security Analysis who are passionate about data protection and security risk management.

You will play a key role in monitoring, investigating, and improving Data Loss Prevention controls, while working closely with security teams and stakeholders across multiple international locations.




Key Responsibilities

Assess and analyze alerts generated by Data Loss Prevention (DLP) tools according to defined procedures.
Perform Level 1 triage and initial investigation of security events.
Escalate complex cases to advanced analysis when required.
Conduct Level 2 investigations to confirm and assess potential data leakage incidents.
Review and analyze security events to identify weaknesses and improvement opportunities within DLP monitoring processes.
Collaborate with managers and stakeholders regarding DLP detections, unusual activity, and rule optimization.
Recommend best practices to improve detection quality and reduce false positives.
Contribute to the testing, validation, and implementation of new DLP policies.
Coordinate policy reviews and ensure alignment with approved security practices.
Monitor entities' compliance with operational processes and service expectations.
Track and report on backlog management and remediation activities.
Produce and maintain monthly DLP dashboards and reporting metrics.
Support the onboarding and deployment of new business entities and scopes into the DLP platform.




Required Profile

Experience in Cybersecurity Operations, Information Security, SOC, DLP, or a related area.
Strong analytical and investigative mindset.
Ability to assess security events and determine appropriate actions.
Experience working with security monitoring platforms and incident analysis processes.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, international environment.
Proactive approach to identifying improvements and strengthening security controls.




Language Requirements

English: Expert level (mandatory)
French: Professional working proficiency / Mastery (mandatory)




Hybrid (2–3 days per week in the office)

Description:
Who We Are




The next step of your career starts here, where you can bring your own unique mix of skills and perspectives to a fast-growing team.

Metyis is a global and forward-thinking firm operating across a wide range of industries, developing and delivering AI & Data, Digital Commerce, Marketing & Design solutions and Advisory services.
