Title: Data Engineer (AWS)
Description:
We're looking for Data Engineers to join our team in Lisbon, hiring multiple experience positions, from graduate to senior.

You'll design and operate production-grade data pipelines on AWS, working with vehicle data at massive scale (datasets in the order of multiple terabytes) that power analytics and decision-making across the business.

This role involves working with vehicle signals and telemetry data into structured, analytics-ready datasets — a unique technical challenge that combines distributed processing, domain knowledge and engineering rigor.

You'll own architecture decisions, collaborate with international stakeholders (including German teams), and contribute to a mature data platform

You’ll be focused in


Designing, building and maintaining data pipeline architecture on AWS for ingestion, transformation and curation
Processing and decoding large-scale vehicle datasets (multi-terabyte) — raw signals and telemetry transformed into ready-to-use analytics data
Assembling large, complex datasets meeting business and analytics requirements
Implementing ETL/ELT infrastructure using Python, PySpark, SQL and AWS big data services
Building analytics tooling on top of these pipelines to deliver actionable business insights
Working at scale - getting data into a ready-to-use state in close alignment with business stakeholders


In order to succeed, you’ll need


+5 years of hands-on experience as a Data Engineer or Software Engineer working on big-data environments
Strong Python — production-grade, not scripting
PySpark / Spark — real experience with distributed processing (academic exposure acceptable for junior profiles; production experience expected for senior)
AWS core stack: S3, Glue, Athena, Lambda, IAM — hands-on experience
SQL — comfortable with complex queries, optimization and data modeling
ETL pipelines — proven experience building and operating them
Knowledge of Data Lake, Data Warehouse and RDS architectures
Fluent English, written and spoken (you'll work daily with international teams)


Nice to have


Experience in the automotive sector or working with vehicle/telemetry data
IaC: AWS CDK, Terraform, or equivalent
CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)
SQS, SNS, EventBridge, Step Functions
Experience with Apache Iceberg or Hive — or strong willingness to learn Iceberg
Experience working with large-scale datasets (TB-scale


Company Benefits


1200€ to equip your home office
Flex allowance: 720€/year (60€ per month x12)
Performance bonus
Health insurance extendable to your household
Wellbeing program extendable to your household
Life insurance
Pension fund
Smartphone & phone data plan
Discounts on VW Group cars and special financing conditions


Cool Things You’ll Experience In Our Work Environment


Tailored remote-friendly setup for every team's vibe!
Flexible working hours
Multicultural reality with + 30 different nationalities
Pet friendly environment
Boost your career with our internal mobility program, focused on your personal & professional growth
Mix and mingle across teams for idea-sharing and bonding


About VW Group Digital Solutions [Portugal]

Technology-driven trends such as diverse mobility, electrification and connectivity will revolutionize the mobility industry in the next decade. Volkswagen Group, as world leader in the automotive industry, is setting the pace for this revolution.

At Volkswagen Group Digital Solutions [Portugal] we are the go-to team to tackle complex systems and programming challenges. We make sure that our products flow smoothly in all stages of the digital value chain.

Our main areas of focus: vehicle software & operations, mobile online services, IT security, fleet management, sales & after sales, purchasing & logistics and product portfolio management.

Ready to #ExploreNewRoads? Join us! 🚗

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Business Intelligence /Data Analytics Engineer Trainee (IEFP)
Description:
Join us as a Business Intelligence /Data Analytics Engineer Trainee!

As a Business Intelligence /Data analytics Engineer Trainee you will design, build, and maintain scalable analytics solutions that transform raw data into actionable insights. You will work closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and analysts to deliver reliable data models, dashboards, and reporting solutions that support strategic and operational decision-making.

Key Accountabilities

You will primarily be responsible for:


Assist in collecting, cleaning, transforming, and validating data from multiple sources.
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, anomalies, and business insights.
Develop and evaluate machine learning models for supervised and unsupervised learning tasks.
Prepare datasets for statistical analysis and predictive modelling.
Stay current with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in data engineering, analytics, and data science.
Designing and maintaining semantic data models (star/snowflake schemas) optimized for analytics and reporting.
Building and optimizing ETL/ELT pipelines from multiple data sources, ensuring data quality and consistency.
Ensure data quality, consistency and performance across business intelligence solutions.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and reports using BI tools such as Power BI, Python and R.
Translating business requirements into robust BI solutions and flexible Power BI semantic models.
Optimizing SQL queries, data models and report performance for large-scale datasets, monitoring BI workloads in cloud and on-premises environments.
Collaborating with business users to understand KPIs, metrics and reporting needs, and partnering with Data Engineers, Data Scientists and Product teams.
Providing guidance and best practices to analysts and report developers.
Defining and enforcing data standards, naming conventions and documentation, supporting data lineage and metadata management.
Ensuring compliance with security and privacy requirements.


Knowledge, Skills And Experience Required


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Engineering or related fields.
Eligible for an IEFP Level VI Internship.
Good programming skills; knowledge of python, C#.
Machine learning model development and evaluation.
Data wrangling using libraries such as Pandas/Modin, Polars, NumPy.
Knowledge of multiple databases such as SQLServer, PostgreSQL, DuckDB.
API-based data ingestion.
Strong SQL skills and experience with data warehouses (e.g. Snowflake, Azure Synapse, Big Query, Redshift, SQL Server).
Hands-on with Power BI (semantic models, DAX, DirectQuery) and ETL/ELT tools (SSIS, ADF, Airflow, dbt, SSIS or similar).
Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric and Azure Foundry (Nice to have).
Knowledge of data modelling techniques (Kimball, dimensional modelling).
Analytical mindset with the ability to translate business needs into data models and KPIs.
Excellent communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Organized, able to manage multiple priorities and share knowledge effectively.
Fluent in English, with good verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills.
Nice to have: experience with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS), Python, DevOps/CI-CD for BI, data governance frameworks.


Apply by submitting your CV in English.

Join Euronext – Shape capital markets for future generations.

Euronext is the leading European capital market covering the entire capital markets value chain, from listing, trading, clearing, settlement and custody to solutions for issuers and investors. We operate seven national stock exchanges, handling 25% of European lit equity trading. Our products include equities, FX, ETFs, bonds, derivatives, commodities and indices. Euronext also provides clearing and settlement services through Euronext Clearing and our network of Euronext Securities CSDs. In addition, Euronext runs MTS, one of Europe’s leading electronic fixed income trading markets, and Nord Pool, the European power market. The company has a diverse domestic and international client base.

Euronext Values

Unity


We respect and value the people we work with
We are unified through a common purpose
We embrace diversity and strive for inclusion


Integrity


We value transparency, communicate honestly and share information openly
We act with integrity in everything we do
We don’t hide our mistakes, and we learn from them


Agility


We act with a sense of urgency and decisiveness
We are adaptable, responsive and embrace change
We take smart risks


Energy


We are positively driven to make a difference and challenge the status quo
We focus on and encourage personal leadership
We motivate each other with our ambition


Accountability


We deliver maximum value to our customers and stakeholders
We take ownership and are accountable for the outcome
We reward and celebrate performance


We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, gender, age, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, or any other legally protected factor. We value the unique talents of all our people, who come from diverse backgrounds with different personal experiences and points of view and we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect.

Queremos fazer diferente com o essencial.




Apostamos numa estratégia de corporativismo sustentável. Work-life balance, beginner’s mind e community involvement são a nossa identidade, os nossos fundamentos e a nossa cultura.

Desenvolvimento Python, com foco em engenharia/processamento de dados
SQL para data querying e validação de dados
Sólida experiência na construção de pipelines ETL/ELT

Local: Porto







team.it, back to basics!

Juntas-te a nós?










Na Team IT defendemos a igualdade e valorizamos a diversidade.

Title: Data Engineer (Hybrid @Porto)
Description:
We’re looking for the special, unique and amazing YOU!

@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, team work.

At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability or any factor other than merit. All applications with skills for the position are welcome!




We are looking for an Amazing: Data Engineer

Seniority: 6+

Type of position: Hybrid (2x/week at Porto Office)




The amazing you, will have:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field;
6+ years of experience as a Data Engineer;
Expertise on end-to-end Data Pipeline Development, ETL processes and Alteryx;
Knowledge in Data Mesh concepts (Starburst, Trino);
Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Java, or Scala;
Strong knowledge of SQL and NoSQL databases;
Experience with Big Data technologies such as Databricks, Hadoop, Apache Spark, Kafka;
Availability for a hybrid regime at Porto (Portugal);
Fluency in English is mandatory.




To turn yourself from amazing to unique, you´ll have (plus):

French AND/OR Portuguese fluency.







Why should you become part of our family?

You can develop a career that fits you: your career development is personalized, taking in consideration your needs and goals from a short to long term
Interesting Challenges Ahead: you can work for several clients from different sectors of activity
Free training programs: Our training and certification programs in languages, tech, behavior and business will help you to reach your full potential faster
International projects in Benelux: you can gain international experience in Benelux and balance a new way of living with work
Type of projects (depending on the project you might find one of this types of projects):
In hybrid Systems: Is important to balance work with socialization, that´s why a hybrid system works for us and for you
Full Remote projects: If you want to work while you enjoy the comfort of your home
Full Onsite projects: if you prefere the company of your colleagues!
Take care of your well-being: Enjoy our free nutrition, psicologist, general medicine appointments and our yoga and personal training days… all remote!




Who are we?




Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Inovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.

Title: Reporting and Business Intelligence Senior Specialist (f/m/div)
Description:
#WeAreIn for jobs that impact everyone’s life Behind every success, there is a plan: your strategic thinking, creative skills, and ability to communicate with others will drive innovation and turn ideas into impact. As a Reporting and Business Intelligence Senior Specialist on our Corporate team, you’ll play a vital part in building the frameworks, relationships, and processes that keep Infineon’s Business thriving. Are you in?

Your Role

Key responsibilities in your new role

As a Reporting and BI Senior Specialist, you will provide user support, training, and analysis for IFX reporting solutions, manage change requests, implement new reporting tools, and analyze data to drive business insights and improvements.


Act as a Solution Owner for one or more global reporting solutions, with a focus on providing dedicated support to a specific region, ensuring seamless solution delivery, and driving business value through data-driven insights
Be responsible for the Change Request management including documentation of the corresponding requirements
Design and create standard reports/dashboards for the respective functions and functional areas and develop the reporting solutions with own ideas
Collaborate with functional users to analyze and define reporting requirements
Analyze data and business processes in the respective functional area to identify opportunities for improvement and optimization
Create training concepts and implement of training for the respective reporting solutions
Develop the reporting landscape through the application of Smart Data methods (e.g. Big Data, Artificial Intelligence, Industrial Analytics) with the aim of enabling rapid learning, decision-making and increased productivity


Your Profile

Qualifications And Skills To Help You Succeed

Results-driven professional with strong communication, analytical skills, and a customer-focused mindset. Excels in fast-paced environments, simplifies complex concepts, and drives business success through data insights and independent ownership.


A degree in Business Administration, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related field
At least 3 years of experience in a data science role, ideally from a business point of view
Experience in working with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Denodo, or similar alternative tools
Proven experience in delivering training sessions to diverse audiences
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Fluency in English is mandatory


Please send us your CV in English.

Contact:

Luis Oliveira, LinkedIn

#WeAreIn for driving decarbonization and digitalization.

As a global leader in semiconductor solutions in power systems and IoT, Infineon enables game-changing solutions for green and efficient energy, clean and safe mobility, as well as smart and secure IoT. Together, we drive innovation and customer success, while caring for our people and empowering them to reach ambitious goals. Be a part of making life easier, safer and greener.

Are you in?

We are on a journey to create the best Infineon for everyone.

This means we embrace diversity and inclusion and welcome everyone for who they are. At Infineon, we offer a working environment characterized by trust, openness, respect and tolerance and are committed to give all applicants and employees equal opportunities. We base our recruiting decisions on the applicant´s experience and skills. Learn more about our various contact channels.

We look forward to receiving your resume, even if you do not entirely meet all the requirements of the job posting.

Please let your recruiter know if they need to pay special attention to something in order to enable your participation in the interview process.

Click here for more information about Diversity & Inclusion at Infineon.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Farfetch is a leading global marketplace for the luxury fashion industry. The Farfetch Marketplace connects customers in over 190 countries and territories with items from more than 50 countries and over 1,400 of the world’s best brands, boutiques, and department stores, delivering a truly unique shopping experience and access to the most extensive selection of luxury on a global marketplace.

TECHNOLOGY
We're on a mission to build end-to-end products and technology that powers the an incredible e-commerce experience for luxury customers everywhere, understanding the motivations and needs of our customers and partners, to designing and testing hypotheses, to creating industry-leading experiences for luxury customers.

PORTO
Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

THE ROLE
We're a Data team that implements methods to improve data reliability and quality by combining raw information from different sources and developing architectures that enable data extraction and transformation for data modeling. Our aim is to provide an unrivaled customer experience and promote FARFETCH's growth. You will be integrated with the Data Engineering team, being responsible for helping maintain and improve the Data models and tools.

What You'll Do


Design and build scalable & reliable data pipelines (ETLs) for our data platform
Constantly evolve data models & schema design of our Data Warehouse to support self-service needs
Work cross functionally with various teams, creating solutions that deal with large volumes of data
Work with the team to set and maintain standards and development practices
Be a keen advocate of quality and continuous improvement





Who You Are


You have experience as a professional with solid technical background building and maintaining data pipelines in a custom or commercial ETL tool (eg. SSIS, Talend, Airflow, Composer)
You have experience working in a Data Warehouse environment with varied forms of data infrastructure, including relational databases, Hadoop, and Column Store, BigQuery
Expert in creating and evolving dimensional data models & schema designs to improve accessibility of data and provide intuitive analytics
Basic experience and knowledge of cloud environments (eg. GCP, Azure)
Expert in SQL
You are proficient in one of the following programming languages: Java, Python
You have 2+ years experience working with a BI reporting tool (eg. Tableau, PowerBI, Looker)
Proficient applier of continuous delivery principles: version control, unit and automated tests
You are fluent in English, both written and spoken
You have good analytical and problem solving skills, the ability to work in a fast moving operational environment and you are enthusiastic and with a positive attitude





Rewards & Benefits


Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools
Extra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the community
Training opportunities
Flexible benefits program





EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT


Farfetch is an equal opportunities employer ensuring that all applicants are treated equally and fairly throughout our recruitment process. We are determined that no applicant experiences discrimination on the basis of sex, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, disability, age, gender identity, ancestry, sexual orientation, veteran status, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.





SCAM DISCLAIMER


It has come to our attention that there may be fraudulent activities involving individuals or organizations falsely claiming to represent Farfetch in order to attract candidates to a SCAM. Please be aware that Farfetch does not conduct recruitment processes through messaging apps or any unofficial communication channels, other than our official careers website. Additionally, Farfetch will never ask candidates for any form of payment during the recruitment process





We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Distributed Cloud | Power BI Specialist
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description

We are seeking a sharp and insightful Power BI Specialist to drive our Business Intelligence initiatives. You will be instrumental in translating complex business requirements into clear data models and compelling visualizations, ensuring strategic decision-making across the organization.

Key Responsibilities:


Design and develop dynamic dashboards and reports using Power BI to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide actionable business insights.
Gather and translate business requirements into technical specifications for data modeling and reporting solutions.
Develop, optimize, and execute SQL queries to extract, transform, and prepare data for analysis.
Perform data validation and collaborate with stakeholders to ensure data integrity and quality.
Drive the adoption of self-service BI best practices across the business.



Qualifications


3+ years of proven experience as a Data Analyst or BI Developer.
Strong mandatory proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating data.
Expert hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI for data modeling (DAX) and visualization.
Proven ability to successfully elicit and document business requirements.
Language Requirement: Professional fluency (B2+) in French is highly valued and may be a mandatory requirement for allocation on specific projects with Francophone clients.



Additional Information

The Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

Title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
Description:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:


Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
Label or classify content based on project guidelines
Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material


Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $17 per hour equivalent.

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in any discipline
Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise

Title: Administrativo de controlo financeiro
Place: Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto, Portugal
HTML:
A Synchro by EGOR, na área de Outsourcing, recruta Administrativo de Controlo Financeiro (m/f) para empresa cliente que se dedica ao setor das viagens, em Vila Nova de Gaia.

FUNÇÃO

  • Verificar os registos contabilísticos efetuados pelos Hotel Managers;
  • Controlar a correta introdução de lançamentos contabilísticos e respetiva documentação de suporte;
  • Validar e introduzir dados de IVA, em articulação com a equipa de Contabilidade Fiscal;
  • Verificar e completar informação contabilística adicional;
  • Analisar a razoabilidade das contagens de consumíveis e bens duradouros;
  • Identificar discrepâncias nas listas de inventário e prestar orientações aos Hotel Managers para a sua correção;
  • Prestar suporte aos Hotel Managers nas áreas da sua responsabilidade, através de MS Teams, e-mail e telefone.

LOCAL E HORÁRIO

  • De 2.ªf a 6.ªf das 9h às 18h;
  • Vila Nova de Gaia.

REQUISITOS

  • 12.º ano de escolaridade (conhecimentos de Contabilidade serão valorizados, mas não são obrigatórios);
  • Experiência mínima de 2 anos em funções administrativas;
  • Nível de Inglês C1;
  • Bons conhecimentos de Excel e das restantes ferramentas Microsoft Office;

  • Condições salariais VB 1000 + 8.5 SA.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Sobre a equipa

Como Data Analyst farás parte da nossa equipa de Data Intelligence, onde oferecemos soluções inovadoras na área da saúde para melhorar a eficiência e a qualidade do percurso do paciente, antecipando todas as suas necessidades.

Sobre a role


Criar e manter dashboards e relatórios em Power BI para diferentes perfis de utilizador — desde gestores operacionais a equipas clínicas
Colaborar com stakeholders das unidades de saúde para levantar requisitos, validar métricas e garantir que os outputs respondem às necessidades reais
Desenvolver e manter pipelines de dados (ETL/SSIS) para integração de fontes heterogéneas presentes em unidades de saúde
Construir e otimizar queries T-SQL para extração, transformação e análise de dados clínicos e operacionais
Garantir a qualidade, consistência e documentação dos dados ao longo do ciclo de vida dos produtos
Participar ativamente na implementação dos produtos de dados nos clientes, incluindo formação e suporte


Que skills procuramos?


2 a 4 anos de experiência em funções de análise de dados ou Business Intelligence
Conhecimento e experiência em SQL / T-SQL
Experiência prática com SSIS ou processos de ETL equivalentes
Experiência prática com SSAS, Modelação e Calculations MDX
Capacidade para desenvolver relatórios e dashboards em Power BI (modelação de dados, DAX, boas práticas de visualização)
Familiaridade com o setor da saúde — sistemas de informação clínica, terminologias ou processos hospitalares são valorizados
Perfil analítico, orientado ao detalhe e com boa capacidade de comunicação com perfis não técnicos


Valorizamos ainda:


Experiência na área da saúde
Autonomia na gestão de tarefas e proatividade


Quem somos?

Somos a Glintt Global, uma empresa de referência na Península Ibérica em Consultoria e Serviços Tecnológicos, com mais de 30 anos de experiência. Pretendemos impactar a qualidade de vida das pessoas, através da inovação, tecnologia, do conhecimento e da ambição que nos acompanham. Este é o nosso compromisso!

Encontra aqui mais sobre a Cultura global | Glintt Global!

Recruiting Privacy Policy

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.




As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Data Engineer!




Responsibilities:

Perform analysis of internal and external data to identify business growth opportunities and support client strategy initiatives.
Ensure accurate tracking and delivery of KPIs aligned with client development goals and business roadmap.
Support global teams (London, New York, Hong Kong) by providing high-quality data insights, tools, and analysis to improve sales effectiveness and decision-making.
Produce high-value reports, dashboards, and documentation for regional and global client reviews.
Leverage multiple data sources to generate actionable insights on business performance, client profitability, and wallet share growth.
Promote and contribute to the automation of reporting and client intelligence deliverables in collaboration with business and IT teams.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across multiple data pipelines, dashboards, and reporting systems.
Support the development and improvement of client data processes, analytics frameworks, and reporting standards.




Requirements:

Technical Requirements

Strong experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or data engineering-related roles.
Solid knowledge of:
Data visualization and BI tools such as:
Tableau
Power BI
Strong programming skills in Python, particularly using Pandas for data analysis.
Strong knowledge of SQL and experience working with ETL processes.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing.
Experience working with large, complex datasets and multi-source data environments.
Experience in report automation and data pipeline optimization is highly valued.

Soft Skills

Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to translate data into clear business insights.
Adaptability and ability to work in fast-paced, global environments.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality and accuracy.
Results-driven mindset with strong delivery focus.
Problem-solving skills with the ability to design and improve analytical processes.
Proactive attitude and ability to work effectively within a team.
Strong collaboration skills across business and technical stakeholders.

Language Requirements

English: Mastery level (mandatory).




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us:

Long-term projects with national and international context (if applicable)
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.




If you’re interested in this job please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com, with the reference “CA/DataAnalyst”.




Thank you! :)




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: SPARK Trainee- Commercial
Description:
Job Purpose

The SPARK Programme is a dynamic, paid traineeship designed to give candidates hands-on experience in the fast-paced biotech industry. Over 2 to 12 months, you’ll gain real exposure to MoonLake’s mission, work alongside experienced professionals, and grow in an international, purpose-driven environment. To learn more about the SPARK experience and explore our programme in detail, click HERE.

Your profile

We are currently seeking SPARK trainees in the following areas:


Commercial/Consulting


Key Accountabilities


Contribute to real, high-impact projects across scientific, operational, or strategic teams
Participate in onboarding and skills workshops tailored to your development
Collaborate cross-functionally with teams in your hub and beyond
Receive guidance from a dedicated mentor, including mid-program and final performance reviews
Actively participate in team life, including a potential visit to another hub


Your profile


Accepted at an institution for higher education, currently enrolled in or recently completed a higher education degree
Curious, motivated, and eager to grow in a biotech setting
B2 level of English or higher (written and spoken)
Strong communication and collaboration skills
Able to work in person at one of our offices at least 3 days/week


Work Location


3 days/week in our Porto (Portugal) office.


Why us?

An Exciting Opportunity Awaits you!

MoonLake is a dynamic and innovative company pushing the boundaries of possibility, and we are looking for passionate individuals to join our stellar team.

What We Offer


Learning Environment: An opportunity to learn and grow alongside experienced professionals in a supportive and innovative biotech environment.
Impactful work: Contribute to ground-breaking projects that have the potential to transform global healthcare.
Flexibility and Balance: Work remotely/hybrid, enjoying a healthy work-life balance while making meaningful contributions to our team.


Our Core Values

We look for potential MoonLakers that embody our three core values that drive our pursuit of excellence.


We do Stellar Science: We aspire to create novel therapies that elevate treatment horizons, with innovative science, and high quality, always.
We go beyond: We aspire to be positively disruptive in our field, and bold in the way we challenge the norms and each other.
We unlock value: We aspire to create long-term value for investors and communities.


About Us

MoonLake Immunotherapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company unlocking the potential of sonelokimab, a novel investigational Nanobody® for the treatment of the inflammatory disease, to revolutionize outcomes for patients.

We continue to make significant progress in our Global Clinical Development Programs. The company’s focus is on inflammatory diseases with a major unmet need, including hidradenitis suppurativa and psoriatic arthritis – conditions affecting millions of people worldwide with a large need for improved treatment options. MoonLake was founded in 2021 and is headquarted in Zug, with hubs in Cambridge & Porto.

Further information is available at www.moonlaketx.com

Title: Data Analytics & Reporting Internship
Description:
We Are:

Drive technology innovations that shape the way we live and connect. Our technology drives the Era of Pervasive Intelligence, where smart tech and AI are seamlessly woven into daily life. From self-driving cars and health-monitoring smartwatches to renewable energy systems that efficiently distribute clean power, Synopsys creates high-performance silicon chips that help build a healthier, safer, and more sustainable world.

Internship Experience:

At Synopsys, interns dive into real-world projects, gaining hands-on experience while collaborating with our passionate teams worldwide—and having fun in the process! You'll have the freedom to share your ideas, unleash your creativity, and explore your interests. This is your opportunity to bring your solutions to life and work with cutting-edge technology that shapes not only the future of innovation but also your own career path. Join us and start shaping your future today!

Mission Statement:

Our mission is to fuel today's innovations and spark tomorrow's creativity. Together, we embrace a growth mindset, empower one another, and collaborate to achieve our shared goals. Every day, we live by our values of Integrity, Excellence, Leadership, and Passion, fostering an inclusive culture where everyone can thrive—both at work and beyond.

What You'll Be Doing:


Help migrate reports to shared data sources, working alongside experienced team members who will guide you through the process
Improve and organize documentation for reports and data, making information easier to find and understand for everyone
Create reports and analyze data that will be used by senior management, giving you real visibility and impact in the organization
Work with different teams to understand what data they need and help turn their questions into clear insights
Bring your ideas to the table and suggest ways to make our reporting processes better and more efficient


What You'll Need:


Currently enrolled in a Master's degree program in Engineering, Mathematics, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field
Basic knowledge of SQL - you should be comfortable writing queries and working with databases
Good analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
Experience with Power BI is a plus, but don't worry if you're still learning - we value your willingness to learn!
Team player attitude with the ability to take initiative and work independently when needed
Responsible and reliable, understanding that your work will be seen by senior leaders
Good communication skills and enthusiasm to learn from experienced professionals


Key Program Facts:


Program Length: 6 months duration
Location: Moreia da Maia, Porto. Portugal
Working Model: Onsite
Full-Time/Part-Time: Flexible - we offer both full-time and part-time options to fit your study schedule
Start Date: September 2026


Equal Opportunity Statement:

Synopsys is committed to creating an inclusive workplace and is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome all qualified applicants to apply, regardless of age, color, family or medical leave, gender identity or expression, marital status, disability, race and ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation during the application process, please reach out to us.

Title: Analista de Crédito (m/f/d)
Place: Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto, Portugal
Description:
A Toyota Financial Services Portugal (TFS) é a solução financeira oficial da marca Toyota, dedicada a proporcionar as melhores opções de financiamento para os nossos clientes. A nossa missão é garantir uma experiência de mobilidade sem complicações, com soluções adaptadas às necessidades dos nossos clientes, tanto particulares como empresas.

A TFS está a aumentar a sua equipa e procura um(a) Analista de Crédito para integrar a equipa de Assessment da Direção de Operações. Esta equipa tem como principal missão o tratamento e decisão das operações de crédito e procuramos um(a) profissional experiente, com sólida capacidade analítica e autonomia na decisão, capaz de lidar com operações mais complexas e de dar suporte técnico a colegas menos experientes, contribuindo para o sucesso do nosso negócio e dos nossos clientes.

Como Analista de Crédito esta pessoa terá a oportunidade de desempenhar atividades desafiantes e diversificadas, entre as quais:

- Validar e assegurar o rigor de todos os documentos associados às propostas de crédito, identificando riscos e propondo medidas de mitigação
- Garantir a fiabilidade e precisão das informações registadas nas propostas de crédito
- Realizar a análise e tomada de decisão de crédito para operações de maior complexidade e valor
- Dar apoio técnico e servir de referência a colegas menos experientes da equipa, contribuindo para a sua capacitação
- Contribuir para a melhoria contínua dos processos e critérios de decisão de crédito, em articulação com as áreas de Risco e Compliance

O que procuramos:

- Experiência mínima de 4/5 anos em funções semelhantes, preferencialmente em análise e
decisão de crédito em Instituições de Crédito ao Consumo ou banca
- Licenciatura em Gestão, Economia, Finanças, Contabilidade, Direito ou áreas afins
- Uma pessoa dinâmica, organizada, com elevada capacidade de análise e decisão, autonomia para gerir casos complexos e ambíguos, capaz de lidar com picos de trabalho e com forte orientação para soluções
- Visão crítica sobre risco e processo e com capacidade para propor melhorias

O que oferecemos:

- A integração numa equipa dinâmica e em crescimento
- A possibilidade de trabalhar numa empresa multinacional de referência no setor automóvel e financeiro
- Condições atrativas, alinhadas com a experiência e competências demonstradas

Se és uma pessoa rigorosa, com experiência consolidada em análise de crédito e tens vontade de assumir um papel de responsabilidade num projeto desafiante, envia-nos a tua candidatura e vem fazer a diferença na Toyota Financial Services Portugal.

Title: Data Analyst - Finance (m/f/d)
HTML:
Chemical Industry | Data Analyst – Finance


Your new Company


Our client is a Global Recognized Organization operating at the forefront of advanced materials and specialty solutions. With a strong international footprint and a workforce of around 1 000 professionals.


Driven by deep scientific expertise and a strong commitment to innovation, the Organization leverages advanced capabilities in material sciences, formulation technologies, and surface modification to deliver tailored solutions.


With a clear focus on innovation, operational excellence, and professional development, this is an opportunity to join a Company that combines global scale with a dynamic and forward-thinking mindset.


Your new Role


The Data Analyst – Finance transforms complex Datasets into clear, actionable insights that support decision making across the organization. Working within a matrix structure and closely aligned with Finance, this person partners with business stakeholders to understand priorities, interpret financial and operational Data, and deliver high-quality analysis, reporting, dashboards, and presentations.


This person reports to the Business Data Manager, and will collaborate closely with the Finance, Business Leaders, Technical Specialists, and Data Governance teams to support planning cycles, improve reporting capabilities, and strengthen Data-driven decision making across the business.


The Data Analyst – Finance will:

  • Partner with Finance stakeholders to understand business priorities and deliver Data-driven insights that support operational and strategic decision-making;
  • Produce accurate and timely management reporting, analyzing financial performance, identifying variances, trends, risks, and opportunities against plans and forecasts;
  • Support planning, budgeting, forecasting, CAPEX submissions, and actuals-to-plan reconciliations, ensuring Data accuracy and consistency across business systems;
  • Manage provisions, accruals, and financial Data, ensuring information integrity, compliance with Data governance standards, and the reliability of ERP and reporting system outputs;
  • Act as a subject matter expert in key Finance areas (including cost accounting, CAPEX, pricing and margins, fixed costs, working capital, and operational finance);
  • Develop, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards, reports, and visualizations to improve business visibility, performance monitoring, and decision-making;
  • Promote self-service reporting capabilities through training, user support, and best-practice guidance on Power BI solutions and planning tools;
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to strengthen analytical capabilities, optimize reporting processes, and improve Data foundations across the organization;
  • Support business projects, process improvements, and automation initiatives that drive efficiency, performance, and continuous improvement.


A typical day will include analyzing financial performance, maintaining dashboards, supporting planning and forecasting activities, collaborating with Finance and business stakeholders, validating Data quality, and delivering insights that support key business decisions.


Please consider that this role requires availability for occasional travel to other company sites.


What you will need to succeed

As a Data Analyst – Finance, you will need:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field;
  • Professional Accounting Qualification (would be a plus);
  • +3 years of experience within International Manufacturing, Industrial, or Global Business environments.
  • +3 years of experience in Financial Analysis, Finance Support, or other analytical Finance-related roles.
  • Proven experience supporting Business Stakeholders through Financial Analysis, Performance Monitoring, and Data-Driven Decision-Making;
  • Experience developing and maintaining Power BI Dashboards, Reports, and Data Visualizations for Business Performance and Management Reporting;
  • Experience working with ERP systems, large business Datasets, and Data Governance practices to ensure Data Integrity and Reporting Accuracy;
  • Experience working within Matrix Organizations and collaborating with Cross-Functional Teams would be advantageous;
  • Experience in Budgeting, Forecasting, Financial Planning, Variance Analysis, Accruals, Reconciliations, CAPEX, Cost Accounting, Pricing & Margins, Fixed Costs, Working Capital, and Operational Finance;
  • Advanced Excel skills (including Pivot Tables, Complex Data Analysis, and Financial Modelling);
  • Knowledge of Python and/or SQL (would be a plus);
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality outputs in a fast-paced international environment;
  • Excellent communication skills – Fluency in English.


What the Company can offer you


Have the opportunity to join a cross-functional team in an international company with a multicultural working environment!


Next Steps


If you are interested in this opportunity, please send us your updated CV. If you are looking for another type of professional challenge, please contact us to discuss other career opportunities, always in complete confidence.


Data Analyst - Finance (m/f/d) Hays Working for your tomorrow

Title: Data Analyst - Operations (m/f/d)
HTML:
Chemical Industry | Data Analyst – Operations


Your new Company


Our client is a Global Recognized Organization operating at the forefront of advanced materials and specialty solutions. With a strong international footprint and a workforce of around 1 000 professionals.


Driven by deep scientific expertise and a strong commitment to innovation, the Organization leverages advanced capabilities in material sciences, formulation technologies, and surface modification to deliver tailored solutions.


With a clear focus on innovation, operational excellence, and professional development, this is an opportunity to join a Company that combines global scale with a dynamic and forward-thinking mindset.


Your new Role


The Data Analyst – Operations transforms complex Datasets into clear, actionable insights that support decision making across the organization. Working within a Matrix structure and closely aligned with Supply Chain, Procurement, and Manufacturing functions, this person partners with business stakeholders to understand priorities, interpret operational Data, and deliver high-quality analysis, reporting, dashboards, and presentations.


This person reports to the Business Data Manager, and will collaborate closely with Operations stakeholders, Business Leaders, Technical Specialists, and Data Governance teams to support operational performance management, improve reporting capabilities, and strengthen Data-driven decision making across the business.


The Data Analyst – Operations will:

  • Partner with Supply Chain, Procurement, Manufacturing, and Logistics stakeholders to understand business priorities and deliver Data-driven insights that support operational and strategic decision-making;
  • Produce and support operational reporting and performance analysis across Supply Chain, Procurement, Manufacturing, Logistics, Inventory, and Demand Planning functions, identifying trends, risks, opportunities, and deviations from plan;
  • Support functional performance cycles (including manufacturing reviews, procurement analysis, demand consensus, supply reviews, OTIF, backorders, SLA performance, inventory reporting, and other business-critical reporting activities);
  • Manage operational Master Data, ensuring accurate creation, maintenance, validation, archiving, and compliance with Data governance standards across suppliers, raw materials, inventory, and Supply Chain Data;
  • Act as a subject matter expert for operational reporting tools and solutions (including OTIF reporting and Power BI dashboards, reports, and visualizations);
  • Perform analysis on suppliers, raw materials, production, transport, inventory, logistics, and Supply Chain alternatives to support business optimization and decision-making;
  • Support SKU lifecycle management, portfolio rationalization, and product deprecation activities in collaboration with Supply Chain and Strategic Marketing teams;
  • Promote self-service reporting capabilities through training, user support, and best-practice guidance while driving improvements in Data quality, analytical capabilities, reporting processes, and business tools;
  • Support operational projects and continuous improvement initiatives focused on process standardization, automation, inventory optimization, cost reduction, and operational efficiency.


A typical day will include analyzing operational performance, maintaining dashboards, producing manufacturing, procurement, and Supply Chain reports, supporting inventory and demand planning activities, validating Data quality, supporting operational users, and delivering insights that support key business decisions.


Please consider that this role requires availability for occasional travel to other company sites, approximately once per year.


What you will need to succeed


As a Data Analyst – Operations, you will need:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Information Systems, Data Management, or another analytical or business-related field;
  • +3 years of experience within an International Manufacturing or Industrial environment;
  • +3 years of experience in analytical or support roles within Sales, Commercial, or related business functions;
  • Proven experience developing and maintaining Power BI dashboards, reports, and Data visualizations that deliver actionable business insights;
  • Experience working within Matrix Organizations and collaborating with Cross-Functional Teams (would be a plus);
  • Experience supporting Manufacturing reporting, Procurement analysis, Supply Chain performance monitoring, logistics reporting, inventory management, and demand planning;
  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills (including advanced Data collection, cleansing, validation, and reporting capabilities);
  • Advanced Excel skills (including Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP, advanced functions, and graphs);
  • Experience with project management tools;
  • Knowledge of Python, SQL, and SharePoint management (would be a plus);
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high-quality outputs in a fast-paced international environment;
  • Excellent communication skills – Fluency in English.


What the Company can offer you


Have the opportunity to join a cross-functional team in an international company with a multicultural working environment!


Next Steps


If you are interested in this opportunity, please send us your updated CV. If you are looking for another type of professional challenge, please contact us to discuss other career opportunities, always in complete confidence.

Title: Junior Application Support
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.

As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We would like to meet a Junior Application Support for a project with a hybrid working model:




Responsibilities:




Provide support for business-critical applications, ensuring high availability and performance
Monitor systems, identify issues, and perform troubleshooting and error resolution
Collaborate with development and infrastructure teams to resolve incidents
Execute and monitor batch processes and scheduling tools
Maintain documentation and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives




Requirements:




Minimum of 1 year of experience in a similar role
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
Proficiency in Linux and Windows environments
Basic knowledge of scheduling tools such as BMC/Control-M or other batch workload systems
Familiarity with scripting languages (Shell, Python, and/or PowerShell)
Experience with Oracle databases and solid understanding of SQL fundamentals
Hands-on experience with monitoring tools and debugging errors
Basic networking knowledge
Experience with Power BI
Familiarity with Jira and Confluence
DevOps mindset with a proactive approach to problem-solving




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us:




Long-term projects with national and international context
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability




Join us Send us your CV in English to career@decskill.com with reference “DA / JAS”.




Your next challenge might start here.




At Decskill, we are committed to equal opportunities and non-discrimination. We promote a culture of diversity and inclusion, where recruitment and career progression are based solely on talent, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality, or any other form of discrimination incompatible with human dignity.

Title: Assistente Armazém: Portfólio Aeroporto do Porto
Description:
FEITO DE PORTUGAL

Oportunidade de integração numa equipa jovem e dinâmica, que representa um conceito exclusivamente português (desde moda, artesanato, restauração) com elevada notoriedade no mercado onde se insere.

Pertencendo ao Grupo Areas, um dos líderes mundiais no setor da Restauração & Retail no mundo das viagens, conta atualmente com 24000 colaboradores, recebe a cada ano 350 milhões de clientes nos seus mais de 2200 estabelecimentos em 11 países, Europa, México, Chile e EUA.

Procuramos perfil de Assistente de Armazém para as suas lojas Portfolio no Aeroporto do Porto.

Principais Funções


Separar, arrumar e preparar mercadorias;
Reposição de loja;
Organização e exposição de moveis;
Inventário;
Outras tarefas pontuais.


Horários diurnos compreendidos entre as 7hrs e as 19hrs, com folgas fixas (sexta-feira e sábado)

Plano Salarial/Prémios e Benefícios


Salário Base Mensal 980€;
Subsídio de Alimentação em cartão refeição no valor de 150,00€
Prémio de Assiduidade no valor de 40,00€;
Acesso a plataforma de formação e-learning – MY AREAS
Seguro de Saúde após 9 meses de trabalho
Programas de saúde, e cuidados na incapacidade e diversidade
Cartão Colaborador – descontos nos nossos estabelecimentos em Portugal e Espanha;
Clube de Poupança


Requisitos mínimos


Experiência anterior na função
Facilidade para trabalhar em equipa
Capacidade de organização e gestão de tempo
Boa capacidade de comunicação
Sentido de responsabilidade
Bons conhecimentos em inglês (fator preferencial)


Notas Importantes

Para trabalhar no Aeroporto, o registo criminal não pode conter nenhum apontamento.

Deverá ter, documentos válidos, cartão cidadão ou título residência, segurança social e contribuinte.

Title: Gestor de Operações (M/F) - Porto
HTML:
Lidere operações, desenvolva equipas e faça a diferença num projeto em crescimento.


A Logiface, empresa do Grupo Starface, procura um(a) Gestor(a) de Operações para assumir a coordenação da atividade operacional na região do Porto.

Se tem experiência em liderança de equipas operacionais, gosta de ambientes dinâmicos e procura um desafio onde possa contribuir diretamente para o crescimento do negócio, esta oportunidade pode ser para si.


O seu desafio: Terá um papel fundamental na gestão e desenvolvimento da operação, garantindo elevados padrões de qualidade, eficiência e satisfação dos clientes.


Principais responsabilidades:

• Coordenar e supervisionar as operações diárias na região do Porto;

• Liderar, apoiar e desenvolver equipas operacionais;

• Garantir o cumprimento dos procedimentos e níveis de serviço definidos;

• Promover a melhoria contínua dos processos e da eficiência operacional;

• Monitorizar indicadores de desempenho e implementar ações corretivas;

• Assegurar uma comunicação eficaz com clientes, parceiros e equipas internas;

• Garantir o cumprimento das normas de segurança e requisitos legais aplicáveis;

• Identificar oportunidades de otimização e apoiar o crescimento da operação.


Procuramos profissionais com:

• Experiência em gestão de operações e coordenação de equipas;

• Capacidade de liderança, organização e tomada de decisão;

• Forte orientação para resultados e resolução de problemas;

• Excelentes competências de comunicação e relacionamento interpessoal;

• Conhecimentos de inglês;

• Domínio das ferramentas informáticas na ótica do utilizador;

• Disponibilidade para horários rotativos.


O que oferecemos:

• Integração num grupo sólido e em forte crescimento;

• Função com elevado grau de autonomia e responsabilidade;

• Participação ativa no desenvolvimento e expansão da operação;

• Oportunidades reais de desenvolvimento profissional e progressão de carreira;

• Equipa dinâmica e ambiente de trabalho colaborativo;

• Pacote remuneratório ajustado à experiência e ao desempenho demonstrado.


Envie o seu CV para recrutamento@starface.pt com a referência GO/PT07.


Junte-se à Logiface e faça parte de um projeto onde a sua capacidade de liderança terá impacto direto nos resultados do negócio.

Title: Recepcionista (m/f)
HTML:
A The Navigator Collection é um grupo hoteleiro em forte expansão, com unidades no Algarve e Porto Santo. A nossa missão é proporcionar experiências excecionais aos nossos hóspedes, enquanto promovemos uma cultura interna de inovação, excelência operacional e crescimento sustentável.


Estamos à procura de um(a) Recepcionista (m/f) para reforçar a equipa de Front-Office no nosso Hotel Colombus, em Porto Santo para regime de turnos rotativos e disponibilidade total e imediata.


Principais Responsabilidades:



  • Receber os hóspedes com eficiência e cordialidade, assegurando um processo de check-in e check-out fluido e acolhedor;

  • Gerir e atualizar reservas com precisão, garantindo uma organização eficaz do fluxo de hóspedes;

  • Prestar informações completas e claras sobre o hotel, os seus serviços, bem como sobre pontos de interesse locais e atividades disponíveis;

  • Atender chamadas, responder a emails e interagir presencialmente com os clientes, mantendo sempre uma postura profissional, simpática e prestável;

  • Executar tarefas administrativas associadas à receção, como emissão de documentos de pagamento, organização de arquivo e fecho de contas;

  • Lidar com reclamações ou situações inesperadas de forma rápida e eficaz, mantendo elevados padrões de qualidade no atendimento;

  • Colaborar com os restantes departamentos para garantir uma experiência integrada e memorável aos hóspedes.


Requisitos:



  • Formação mínima ao nível do 12.º ano, sendo valorizada formação técnica na área de Turismo, Hotelaria ou similar;

  • Experiência comprovada de pelo menos 2 anos em funções de receção, preferencialmente em contexto hoteleiro;

  • Conhecimentos sólidos de sistemas de reservas, com destaque para o PMS Newhotel Cloud;

  • Domínio das ferramentas informáticas, nomeadamente Word, Excel e Outlook;

  • Fluência em Português e Inglês (fator eliminatório);

  • Excelentes competências de comunicação, com capacidade para lidar com diferentes perfis de clientes;

  • Boa capacidade de organização, gestão de prioridades e resistência ao stress;

  • Atitude proativa, com forte orientação para o serviço ao cliente;

  • Autonomia, sentido de responsabilidade e espírito de iniciativa;

  • Elevada atenção ao detalhe, garantindo rigor e qualidade em todas as tarefas.


Oferta:



  • Envolvimento direto num projeto em forte crescimento com impacto real nas operações.

  • Remuneração compatível com a experiência demostrada com a experiência para a função.

Title: Especialista de Cobranças (m/f) – Matosinhos
HTML:
Como parte do nosso percurso de crescimento e evolução contínua, na Transdev Portugal procuramos reforçar a nossa equipa com a contratação de um Especialista de Cobranças (m/f) para Matosinhos.

Como será o seu dia a dia?

  • Realizar o acompanhamento integral do ciclo de cobranças, desde a identificação de valores em falta até à sua recuperação;
  • Monitorizar saldos em aberto, analisar contas correntes e garantir a resolução célere de discrepâncias;
  • Realizar contactos regulares com clientes para negociação de prazos, acordos de pagamento e esclarecimento de pendências;
  • Gerir cobranças junto de entidades públicas, demonstrando conhecimento dos seus processos administrativos e constrangimentos próprios;
  • Acompanhar plataformas de submissão de faturas e documentação obrigatória (ex.: plataformas públicas de faturação);
  • Assegurar o cumprimento dos requisitos legais e formais aplicáveis à faturação e cobrança no setor público;
  • Preparar relatórios periódicos de performance de cobranças, indicadores de atraso, aging e análise de tendências;
  • Elaborar análises detalhadas para apoio à tomada de decisão;
  • Desenvolver apresentações e reports para reuniões com a Administração e Direção Financeira;
  • Colaborar com equipas internas (Controlo de Gestão, Contabilidade, Faturação, Comercial, Operações) para resolução de pendências e identificação de problemas;
  • Propor e implementar melhorias aos processos de cobrança e controlo de crédito;
  • Garantir o cumprimento das políticas internas de controlo de crédito e níveis de serviço.

Requisitos

  • Licenciatura em Gestão, Economia, Contabilidade ou área relacionada (preferencial);
  • Experiência mínima de 5 anos na área de cobranças, preferencialmente em ambiente empresarial com grande volume de operações;
  • Experiência comprovada em gestão de clientes públicos (fator preferencial);
  • Domínio de ferramentas Office, com especial foco em Excel e PowerPoint;
  • Experiência em sistemas ERP (preferencialmente Navision);
  • Forte capacidade analítica e de leitura de dados financeiros.

Perfil Pretendido

  • Ecelente capacidade de comunicação e negociação;
  • Rigor, organização e atenção ao detalhe;
  • Proatividade e orientação para resultados;
  • Capacidade de trabalhar sob pressão e cumprir prazos exigentes.

Condições remuneratórias

  • Vencimento base mensal bruto: entre 1.000,00€, e 1.400,00€ pago em 14 meses.
  • Isenção de horário de trabalho (IHT): acresce IHT de 20% sobre o vencimento base, quando aplicável à função.
  • Remuneração mensal bruta estimada (com IHT): entre 1.200,00€, e 1.700,00€.
  • Subsídio de alimentação: 7,90€/dia.
  • Remuneração fixa anual bruta estimada: entre 14.400,00€, e 20.400,00€
  • Prémio anual de objetivos: poderá acrescer prémio anual de desempenho, de natureza variável, associado ao cumprimento de objetivos individuais e/ou coletivos, nos termos da política interna em vigor.
  • Poderão acrescer outras rubricas aplicáveis nos termos legais e/ou convencionais.

O que oferecemos

Na Transdev, valorizamos os nossos colaboradores e promovemos o seu desenvolvimento pessoal e profissional:

  • Seguro de saúde;
  • Descontos em diversas empresas parceiras;
  • Integração numa organização sólida, inovadora e em crescimento, com oportunidades de desenvolvimento e mobilidade interna.

Junte-se a nós e conduza o futuro da mobilidade!

Os/as candidatos/as interessados/as poderão submeter a sua candidatura através do nosso site.

A Transdev Portugal promove a igualdade de oportunidades no acesso ao emprego, não discriminando com base em etnia, género, idade, deficiência, crença, orientação sexual ou qualquer outra característica pessoal.

Incentivamos a candidatura de pessoas com deficiência, em cumprimento da Lei n.º 4/2019.

Title: Técnico de Desenvolvimento de Negócio (m/f/d)
Place: Vila Nova de Gaia, Porto, Portugal
Description:
Bem-vindo ao universo Caetano. Somos a organização que reúne as empresas da Salvador Caetano dedicadas à distribuição e reparação automóvel, com forte presença em Portugal e Espanha. Representamos 21 marcas automóveis, com mais de 100 pontos de venda e assistência, mas somos muito mais do que números: criamos experiências de mobilidade que melhoram a vida das pessoas.

Estamos sempre em movimento à procura de talentos que queiram crescer connosco, aprender rápido e criar soluções com impacto real.

Como é Ser Técnico de Desenvolvimento de Negócio?

- Cria, automatiza e otimiza processos digitais
- Tem vontade de aprender, crescer rapidamente e trabalhar em projetos reais com impacto direto no negócio — desde automações e integrações de sistemas até desenvolvimento de ferramentas internas e soluções com recurso a IA
- Tem motivação para trabalhar com diferentes tecnologias, dados e plataformas, contribuindo ativamente para a transformação digital da empresa

O que oferecemos:

- Dia extra de férias
- Seguro de saúde
- Atribuição de subsídios de apoio aos projetos de vida pessoal e familiar
- Descontos na compra de viaturas representadas pela Salvador Caetano
- Vantagens na aquisição de produtos/serviços em diversas áreas (lazer, tecnologia, bem-estar, educação…)
- Seguro de vida
- Formação e desenvolvimento contínuo
- Oportunidades de progressão de carreira no Grupo com forte representação nacional e internacional
- Integração em equipa dinâmica com espírito jovem e no maior grupo automóvel português

O que valorizamos:

- Licenciatura em Engenharia Informática, Sistemas de Informação ou área similar
- Conhecimentos base de programação (preferencialmente Python, JavaScript ou similares)
- Noções de APIs, integrações entre sistemas e manipulação de dados (JSON, CSV, etc.)
- Conhecimentos básicos de bases de dados (SQL)
- Interesse por automação de processos (RPA, scripts, etc.)
- Familiaridade com ferramentas low-code/no-code (valorizado)
- Interesse ou primeiros contactos com IA (ex: APIs, modelos, automação inteligente)
- Capacidade de resolver problemas de forma autónoma, pensamento lógico e estruturado
- Boa comunicação e capacidade de trabalhar em equipa
- Atenção ao detalhe, mas com foco em execução

Na Salvador Caetano ajudamos as Pessoas a moverem-se.

Na Salvador Caetano queremos contribuir para um futuro inclusivo e representativo. Acreditamos que a diversidade de talento empodera as nossas equipas, cria ambientes propícios à inovação e contribui para a evolução do negócio. Aqui incentivamos a diferença e incluímo-la. Independentemente da idade, género, etnia ou religião, promovemos a igualdade de oportunidades e garantimos a integração numa cultura baseada na dignidade e respeito por todas as pessoas.

Title: Operacional Rent-a-Car
Description:
Descrição da função

Procuramos Operacional Rent-a-Car para reforço da equipa da Auto-Industrial Rent - Porto.

Será responsável por dar apoio operacional e logístico à frota de viaturas, assegurando uma experiência de excelência aos nossos clientes em todas as fases do aluguer.


Receção, entrega e inspeção das viaturas junto dos clientes;
Apoio no atendimento ao cliente;
Verificação do estado, preparação e limpeza das viaturas;
Transferências entre estações e Entrega/Recolha de viaturas em locais externos (aeroporto, hotéis);
Transporte de clientes com elevados padrões de pontualidade e discrição;
Registo e atualização de dados no sistema de gestão de frota;


Perfil pretendido


Bons conhecimentos de Inglês
Orientação para o cliente e boa capacidade de comunicação
Elevado sentido de responsabilidade, organização e atenção ao detalhe.
Disponibilidade para horários rotativos, incluindo fins de semana e feriados.
Experiência anterior em rent-a-car ou sector automóvel (valorizado).
Domínio de ferramentas informáticas na ótica do utilizador.
Facilidade de relacionamento pessoal, de trabalho em equipa e de comunicação.


Oferece-se


Integração num dos principais grupos nacionais do sector da distribuição de automóveis, motociclos, máquinas agrícolas e náutica.
Remuneração compatível com a função e a experiência.
Formação inicial e contínua.

31 julho 2026

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Descrição da Função

Dellent is a consulting company focused in System Information and Telecommunications. Our goal is to help our candidates and consultants to take a step forward in their careers through projects that meet their needs and expectations. This project is part of the business investment banking and financial services area, that currently has its technological hub in Portugal and is working on the development of innovative solutions and support for all operations related to the business. Title: Assistente de Armazém
HTML:
A Synchro, Outsourcing by Egor, recruta Assistente de Armazém, para empresa de referência no setor de materiais de construção, situada em Lousada.

FUNÇÃO

  • Garantir a movimentação de operações de carga e descarga de mercadorias;
  • Conferência de material;
  • Garantir a reposição de mercadorias nos lotes;
  • Assegurar a manutenção e organização do armazém;
  • Garantir as operações de entrada e saída de mercadorias;
  • Separação de encomendas e respetivas cargas;
  • Repor lineares e participar na execução de ações comerciais (montagem de topos);
  • Participação de inventários, entre outras tarefas inerentes à função.

LOCAL E HORÁRIO

  • Segunda a sexta-feira;
  • Alguns sábados de manhã;
  • 9h00 às 18h00;
  • Lousada.

REQUISITOS

  • Experiência prévia em funções semelhantes;
  • Conhecimento ou experiência na condução de empilhadores;
  • Capacidade de organização, responsabilidade e proatividade;
  • Gosto pelo contacto com o cliente;
  • Assiduidade e pontualidade;
  • Gosto pelo trabalho em equipa;
  • Disponibilidade para trabalhar de segunda a sábado (manhã).

CONDIÇÕES

  • Contrato de trabalho;
  • Vencimento Base + Subsídio de Alimentação;
  • Integração num ambiente organizacional desafiante, numa equipa dinâmica e com projetos inovadores;
  • Formação inicial e continua com excelentes perspetivas de desenvolvimento profissional.

Os candidatos interessados devem submeter o CV atualizado.

Synchro by Egor

Success through People

Os candidatos considerados serão contactados num prazo máximo de 10 dias úteis.

Title: Técnico de Controlo de Vindima (Temporário)
Description:
A Comissão de Viticultura da Região dos Vinhos Verdes pretende reforçar a equipa de controlo da vindima entre o início do mês de Agosto até início de Outubro. Pretende-se, para o efeito, contratar técnicos de controlo em regime temporário, que irão receber formação para o adequado desempenho das suas funções.




FUNÇÕES A DESEMPENHAR:

• Registo de entregas de uva recebidas em adega

• Acompanhamento de acções controlo nas vinhas

• Contabilização e registo de volumes de vinho novo existente em adega;

• Acompanhamento de trânsitos de uva, mosto ou vinho em controlos de estrada

Todo o trabalho a desempenhar é coordenado e acompanhando por um auditor interno da CVR dos Vinhos Verdes




DURAÇÃO

• Recrutamento durante o período de vindima - de início de Agosto a início de Outubro

• Desempenho das funções em horários diurnos e noturnos;




CANDIDATAS/OS

Serão especialmente valorizadas as candidaturas de:

• Licenciados ou finalistas em cursos relevantes para o trabalho previsto;

• Experiência, sentido de responsabilidade, capacidade de trabalho em grupo

e iniciativa;

• Carta de condução

• Residentes em Amarante, Baião, Barcelos, Celorico, Fafe, Felgueiras,

Monção/Melgaço, Paredes/Penafiel, Valongo, Vizela. Aceitam-se candidatos de fora destas regiões que estejam 

disponíveis para serem instalados nestes concelhos pela CVR dos Vinhos Verdes durante o

período do contrato.




OFERECEMOS

• Ação de formação inicial 

- Equipamento informático para o desempenho das funções

• Integração em equipa dinâmica;

• Experiência enriquecedora em termos pessoais e profissionais;

• Remuneração fixa.




Envie a sua candidatura para sgoncalves@vinhoverde.pt
